framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Collects all defined terms and regulatory references in Part III Section 4 of the framework.
policies.scope: Applicable to listed entities and their disclosures under SEBI regulations.
policies.objective: Standardize terminology for compliance and reporting.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines non‑convertible securities, non‑convertible redeemable preference shares, perpetual debt instruments, and perpetual non‑cumulative preference shares.
subpolicies.control: ‘non‑convertible securities’, ‘non‑convertible redeemable preference shares’, ‘perpetual debt instrument’ and ‘perpetual non‑cumulative preference share’ shall have the same meaning as assigned to them in the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Non‑Convertible Securities) Regulations, 2021;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This clause provides the definition of several key securities terms referenced in the framework.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: ‘non‑convertible securities’, ‘non‑convertible redeemable preference shares’, ‘perpetual debt instrument’ and ‘perpetual non‑cumulative preference share’ shall have the same meaning as assigned to them in the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Non‑Convertible Securities) Regulations, 2021;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies the meaning of ‘offer document’ as per multiple SEBI regulations.
subpolicies.control: 'offer document' shall have the same meaning assigned to it under clause 12[(kk)] of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 2 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018, clause (y) of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 2 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Non‑Convertible Securities) regulations, 2021, clause (r) of regulation 2 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 1996 and clause (l) of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 2 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Securitised Debt Instruments and Security Receipts) Regulations, 2008, as may be applicable;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the term ‘offer document’ by referencing multiple regulatory clauses.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'offer document' shall have the same meaning assigned to it under clause 12[(kk)] of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 2 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018, ...

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: w - Definition of Promoter and Promoter Group
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines ‘promoter’ and ‘promoter group’ according to SEBI regulations.
subpolicies.control: 'promoter' and 'promoter group' shall have the same meaning as assigned to them respectively in clauses 16[(oo)] and 17[(pp)] of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 2 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Provides definitions for ‘promoter’ and ‘promoter group’ referencing specific regulatory clauses.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'promoter' and 'promoter group' shall have the same meaning as assigned to them respectively in clauses 16[(oo)] and 17[(pp)] of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 2 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines ‘public’ as per rule 2(d) of the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Rules, 1957.
subpolicies.control: 'public' means public as defined under clause (d) of rule 2 of the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Rules, 1957;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets the meaning of ‘public’ for regulatory purposes.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'public' means public as defined under clause (d) of rule 2 of the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Rules, 1957;

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: t - Definition of Non‑Convertible Debt Securities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines ‘non‑convertible debt securities’ as debt securities under SEBI regulations.
subpolicies.control: 'non‑convertible debt securities' which is ‘debt securities’ as defined under regulation 2(1)(e) of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Debt Securities) Regulations, 2008’;

ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'non‑convertible debt securities' which is ‘debt securities’ as defined under regulation 2(1)(e) of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Debt Securities) Regulations, 2008'

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines ‘public shareholding’ according to rule 2(e) of the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Rules, 1957.
subpolicies.control: 'public shareholding' means public shareholding as defined under clause (e) of rule 2 of the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Rules, 1957;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Provides the definition of public shareholding for compliance purposes.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'public shareholding' means public shareholding as defined under clause (e) of rule 2 of the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Rules, 1957;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines ‘quarter’ as a three‑month period starting on April, July, October, or January.
subpolicies.control: 'quarter' means the period of three months commencing on the first day of April, July, October or January of a financial year;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Establishes the time frame for ‘quarter’ used in reporting.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'quarter' means the period of three months commencing on the first day of April, July, October or January of a financial year;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines ‘quarterly results’ as financial results prepared in accordance with the regulations for a quarter.
subpolicies.control: 'quarterly results' means the financial results prepared in accordance with these regulations in respect of a quarter;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies what constitutes quarterly results under the framework.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'quarterly results' means the financial results prepared in accordance with these regulations in respect of a quarter;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines ‘related party’ as per sub‑section (76) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013 or applicable accounting standards, with additional thresholds effective April 1, 2023.
subpolicies.control: 'related party' means a related party as defined under sub-section (76) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013 or under the applicable accounting standards: Provided that: (a) any person or entity forming a part of the promoter or promoter group of the listed entity; or (b) any person or any entity, holding equity shares: (i) of twenty per cent or more; or (ii) of ten per cent or more, with effect from April 1, 2023; in the listed entity either directly or on a beneficial interest basis as provided under section 89 of the Companies Act, 2013, at any time, during the immediate preceding financial year; shall be deemed to be a related party: Provided further that this definition shall not be applicable for the units issued by mutual funds which are listed on a recognised stock exchange(s);

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Provides a detailed definition of ‘related party’ including thresholds and exclusions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'related party' means a related party as defined under sub-section (76) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013 or under the applicable accounting standards: Provided that: (a) any person or entity forming a part of the promoter or promoter group ... shall be deemed to be a related party:

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: zc - Definition of Related Party Transaction
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines ‘related party transaction’ as a transaction benefiting a related party, effective April 1, 2023, with exclusions for certain corporate actions.
subpolicies.control: 'related party transaction' means a transaction involving a transfer of resources, services or obligations between: (i) a listed entity or any of its subsidiaries on one hand and a related party of the listed entity or any of its subsidiaries on the other hand; or (ii) a listed entity or any of its subsidiaries on one hand, and any other person or entity on the other hand, the purpose and effect of which is to benefit a related party of the listed entity or any of its subsidiaries, with effect from April 1, 2023; Provided that this definition shall not be applicable for the units issued by mutual funds which are listed on a recognised stock exchange(s); (c) acceptance of fixed deposits by banks/Non‑Banking Finance Companies at the terms uniformly applicable/offered to all shareholders/public, subject to disclosure of the same along with the disclosure of related party transactions every six months to the stock exchange(s), in the format as specified by the

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets out the scope of related party transactions and the exclusions for certain corporate actions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'related party transaction' means a transaction involving a transfer of resources, services or obligations between: (i) a listed entity or any of its subsidiaries on one hand and a related party of the listed entity or any of its subsidiaries on the other hand; or (ii) a listed entity or any of its subsidiaries on one hand, and any other person or entity on the other hand, the purpose and effect of which is to benefit a related party of the listed entity or any of its subsidiaries, with effect from April 1, 2023;

policies.policy_title: Board acceptance of deposits and retail purchases
policies.policy_description: Regulation concerning acceptance of deposits by banks and retail purchases by directors/employees of listed entities.
policies.scope: Banks and listed entities
policies.objective: Ensure compliance with RBI directions and uniform terms for retail purchases.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement for banks to accept current account and savings deposits in compliance with RBI directions, including interest payment.
subpolicies.control: Acceptance of current account deposits and saving account deposits by banks in compliance with the directions issued by the Reserve Bank of India or any other central bank in the relevant jurisdiction from time to time: Explanation: For the purpose of clauses (c) and (d) above, acceptance of deposits includes payment of interest thereon.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the deposit acceptance requirement as a distinct control.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Acceptance of current account deposits and saving account deposits by banks in compliance with the directions issued by the Reserve Bank of India or any other central bank in the relevant jurisdiction from time to time: Explanation: For the purpose of clauses (c) and (d) above, acceptance of deposits includes payment of interest thereon.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Retail purchases by directors or employees of a listed entity without establishing a business relationship, on uniform terms, except for mutual fund units.
subpolicies.control: Retail purchases from any listed entity or its subsidiary by its directors or its employees, without establishing a business relationship and at the terms which are uniformly applicable/offered to all employees and directors:] Provided further that this definition shall not be applicable for the units issued by mutual funds which are listed on a recognised stock exchange(s);

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the retail purchase condition as a separate control.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Retail purchases from any listed entity or its subsidiary by its directors or its employees, without establishing a business relationship and at the terms which are uniformly applicable/offered to all employees and directors:] Provided further that this definition shall not be applicable for the units issued by mutual funds which are listed on a recognised stock exchange(s);

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the policy governing acceptance of deposits and retail purchases as defined in the source text.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Board: 23[(d) acceptance of current account deposits and saving account deposits by banks in compliance with the directions issued by the Reserve Bank of India or any other central bank in the relevant jurisdiction from time to time: Explanation: For the purpose of clauses (c) and (d) above, acceptance of deposits includes payment of interest thereon. (e) retail purchases from any listed entity or its subsidiary by its directors or its employees, without establishing a business relationship and at the terms which are uniformly applicable/offered to all employees and directors:]

policies.policy_description: Specifies when the regulations apply to listed entities with designated securities.
policies.scope: Listed entities with listed securities on recognized stock exchanges
policies.objective: Determine entities subject to the regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulations apply to listed entities that have listed designated securities on recognized exchanges.
subpolicies.control: Unless otherwise provided, these regulations shall apply to a listed entity which has listed any of the following designated securities on recognised stock exchange(s): (a) specified securities listed on main board or SME Exchange or Innovators Growth Platform; (b) non-convertible securities;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the applicability condition as a distinct control.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Unless otherwise provided, these regulations shall apply to a listed entity which has listed any of the following designated securities on recognised stock exchange(s): (a) specified securities listed on main board or SME Exchange or Innovators Growth Platform; (b) non-convertible securities;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the clause defining the applicability of the regulations to listed entities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Unless otherwise provided, these regulations shall apply to a listed entity which has listed any of the following designated securities on recognised stock exchange(s): (a) specified securities listed on main board or SME Exchange or Innovators Growth Platform; (b) non-convertible securities;

policies.policy_description: Provides that undefined words take meaning from the Act, Companies Act, etc.
policies.scope: All terms not defined in the regulations
policies.objective: Ensure consistent interpretation using existing statutes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Undefined terms adopt meanings from applicable statutes.
subpolicies.control: All other words and expressions used but not defined in these regulations, but defined in the Act or the Companies Act, 2013, the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Act, 1956, the Depositories Act, 1996 and/or the rules and regulations made thereunder shall have the same meaning as respectively assigned to them in such Acts or rules or regulations or any statutory modification or re-enactment thereto, as the case may be.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the interpretation rule for undefined terms.

policies.policy_description: Regulations that determine when SEBI Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements provisions apply to listed entities based on market capitalisation rankings and debt securities thresholds.
policies.scope: Listed entities on recognized stock exchanges
policies.objective: Define criteria for applicability, continuation, and cessation of regulatory provisions based on market capitalisation and debt thresholds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Preparation of market capitalisation ranking list by stock exchanges.
subpolicies.control: Every recognized stock exchange shall, at the end of the calendar year i.e., 31st December, prepare a list of entities that have listed their specified securities ranking such entities on the basis of their average market capitalisation from 1st July to 31st December of that calendar year.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the rule that establishes the annual ranking list used to determine applicability.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every recognized stock exchange shall, at the end of the calendar year i.e., 31st December, prepare a list of entities that have listed their specified securities ranking such entities on the basis of their average market capitalisation from 1st July to 31st December of that calendar year.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Timing of applicability and compliance obligations.
subpolicies.control: The relevant provisions shall then become applicable to a listed entity that is required to comply with such requirements for the first time (or, if applicable, required to comply after any interim period) after a period of three months from December 31 (i.e. April 1) or from the beginning of the immediate next financial year, whichever is later: Provided that the listed entity, which is required to comply for the first time or after a period of cessation, shall put in place systems and processes for compliance with clause (f) of sub- regulation (2) of regulation 34 within a period of three months from December 31 (i.e. on or before April 1) or from the beginning of the immediate next financial year, whichever is later, and further disclose the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report and/or [assessment or assurance of the specified parameters] as per the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report Core in the Annual Report prepared for the financial year in which systems and processes were required to be put in place in accordance with this proviso.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies when provisions become applicable and the compliance timeline for new entities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The relevant provisions shall then become applicable to a listed entity that is required to comply with such requirements for the first time (or, if applicable, required to comply after any interim period) after a period of three months from December 31 (i.e. April 1) or from the beginning of the immediate next financial year, whichever is later:

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Continuation of applicability unless ranking changes for three consecutive years.
subpolicies.control: The provisions of these regulations, which become applicable to a listed entity on the basis of criteria of market capitalisation, shall continue to apply to such an entity unless its ranking changes in the list prepared in accordance with sub-regulation (2) of this regulation and such change results in the listed entity remaining outside the applicable threshold for a period of three consecutive years.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the condition under which applicability persists despite ranking changes.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The provisions of these regulations, which become applicable to a listed entity on the basis of criteria of market capitalisation, shall continue to apply to such an entity unless its ranking changes in the list prepared in accordance with sub-regulation (2) of this regulation and such change results in the listed entity remaining outside the applicable threshold for a period of three consecutive years.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Cessation of applicability after three consecutive years outside threshold.
subpolicies.control: For such listed entities which remain outside the applicable threshold for a period of three consecutive years in terms of sub-regulation (2A) of this regulation, the provisions that apply on the basis of criteria of market capitalisation shall cease to apply at the end of the financial year following the 31st December of the third consecutive year: Provided that for those listed entities that follow January to December as its financial year, the provisions shall cease to apply at the end of three months from 31st December of the third consecutive year (i.e. on 31st March).

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets out the termination point for applicability after three consecutive years outside the threshold.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: For such listed entities which remain outside the applicable threshold for a period of three consecutive years in terms of sub-regulation (2A) of this regulation, the provisions that apply on the basis of criteria of market capitalisation shall cease to apply at the end of the financial year following the 31st December of the third consecutive year:

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Continuation of debt‑securities based applicability even if thresholds are fallen below.
subpolicies.control: The provisions of these regulations which become applicable to listed entities on the basis of the criterion of the value of outstanding listed debt securities shall continue to apply to such entities even if they fall below such thresholds as mentioned in sub-regulation (1A) of regulation 15.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Ensures that debt‑securities based criteria remain applicable irrespective of threshold breaches.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The provisions of these regulations which become applicable to listed entities on the basis of the criterion of the value of outstanding listed debt securities shall continue to apply to such entities even if they fall below such thresholds as mentioned in sub-regulation (1A) of regulation 15.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies sub-regulations governing applicability based on market capitalisation and debt thresholds.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every recognized stock exchange shall, at the end of the calendar year i.e., 31st December, prepare a list of entities that have listed their specified securities ranking such entities on the basis of their average market capitalisation from 1st July to 31st December of that calendar year.

policies.policy_description: Framework of principles that listed entities must follow for disclosures, information dissemination, and corporate governance.
policies.scope: Listed entities with listed securities
policies.objective: Ensure accurate, timely, and stakeholder‑focused disclosures and compliance with corporate governance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Preparation and disclosure according to accounting standards.
subpolicies.control: Information shall be prepared and disclosed in accordance with applicable standards of accounting and financial disclosure.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets the requirement for preparing disclosures per accounting standards.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Information shall be prepared and disclosed in accordance with applicable standards of accounting and financial disclosure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of accounting standards and audit.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall implement the prescribed accounting standards in letter and spirit in the preparation of financial statements taking into consideration the interest of all stakeholders and shall also ensure that the annual audit is conducted by an independent, competent and qualified auditor.

policies.policy_description: Specifies the use of RBI‑approved electronic payment modes for dividends, interest, and redemption amounts, with fallback provisions for cheques and speed post for large dividends.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to use RBI‑approved electronic payment modes for dividends, interest, and redemption, with fallback to cheques and speed post for large dividends.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall use any of the electronic mode of payment facility approved by the Reserve Bank of India, in the manner specified in Schedule I, for the payment of the following: (a) dividends; (b) interest; (c) redemption or repayment amounts: Provided that where it is not possible to use electronic mode of payment, ‘payable-at-par’ warrants or cheques may be issued: Provided further that where the amount payable as dividend exceeds one thousand and five hundred rupees, the ‘payable-at-par’ warrants or cheques shall be sent by speed post.

policies.policy_description: Defines the scope of Chapter IV obligations for listed entities with specified securities and high‑value non‑convertible debt securities.
policies.scope: Listed entities on recognized stock exchanges, including high‑value debt issuers
policies.objective: Determine which entities are subject to the detailed obligations in Chapter IV

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement defining applicability of Chapter IV to listed entities with specified securities and high‑value debt.
subpolicies.control: The provisions of this chapter shall apply to a listed entity which has listed its specified securities on any recognised stock exchange(s) either on the main board or on SME Exchange or on Innovators Growth Platform: (1A) The provisions of this regulation and regulation 16 to regulation 27 of this chapter shall apply to a listed entity which has listed its non‑convertible debt securities and has an outstanding value of listed non‑convertible debt securities of Rupees One Thousand Crore and above: Provided that in case the value of the outstanding listed non‑convertible debt securities becomes equal to or greater than the specified threshold of Rupees One Thousand Crore during the course of the year, a high value debt listed entity shall ensure compliance with these provisions within six months from the date of such trigger, and the disclosures of such compliance may be made in the corporate governance compliance report on and from the third quarter following the date of the trigger: Provided further that these provisions shall be applicable to a ‘high value debt listed entity’ on a ‘comply or explain’ basis until March 31, 2025 and on a mandatory basis thereafter.

policies.policy_title: Corporate Governance Requirements for High Value Debt Listed Entities
policies.policy_description: Defines the applicability, compliance timelines, and governance obligations for listed entities classified as high value debt issuers, including REITs and InvITs, and for entities listed on the SME Exchange.
policies.scope: Applies to listed high value debt entities, REITs, InvITs, and SME Exchange listed entities.
policies.objective: Ensure compliance with SEBI Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements regulations 15-27 and related corporate governance provisions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: REIT manager board must comply with regulations 15-27 corporate governance.
subpolicies.control: In case of a ‘high value debt listed entity’ that is a Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT), the Board of the Manager of the Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT), shall comply with regulation 15 to regulation 27 of these regulations related to corporate governance;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: InvIT manager board must comply with regulations 15-27 corporate governance.
subpolicies.control: In case of a ‘high value debt listed entity’ that is an Infrastructure Investment Trust (InvIT), the Board of the Investment Manager of the Infrastructure Investment Trust (InvIT), shall comply with regulation 15 to regulation 27 of these regulations related to corporate governance;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Applicable regulations continue until debt threshold is breached for three consecutive years.
subpolicies.control: Once the regulation 15 to 27 become applicable to a ‘high value debt listed entity’, the said regulations continue to apply till value of the outstanding listed debt securities as on March 31 in a year, reduces and remains below the specified threshold for a period of three consecutive financial years.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Small listed entities must comply within six months of applicability and continue until thresholds remain below for three years.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: SME listed entities must meet regulation 23 requirements if capital or net‑worth exceeds thresholds, with six‑month compliance and three‑year continuation.
subpolicies.control: With effect from April 01, 2025, the provisions of regulation 23 shall be applicable in respect of a listed entity which has listed its specified securities on the SME Exchange and which has either paid up equity share capital exceeding Rupees ten crore or net worth exceeding Rupees twenty five crore, as on the last day of the previous financial year: Provided further that where the provisions of regulation 23 become applicable at a later date to a listed entity which has listed its specified securities on the SME Exchange, it shall ensure compliance with the same within six months from such date: Provided further that once the provisions of regulation 23 become applicable to a listed entity which has listed its specified securities on the SME Exchange, they shall continue to remain applicable till such time the equity share capital and the net‑worth of such entity reduces and remains below the specified threshold for a period of three consecutive financial years.

policies.policy_description: Specifies that certain governance provisions do not apply during the insolvency resolution period for listed entities, with conditions for compliance after resolution plan approval.
policies.scope: Listed entities or high value debt listed entities undergoing corporate insolvency resolution under the Insolvency Code
policies.objective: Clarify applicability of governance provisions during insolvency and ensure timely compliance post‑resolution

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Exempts regulation 17 from applicability during insolvency resolution, with board duties fulfilled by resolution professional.
subpolicies.control: The provisions as specified in regulation 17 shall not be applicable during the insolvency resolution process period in respect of a listed entity ... Provided that the role and responsibilities of the board of directors as specified under regulation 17 shall be fulfilled by the interim resolution professional or resolution professional ... Provided further that such listed entity shall ensure compliance with regulation 17 within a period of three months of approval of resolution plan under section 31 of the Insolvency Code.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (2A) explicitly exempts regulation 17 and defines compliance responsibilities during insolvency.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (2A) The provisions as specified in regulation 17 shall not be applicable during the insolvency resolution process period ... Provided that the role and responsibilities of the board of directors as specified under regulation 17 shall be fulfilled by the interim resolution professional or resolution professional ... Provided further that such listed entity shall ensure compliance with regulation 17 within a period of three months of approval of resolution plan under section 31 of the Insolvency Code.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Exempts regulations 18‑21 from applicability during insolvency resolution, with committee duties fulfilled by resolution professional.
subpolicies.control: The provisions as specified in regulations 18, 19, 20 and 21 shall not be applicable during the insolvency resolution process period in respect of a listed entity ... Provided that the roles and responsibilities of the committees specified in the respective regulations shall be fulfilled by the interim resolution professional or resolution professional ... Provided further that such listed entity shall ensure compliance with regulations 18, 19, 20 and 21 within a period of three months of approval of resolution plan under section 31 of the Insolvency Code.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (2B) explicitly exempts regulations 18‑21 and defines compliance responsibilities during insolvency.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (2B) The provisions as specified in regulations 18, 19, 20 and 21 shall not be applicable during the insolvency resolution process period ... Provided that the roles and responsibilities of the committees specified in the respective regulations shall be fulfilled by the interim resolution professional or resolution professional ... Provided further that such listed entity shall ensure compliance with regulations 18, 19, 20 and 21 within a period of three months of approval of resolution plan under section 31 of the Insolvency Code.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Describes exemptions for regulations 17 and 18‑21 during insolvency resolution, with compliance timelines.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (2A) The provisions as specified in regulation 17 shall not be applicable during the insolvency resolution process period ... Provided that ... such listed entity shall ensure compliance with regulation 17 within a period of three months of approval of resolution plan under section 31 of the Insolvency Code. (2B) The provisions as specified in regulations 18, 19, 20 and 21 shall not be applicable during the insolvency resolution process period ... Provided further that such listed entity shall ensure compliance with regulations 18, 19, 20 and 21 within a period of three months of approval of resolution plan under section 31 of the Insolvency Code.

policies.policy_description: Provides meanings for key terms used throughout the framework, including 'control' and 'independent director'.
policies.scope: Interpretation of defined terms for application in the regulatory framework
policies.objective: Clarify the meaning of essential terms to ensure consistent application

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines 'control' by reference to SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011.
subpolicies.control: control shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (a) explicitly defines 'control' with reference to a specific regulation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (a) "control" shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provides a detailed definition of 'independent director' with eligibility criteria.
subpolicies.control: independent director means a non‑executive director, other than a nominee director of the listed entity: (i) who, in the opinion of the board of directors, is a person of integrity and possesses relevant expertise and experience; (ii) who is or was not a promoter of the listed entity or its holding, subsidiary or associate company; (iii) who is not related to promoters or directors in the listed entity, its holding, subsidiary or associate company; (iv) who, apart from receiving director's remuneration, has or had no material pecuniary relationship with the listed entity, its holding, subsidiary or associate company, or their promoters, or directors, during the three immediately preceding financial years or during the current financial year; (v) none of whose relatives- (A) is holding securities of or interest in the listed entity, its holding, subsidiary or associate company during the three immediately preceding financial years or during the current financial year of face value in excess of fifty lakh rupees or two percent of the paid‑up capital of the listed entity, its holding, subsidiary or associate company, respectively, or such higher sum as may be specified; (B) is indebted to the listed entity, its holding, subsidiary or associate company or their promoters or directors, in excess of such amount as may be specified during the three immediately preceding financial years or during the current financial year; (C) has given a guarantee or provided any security in connection with the indebtedness of any third person to the listed entity, its holding, subsidiary or associate company or their promoters or directors, for such amount as may be specified during the three immediately preceding financial years or during the current financial year; or (D) has any other pecuniary transaction or relationship with the listed entity, its holding, subsidiary or associate company amounting to two percent or more of its gross turnover or total income: Provided that the pecuniary relationship or transaction with the listed entity, its holding, subsidiary or associate company or their promoters, or directors in relation to points (A) to (D) above shall not exceed two percent of its gross turnover or total income or fifty lakh rupees or such higher amount as may be specified from time to time, whichever is lower.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (b) provides a comprehensive definition of 'independent director' with multiple sub‑conditions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (b) "independent director" means a non‑executive director, other than a nominee director of the listed entity: (i) who, in the opinion of the board of directors, is a person of integrity and possesses relevant expertise and experience; (ii) who is or was not a promoter of the listed entity or its holding, subsidiary or associate company ... (long definition continues)

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Lists definitions of 'control' and 'independent director' that are essential for interpreting the regulations.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 16. (1) For the purpose of this chapter , unless the context otherwise requires - (a) "control" shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011; (b) "independent director" means a non‑executive director, other than a nominee director of the listed entity: ...

policies.policy_description: Defines entities and relationships that constitute a related party for listed companies.
policies.scope: Applies to listed entities and their directors, senior management, and affiliates.
policies.objective: Identify related parties for disclosure and compliance purposes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Related party holding 10% or more of gross turnover.
subpolicies.control: transaction with the listed entity, its holding, subsidiary or associate company amounting to ten per cent or more of the gross turnover of such firm;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This subpolicy extracts the condition defining related parties by turnover threshold.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: transaction with the listed entity, its holding, subsidiary or associate company amounting to ten per cent or more of the gross turnover of such firm;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Related party holding 2% or more of voting power with relatives.
subpolicies.control: holds together with his relatives two per cent or more of the total voting power of the listed entity;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the voting power threshold condition for related party status.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: holds together with his relatives two per cent or more of the total voting power of the listed entity;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Related party who is a chief executive or director of a non-profit receiving 25%+ receipts from listed entity.
subpolicies.control: is a chief executive or director, by whatever name called, of any non- profit organisation that receives twenty-five per cent or more of its receipts or corpus from the listed entity, any of its promoters, directors or its holding, subsidiary or associate company or that holds two per cent or more of the total voting power of the listed entity;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies non-profit director/CEO condition as related party.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: is a chief executive or director, by whatever name called, of any non- profit organisation that receives twenty-five per cent or more of its receipts or corpus from the listed entity, any of its promoters, directors or its holding, subsidiary or associate company or that holds two per cent or more of the total voting power of the listed entity;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Related party who is a material supplier, service provider, customer, lessor or lessee.
subpolicies.control: is a material supplier, service provider or customer or a lessor or lessee of the listed entity;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines material commercial relationships as related party.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: is a material supplier, service provider or customer or a lessor or lessee of the listed entity;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Related party who is not less than 21 years of age.
subpolicies.control: who is not less than 21 years of age.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets minimum age requirement for related party status.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: who is not less than 21 years of age.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Related party who is not a non-independent director of another company where a non-independent director of the listed entity is an independent director.
subpolicies.control: who is not a non-independent director of another company on the board of which any non-independent director of the listed entity is an independent director:

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the cross-directorship restriction for related parties.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: who is not a non-independent director of another company on the board of which any non-independent director of the listed entity is an independent director:

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This policy captures the comprehensive definition of related parties enumerated in the text.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: transaction with the listed entity, its holding, subsidiary or associate company amounting to ten per cent or more of the gross turnover of such firm; (C) holds together with his relatives two per cent or more of the total voting power of the listed entity; or (D) is a chief executive or director, by whatever name called, of any non- profit organisation that receives twenty-five per cent or more of its receipts or corpus from the listed entity, any of its promoters, directors or its holding, subsidiary or associate company or that holds two per cent or more of the total voting power of the listed entity; (E) is a material supplier, service provider or customer or a lessor or lessee of the listed entity; (viii) who is not less than 21 years of age.

policies.policy_description: Specifies criteria for identifying a material subsidiary based on financial thresholds.
policies.scope: Applies to listed entities for assessing subsidiary materiality.
policies.objective: Determine when a subsidiary is considered material for disclosure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Threshold of 10% of consolidated turnover for material subsidiary.
subpolicies.control: turnover exceeds ten percent of the consolidated turnover ... of the listed entity and its subsidiaries in the immediately preceding accounting year.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the turnover threshold condition.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: turnover exceeds ten percent of the consolidated turnover ... of the listed entity and its subsidiaries in the immediately preceding accounting year.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Threshold of 10% of consolidated net worth for material subsidiary.
subpolicies.control: net worth exceeds ten percent of the consolidated net worth respectively, of the listed entity and its subsidiaries in the immediately preceding accounting year.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the net worth threshold condition.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: net worth exceeds ten percent of the consolidated net worth respectively, of the listed entity and its subsidiaries in the immediately preceding accounting year.

ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall formulate a policy for determining ‘material’ subsidiary.

ai_analysis.source_excerpt: material subsidiary shall mean a subsidiary, whose turnover or net worth exceeds ten percent of the consolidated turnover or net worth respectively, of the listed entity and its subsidiaries in the immediately preceding accounting year.

policies.policy_description: Defines the scope of senior management for listed entities.
policies.scope: Applies to listed entities for governance and remuneration purposes.
policies.objective: Identify individuals considered part of senior management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Core management team members excluding board directors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Management one level below CEO/MD/WT Director/Manager, including CEO/Manager if not on board.
subpolicies.control: comprise all the members of the management one level below the Chief Executive Officer or Managing Director or Whole Time Director or Manager (including Chief Executive Officer and Manager, in case they are not part of the board of directors)

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the hierarchical level included in senior management.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: comprise all the members of the management one level below the Chief Executive Officer or Managing Director or Whole Time Director or Manager (including Chief Executive Officer and Manager, in case they are not part of the board of directors)

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Functional heads and key managerial personnel designated by the listed entity.
subpolicies.control: shall specifically include the functional heads, by whatever name called and the persons identified and designated as key managerial personnel, other than the board of directors, by the listed entity

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies inclusion of functional heads and key managerial personnel.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: shall specifically include the functional heads, by whatever name called and the persons identified and designated as key managerial personnel, other than the board of directors, by the listed entity

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This policy captures the definition of senior management as per the text.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: senior management shall mean the officers and personnel of the listed entity who are members of its core management team, excluding the Board of Directors, and shall also comprise all the members of the management one level below the Chief Executive Officer or Managing Director or Whole Time Director or Manager (including Chief Executive Officer and Manager, in case they are not part of the board of directors) and shall specifically include the functional heads, by whatever name called and the persons identified and designated as key managerial personnel, other than the board of directors, by the listed entity.

policies.policy_description: Sets composition, independence, gender, and size requirements for the board of directors of listed entities.
policies.scope: Applies to listed entities regarding board structure and independence.
policies.objective: Ensure adequate representation, independence, and minimum size of the board.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum proportion of non-executive directors (50%).
subpolicies.control: not less than fifty per cent. of the board of directors shall comprise of non-executive directors;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies that at least half of the board must be non-executive.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: not less than fifty per cent. of the board of directors shall comprise of non-executive directors;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement for at least one woman director.
subpolicies.control: board of directors shall have an optimum combination of executive and non- executive directors with at least one woman director;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates inclusion of at least one female director.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: at least one woman director

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Independent director proportion when chairperson is non-executive.
subpolicies.control: where the chairperson of the board of directors is a non-executive director, at least one-third of the board of directors shall comprise of independent directors; where the listed entity does not have a regular non-executive chairperson, at least half of the board of directors shall comprise of independent directors;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines independent director thresholds based on chairperson status.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: where the chairperson of the board of directors is a non-executive director, at least one-third of the board of directors shall comprise of independent directors and where the listed entity does not have a regular non-executive chairperson, at least half of the board of directors shall comprise of independent directors;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum six directors for top 2000 listed entities.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors of the top 2000 listed entities shall comprise of not less than six directors;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets a minimum board size for the top 2000 entities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The board of directors of the top 2000 listed entities shall comprise of not less than six directors;

ai_analysis.source_excerpt: where the listed company has outstanding SR equity shares, atleast half of the board of directors shall comprise of independent directors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Age limit for non-executive director appointments (75 years).
subpolicies.control: No listed entity shall appoint a person or continue the directorship of any person as a non-executive director who has attained the age of seventy five years unless a special resolution is passed to that effect, in which case the explanatory statement annexed to the notice for such motion shall indicate the justification for appointing such a person

policies.scope: Listed entities under SEBI Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements Regulations
policies.objective: Ensure proper governance and shareholder oversight of director appointments and continuations

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Shareholder approval required at next general meeting or within three months for appointment/reappointment of directors/managers, with exclusions for regulatory approvals and public sector companies.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall ensure that approval of shareholders for appointment or reappointment of a person on the board of directors or as a manager is taken at the next general meeting or within a time period of three months from the date of appointment, whichever is earlier: Provided that if such appointment or re-appointment of a person to the board of directors or as a manager is subject to approval of regulatory, government or statutory authorities, then the time taken to receive such approvals shall be excluded for the purposes of this clause: Provided further that a public sector company shall ensure that the approval of the shareholders for appointment or re-appointment of a person on the board of directors or as a Manager is taken at the next general meeting:

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the requirement for shareholder approval timing and exclusions as per sub-regulation (1C)(a).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall ensure that approval of shareholders for appointment or reappointment of a person on the board of directors or as a manager is taken at the next general meeting or within a time period of three months from the date of appointment, whichever is earlier: Provided that if such appointment or re-appointment of a person to the board of directors or as a manager is subject to approval of regulatory, government or statutory authorities, then the time taken to receive such approvals shall be excluded for the purposes of this clause: Provided further that a public sector company shall ensure that the approval of the shareholders for appointment or re-appointment of a person on the board of directors or as a Manager is taken at the next general meeting:

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prior shareholder approval required for re-appointment of persons previously rejected, with detailed justification statement.
subpolicies.control: The appointment or a re-appointment of a person, including as a managing director or a whole-time director or a manager, who was earlier rejected by the shareholders at a general meeting, shall be done only with the prior approval of the shareholders: Provided that the statement referred to under sub-section (1) of section 102 of the Companies Act, 2013, annexed to the notice to the shareholders, for considering the appointment or re-appointment of such a person earlier rejected by the shareholders shall contain a detailed explanation and justification by the Nomination and Remuneration Committee and the board of directors for recommending such a person for appointment or re-appointment.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the sub-requirement concerning prior approval and justification for re-appointing previously rejected persons.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The appointment or a re-appointment of a person, including as a managing director or a whole-time director or a manager, who was earlier rejected by the shareholders at a general meeting, shall be done only with the prior approval of the shareholders: Provided that the statement referred to under sub-section (1) of section 102 of the Companies Act, 2013, annexed to the notice to the shareholders, for considering the appointment or re-appointment of such a person earlier rejected by the shareholders shall contain a detailed explanation and justification by the Nomination and Remuneration Committee and the board of directors for recommending such a person for appointment or re-appointment.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Continuation of directors requires shareholder approval every five years, with exceptions.
subpolicies.control: With effect from April 1, 2024, the continuation of a director serving on the board of directors of a listed entity shall be subject to the approval by the shareholders in a general meeting at least once in every five years from the date of their appointment or reappointment, as the case may be: Provided that the continuation of the director serving on the board of directors of a listed entity as on March 31, 2024, without the approval of the shareholders for the last five years or more shall be subject to the approval of shareholders in the first general meeting to be held after March 31, 2024: Provided further that the requirement specified in this sub-regulation shall not be applicable to the Whole-Time Director, Managing Director, Manager, Independent Director or a Director retiring as per the sub-section (6) of section 152 of the Companies Act, 2013, if the approval of the shareholders for the reappointment or continuation of the aforesaid directors or Manager is otherwise provided for by the provisions of these regulations or the Companies Act, 2013 and has been complied with: Provided further that the requirement specified in this sub-regulation shall not be applicable to the director appointed pursuant to the order of a Court or a Tribunal or to a nominee director of the Government on the board of a listed entity, other than a public sector company, or to a nominee director of a financial sector regulator on the board of a listed entity: Provided further that the requirement specified in this sub-regulation shall not be applicable to a director nominated by a financial institution registered with or regulated by the Reserve Bank of India under a lending arrangement in its normal course of

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the five‑yearly shareholder approval requirement for director continuation and its listed exemptions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: With effect from April 1, 2024, the continuation of a director serving on the board of directors of a listed entity shall be subject to the approval by the shareholders in a general meeting at least once in every five years from the date of their appointment or reappointment, as the case may be: Provided that the continuation of the director serving on the board of directors of a listed entity as on March 31, 2024, without the approval of the shareholders for the last five years or more shall be subject to the approval of shareholders in the first general meeting to be held after March 31, 2024: Provided further that the requirement specified in this sub-regulation shall not be applicable to the Whole-Time Director, Managing Director, Manager, Independent Director or a Director retiring as per the sub-section (6) of section 152 of the Companies Act, 2013, if the approval of the shareholders for the reappointment or continuation of the aforesaid directors or Manager is otherwise provided for by the provisions of these regulations or the Companies Act, 2013 and has been complied with: Provided further that the requirement specified in this sub-regulation shall not be applicable to the director appointed pursuant to the order of a Court or a Tribunal or to a nominee director of the Government on the board of a listed entity, other than a public sector company, or to a nominee director of a financial sector regulator on the board of a listed entity: Provided further that the requirement specified in this sub-regulation shall not be applicable to a director nominated by a financial institution registered with or regulated by the Reserve Bank of India under a lending arrangement in its normal course of

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the overarching policy covering director appointment, re-appointment, and continuation requirements extracted from the regulations.

policies.policy_description: Sets out the procedures for filling director vacancies and ensuring compliance with applicable regulatory sub‑regulations.
policies.scope: Listed entities and their directors under the Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements framework
policies.objective: Ensure timely vacancy filling and regulatory compliance

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to fill director vacancies promptly
subpolicies.control: Any vacancy in the office of a director shall be filled by the listed entity at the earliest and in any case not later than three months from the date of such vacancy.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the primary vacancy‑filling timeline requirement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Any vacancy in the office of a director shall be filled by the listed entity at the earliest and in any case not later than three months from the date of such vacancy:

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Compliance condition when vacancy affects regulatory sub‑regulations
subpolicies.control: Provided that if the vacancy in the office of a director results in non‑compliance with the provisions of sub‑regulation (1) of regulation 18, sub‑regulation (1) or (2) of regulation 19, sub‑regulation (2) or (2A) of regulation 20 or sub‑regulation (2) or (3) of regulation 21, the listed entity shall ensure compliance at the earliest and in any case not later than three months from the date of such vacancy:

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Adds the compliance condition linked to specific regulatory sub‑regulations.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Provided that if the vacancy ... results in non‑compliance with the provisions of sub‑regulation (1) of regulation 18, ... the listed entity shall ensure compliance ... not later than three months from the date of such vacancy:

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Further condition on non‑compliance due to term expiration
subpolicies.control: Provided further that if the listed entity becomes non‑compliant with the requirement under sub‑regulation (1) of this regulation, sub‑regulation (1) or (2) of regulation 18, sub‑regulation (1) or (2) of regulation 19, sub‑regulation (2) or (2A) of regulation 20 or sub‑regulation (2) or (3) of regulation 21, due to expiration of the term of office of any director, the resulting vacancy shall be filled by the listed entity not later than the date such office is vacated:

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the deadline for filling vacancies when non‑compliance arises from term expiration.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Provided further that if the listed entity becomes non‑compliant ... due to expiration of the term of office of any director, the resulting vacancy shall be filled ... not later than the date such office is vacated:

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Exception when entity already complies without filling vacancy
subpolicies.control: Provided further that this sub‑regulation shall not apply if the listed entity fulfils the requirement under sub‑regulation (1) of this regulation, sub‑regulation (1) and (2) of regulation 19, sub‑regulation (2) and (2A) of regulation 20 and sub‑regulation (2) and (3) of regulation 21 without filling the vacancy.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the exception where compliance can be achieved without filling the vacancy.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Provided further that this sub‑regulation shall not apply if the listed entity fulfils the requirement ... without filling the vacancy.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the set of numbered requirements governing director vacancy handling and compliance obligations.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Any vacancy in the office of a director shall be filled by the listed entity at the earliest and in any case not later than three months from the date of such vacancy: Provided that if the vacancy ... results in non‑compliance ... the listed entity shall ensure compliance ... not later than three months from the date of such vacancy: Provided further that if the listed entity becomes non‑compliant ... the resulting vacancy shall be filled ... not later than the date such office is vacated: Provided further that this sub‑regulation shall not apply if the listed entity fulfils the requirement ... without filling the vacancy.

policies.policy_description: Mandates the minimum number of board meetings and the maximum interval between consecutive meetings.
policies.scope: Boards of listed entities
policies.objective: Ensure regular board oversight through frequent meetings

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum meetings per financial year and maximum interval
subpolicies.control: The board of directors shall meet at least four times a financial year, with a maximum time gap of one hundred and twenty days between any two consecutive meetings.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the specific meeting frequency and interval rule.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The board of directors shall meet at least four times a financial year, with a maximum time gap of one hundred and twenty days between any two consecutive meetings.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the requirement that boards must meet at least four times a financial year with a maximum gap of 120 days between meetings.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The board of directors shall meet at least four times a financial year, with a maximum time gap of one hundred and twenty days between any two consecutive meetings.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Collection of definitions that interpret key terms used throughout the Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements Regulations.
policies.scope: Applies to all listed entities and related parties under the SEBI Listing Regulations.
policies.objective: Provide clear meanings for terms such as 'Act', 'associate', 'Board', etc., to ensure consistent application of the regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'Act'
subpolicies.control: 'Act' means the Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992 (15 of 1992);

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the definition of the term 'Act' as a specific regulatory requirement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'Act' means the Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992 (15 of 1992);

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'associate'
subpolicies.control: 'associate' shall mean any entity which is an associate under sub-section (6) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013 or under the applicable accounting standards: Provided that this definition shall not be applicable for the units issued by mutual fund which are listed on a recognised stock exchange(s) for which the provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 1996 shall be applicable;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the definition of 'associate' and its exception for mutual fund units.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'associate' shall mean any entity which is an associate under sub-section (6) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013 or under the applicable accounting standards: Provided that this definition shall not be applicable for the units issued by mutual fund which are listed on a recognised stock exchange(s) for which the provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 1996 shall be applicable;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'Board'
subpolicies.control: 'Board' means the Securities and Exchange Board of India established under section 3 of the Act;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the SEBI Board as the regulatory authority.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'Board' means the Securities and Exchange Board of India established under section 3 of the Act;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'board of directors' or 'board of trustees'
subpolicies.control: 'board of directors' or 'board of trustees' shall mean the board of directors or board of trustees, whichever applicable, of the listed entity;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the governing bodies of listed entities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'board of directors' or 'board of trustees' shall mean the board of directors or board of trustees, whichever applicable, of the listed entity;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'chief executive officer' etc.
subpolicies.control: 'chief executive officer' or 'managing director' or 'manager' shall mean the person so appointed in terms of the Companies Act, 2013;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines key managerial positions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'chief executive officer' or 'managing director' or 'manager' shall mean the person so appointed in terms of the Companies Act, 2013;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'chief financial officer' etc.
subpolicies.control: 'chief financial officer' or 'whole time finance director' or 'head of finance', by whatever name called, shall mean the person heading and discharging the finance function of the listed entity as disclosed by it to the recognised stock exchange(s) in its filing under these regulations;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the finance head role and disclosure requirement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'chief financial officer' or 'whole time finance director' or 'head of finance', by whatever name called, shall mean the person heading and discharging the finance function of the listed entity as disclosed by it to the recognised stock exchange(s) in its filing under these regulations;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'committee'
subpolicies.control: 'committee' shall mean committee of board of directors or any other committee so constituted;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines committees within the board.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'committee' shall mean committee of board of directors or any other committee so constituted;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'designated securities'
subpolicies.control: 'designated securities' means specified securities, non-convertible debt securities, non-convertible redeemable preference shares, perpetual debt instrument, perpetual non-cumulative preference shares, Indian depository receipts, securitised debt instruments, [security receipts]1 units issued by mutual funds, 2[Zero Coupon Zero Principal Instruments] and any other securities as may be specified by the Board ;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Lists the categories of securities covered by the term.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'designated securities' means specified securities, non-convertible debt securities, non-convertible redeemable preference shares, perpetual debt instrument, perpetual non-cumulative preference shares, Indian depository receipts, securitised debt instruments, [security receipts]1 units issued by mutual funds, 2[Zero Coupon Zero Principal Instruments] and any other securities as may be specified by the Board ;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'financial year'
subpolicies.control: 'financial year' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under sub-section (41) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Adopts the definition from the Companies Act.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'financial year' shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under sub-section (41) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'fugitive economic offender'
subpolicies.control: (ia) 'fugitive economic offender' shall mean an individual who is declared a fugitive economic offender under section 12 of the Fugitive Economic Offenders Act, 2018 (17 of 2018);

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Introduces the term 'fugitive economic offender' with legal reference.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (ia) 'fugitive economic offender' shall mean an individual who is declared a fugitive economic offender under section 12 of the Fugitive Economic Offenders Act, 2018 (17 of 2018);

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'global depository receipts'
subpolicies.control: 'global depository receipts' means global depository receipts as defined in sub- section (44) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines GDRs referencing the Companies Act.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'global depository receipts' means global depository receipts as defined in sub- section (44) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'half yearly results'
subpolicies.control: 'half yearly results' means the financial results prepared in accordance with these regulations in respect of a half year;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the meaning of half-yearly results.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'half yearly results' means the financial results prepared in accordance with these regulations in respect of a half year;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'holding company'
subpolicies.control: 'holding company' means a holding company as defined in sub-section (46) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines holding company for regulatory purposes.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'holding company' means a holding company as defined in sub-section (46) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'Indian depository receipts'
subpolicies.control: 'Indian depository receipts' means Indian depository receipts as defined in sub- section(48) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines IDRs referencing the Companies Act.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'Indian depository receipts' means Indian depository receipts as defined in sub- section(48) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'mainstream media'
subpolicies.control: (ra) 'mainstream media' shall include print or electronic mode of the following: i. Newspapers registered with the Registrar of Newspapers for India; ii. News channels permitted by Ministry of Information and Broadcasting under Government of India; iii. Content published by the publisher of news and current affairs content as defined under the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021; and iv. Newspapers or news channels or news and current affairs content similarly registered or permitted or regulated, as the case may be, in jurisdictions outside India;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Broadens the scope of mainstream media for disclosure purposes.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (ra) 'mainstream media' shall include print or electronic mode of the following: i. Newspapers registered with the Registrar of Newspapers for India; ii. News channels permitted by Ministry of Information and Broadcasting under Government of India; iii. Content published by the publisher of news and current affairs content as defined under the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021; and iv. Newspapers or news channels or news and current affairs content similarly registered or permitted or regulated, as the case may be, in jurisdictions outside India;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'net worth'
subpolicies.control: 'net worth' means net worth as defined in sub-section (57) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Adopts the net worth definition from the Companies Act.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 'net worth' means net worth as defined in sub-section (57) of section 2 of the Companies Act, 2013;

subpolicies.control: 10[’non-convertible debt securities’ means ‘debt securities’ as defined under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Non-Convertible Securities) Regulations, 2021;]

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Links non-convertible debt securities to a specific regulation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 10[’non-convertible debt securities’ means ‘debt securities’ as defined under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Non-Convertible Securities) Regulations, 2021;]
