This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for the board to review compliance reports and provide necessary certificates
policies.scope: Board of directors of listed entities
policies.objective: Ensure ongoing compliance with applicable laws and regulations

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board must periodically review compliance reports prepared by the entity.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors shall periodically review compliance reports pertaining to all laws applicable to the listed entity, prepared by the listed entity as well as steps taken by the listed entity to rectify instances of non-compliances.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from clause (3) which specifies the board's duty to review compliance reports.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (3) The board of directors shall periodically review compliance reports pertaining to all laws applicable to the listed entity, prepared by the listed entity as well as steps taken by the listed entity to rectify instances of non-compliances.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board must be provided with minimum information as specified.
subpolicies.control: The minimum information to be placed before the board of directors is specified in Part A of Schedule II.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from clause (7) which sets out the minimum information required before the board.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (7) The minimum information to be placed before the board of directors is specified in Part A of Schedule II.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: CEO and CFO must provide a compliance certificate to the board as specified.
subpolicies.control: The chief executive officer and the chief financial officer shall provide the compliance certificate to the board of directors as specified in Part B of Schedule II.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from clause (8) which requires the CEO and CFO to furnish a compliance certificate.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (8) The chief executive officer and the chief financial officer shall provide the compliance certificate to the board of directors as specified in Part B of Schedule II.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identified from clauses (3), (7), and (8) which mandate periodic compliance review, provision of compliance certificates, and minimum information before the board.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (3) The board of directors shall periodically review compliance reports pertaining to all laws applicable to the listed entity, prepared by the listed entity as well as steps taken by the listed entity to rectify instances of non-compliances.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for orderly succession planning for board and senior management appointments
policies.scope: Board of directors of listed entities
policies.objective: Ensure continuity and preparedness in leadership succession

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board must ensure succession plans for board and senior management.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors shall satisfy itself that plans are in place for orderly succession for appointment to the board of directors and senior management.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from clause (4) which sets out the succession planning obligation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (4) The board of directors shall satisfy itself that plans are in place for orderly succession for appointment to the board of directors and senior management.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identified from clause (4) which mandates the board to satisfy itself that succession plans are in place.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (4) The board of directors shall satisfy itself that plans are in place for orderly succession for appointment to the board of directors and senior management.

policies.policy_description: Establishment and content of a code of conduct for directors and senior management
policies.scope: Board of directors and senior management of listed entities
policies.objective: Define expected conduct and duties, especially for independent directors

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board must lay down a code of conduct for directors and senior management.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors shall lay down a code of conduct for all members of board of directors and senior management of the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from clause (5)(a) which mandates the creation of a code of conduct.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (5) (a) The board of directors shall lay down a code of conduct for all members of board of directors and senior management of the listed entity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Code of conduct must incorporate duties of independent directors as per Companies Act 2013.
subpolicies.control: The code of conduct shall suitably incorporate the duties of independent directors as laid down in the Companies Act, 2013.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from clause (5)(b) which requires the code to incorporate independent director duties.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (5) (b) The code of conduct shall suitably incorporate the duties of independent directors as laid down in the Companies Act, 2013.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identified from clauses (5)(a) and (5)(b) which require a code of conduct and its incorporation of independent director duties.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (5) (a) The board of directors shall lay down a code of conduct for all members of board of directors and senior management of the listed entity. (5) (b) The code of conduct shall suitably incorporate the duties of independent directors as laid down in the Companies Act, 2013.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for remuneration of directors and shareholder approval thresholds
policies.scope: Board of directors and listed entities
policies.objective: Regulate payment of fees and compensation and obtain shareholder approval where required

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board must recommend fees for non-executive directors and obtain shareholder approval.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors shall recommend all fees or compensation, if any, paid to non-executive directors, including independent directors, and shall require approval of shareholders in general meeting.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from clause (6)(a) which sets out the requirement to recommend fees and obtain shareholder approval.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (6) (a) The board of directors shall recommend all fees or compensation, if any, paid to non-executive directors, including independent directors, and shall require approval of shareholders in general meeting.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Shareholder approval not required for sitting fees within prescribed limits.
subpolicies.control: The requirement of obtaining approval of shareholders in general meeting shall not apply to payment of sitting fees to non-executive directors, if made within the limits prescribed under the Companies Act, 2013 for payment of sitting fees without approval of the Central Government.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from clause (6)(b) which provides an exception for sitting fees.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (6) (b) The requirement of obtaining approval of shareholders in general meeting shall not apply to payment of sitting fees to non-executive directors, if made within the limits prescribed under the Companies Act, 2013 for payment of sitting fees without approval of the Central Government.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Shareholder approval must specify limits for stock options to non-executive directors.
subpolicies.control: The approval of shareholders mentioned in clause (a), shall specify the limits for the maximum number of stock options that may be granted to non-executive directors, in any financial year and in aggregate.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from clause (6)(c) which requires shareholder approval to specify stock option limits.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (6) (c) The approval of shareholders mentioned in clause (a), shall specify the limits for the maximum number of stock options that may be granted to non-executive directors, in any financial year and in aggregate.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identified from clauses (5)(c), (6)(a), (6)(b), and (6)(c) which cover shareholder approval for fees, exceptions for sitting fees, and stock option limits.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (6) (a) The board of directors shall recommend all fees or compensation, if any, paid to non-executive directors, including independent directors, and shall require approval of shareholders in general meeting. (6) (b) The requirement of obtaining approval of shareholders in general meeting shall not apply to payment of sitting fees to non-executive directors, if made within the limits prescribed under the Companies Act, 2013 for payment of sitting fees without approval of the Central Government. (6) (c) The approval of shareholders mentioned in clause (a), shall specify the limits for the maximum number of stock options that may be granted to non-executive directors, in any financial year and in aggregate.

policies.policy_description: Procedures for risk assessment communication and oversight
policies.scope: Board of directors and listed entities
policies.objective: Enable identification, assessment, and monitoring of risks

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Procedures to inform board members about risk assessment and minimization.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall lay down procedures to inform members of board of directors about risk assessment and minimization procedures.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from clause (9)(a) which mandates procedures for risk assessment communication.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (9) (a) The listed entity shall lay down procedures to inform members of board of directors about risk assessment and minimization procedures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board responsible for framing, implementing, and monitoring risk management plan.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors shall be responsible for framing, implementing and monitoring the risk management plan for the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from clause (9)(b) which assigns risk management responsibilities to the board.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (9) (b) The board of directors shall be responsible for framing, implementing and monitoring the risk management plan for the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identified from clauses (9)(a) and (9)(b) which require procedures for risk assessment and board oversight of risk management.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (9) (a) The listed entity shall lay down procedures to inform members of board of directors about risk assessment and minimization procedures. (9) (b) The board of directors shall be responsible for framing, implementing and monitoring the risk management plan for the listed entity.

policies.policy_description: Evaluation process for independent directors by the board
policies.scope: Board of directors of listed entities
policies.objective: Assess performance and independence of independent directors

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board evaluates independent directors on performance and independence, with non-participation rule.
subpolicies.control: The evaluation of independent directors shall be done by the entire board of directors which shall include - (a) performance of the directors; and (b) fulfillment of the independence criteria as specified in these regulations and their independence from the management: Provided that in the above evaluation, the directors who are subject to evaluation shall not participate.

policies.policy_description: Maximum number of directorships a person may hold and related exclusions
policies.scope: Directors and independent directors of listed entities
policies.objective: Prevent excessive board participation and manage conflicts of interest

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limits on the number of directorships a person may hold, including cumulative counting and exclusions.
subpolicies.control: Maximum number of directorships. 17A. The directors of listed entities shall comply with the following conditions with respect to the maximum number of directorships, including any alternate directorships that can be held by them at any point of time - (1) A person shall not be a director in more than eight listed entities with effect from April 1, 2019 and in not more than seven listed entities with effect from April 1, 2020: Provided that a person shall not serve as an independent director in more than seven listed entities. (2) Notwithstanding the above, any person who is serving as a whole time director / managing director in any listed entity shall serve as an independent director in not more than three listed entities. Explanation 148[1]- For the purpose of this regulation, the count for the number of listed entities on which a person is a director / independent director shall be cumulative of those whose equity shares are listed on a stock exchange and ‘high value debt listed entities’. Explanation (2) - For the purpose of this regulation, the directorship(s) held by a person on an ex-officio basis due to statute or applicable contractual framework in case of public sector undertakings and entities set up under a public private partnership arrangement shall not be included in calculating the maximum number of directorships: Provided that nothing in this provision relating to HVDLE shall come into effect for a period of six months from the date of publication of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) (Amendment) Regulations, 2025 in the Official Gazette or the date of Annual General Meeting, whichever is

policies.policy_description: Requirements for constituting and operating a qualified independent audit committee for listed entities.
policies.scope: Listed entities
policies.objective: Ensure independent oversight, financial literacy, and proper functioning of the audit committee.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum number of directors on the audit committee.
subpolicies.control: The audit committee shall have minimum three directors as members.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the statutory minimum director count requirement for the audit committee.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The audit committee shall have minimum three directors as members.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Independent director composition requirement.
subpolicies.control: At least two-thirds of the members of audit committee shall be independent directors and in case of a listed entity having outstanding SR equity shares, the audit committee shall only comprise of independent directors.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the proportion of independent directors and the exclusive independence requirement for entities with SR equity shares.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: At least two-thirds of the members of audit committee shall be independent directors 152[At least] ... and in case of a listed entity having outstanding SR equity shares, the audit committee shall only comprise of independent directors]

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Financial literacy and accounting expertise requirement.
subpolicies.control: All members of audit committee shall be financially literate and at least one member shall have accounting or related financial management expertise.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates financial literacy for all members and requires at least one member with accounting expertise.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: All members of audit committee shall be financially literate and at least one member shall have accounting or related financial management expertise.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of financially literate.
subpolicies.control: For the purpose of this regulation, “financially literate” shall mean the ability to read and understand basic financial statements i.e. balance sheet, profit and loss account, and statement of cash flows.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Provides the definition of financial literacy for audit committee members.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: For the purpose of this regulation, “financially literate” shall mean the ability to read and understand basic financial statements i.e. balance sheet, profit and loss account, and statement of cash flows.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of accounting or financial management expertise.
subpolicies.control: A member shall be considered to have accounting or related financial management expertise if he or she possesses experience in finance or accounting, or requisite professional certification in accounting, or any other comparable experience or background which results in the individual’s financial sophistication, including being or having been a chief executive officer, chief financial officer or other senior officer with financial oversight responsibilities.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the criteria for accounting or financial management expertise.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: For the purpose of this regulation , a member shall be considered to have accounting or related financial management expertise if he or she possesses experience in finance or accounting, or requisite professional certification in accounting, or any other comparable experience or background which results in the individual’s financial sophistication, including being or having been a chief executive officer, chief financial officer or other senior officer with financial oversight responsibilities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Chairperson independence and AGM presence.
subpolicies.control: The chairperson of the audit committee shall be an independent director and shall be present at Annual general meeting to answer shareholder queries.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires the chairperson to be independent and to attend the AGM to answer shareholder queries.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The chairperson of the audit committee shall be an independent director and he/she shall be present at Annual general meeting to answer shareholder queries.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Meeting frequency and interval.
subpolicies.control: The audit committee shall meet at least four times in a financial year and not more than one hundred and twenty days shall elapse between two consecutive meetings.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets the minimum number of meetings per financial year and the maximum interval between meetings.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The audit committee shall meet at least four times in a financial year and not more than one hundred and twenty days shall elapse between two consecutive meetings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Quorum requirement.
subpolicies.control: The quorum for audit committee meeting shall either be two members or one third of the members of the audit committee, whichever is greater, with at least two independent directors.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the quorum as the greater of two members or one third of members, with a minimum of two independent directors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The quorum for audit committee meeting shall either be two members or one third of the members of the audit committee, whichever is greater, with at least two independent directors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Powers of investigation and information gathering.
subpolicies.control: The audit committee shall have powers to investigate any activity within its terms of reference, seek information from any employee, obtain outside legal or other professional advice and secure attendance of outsiders with relevant expertise, if it considers necessary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Grants the audit committee authority to investigate, obtain information, and secure external expertise as needed.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The audit committee shall have powers to investigate any activity within its terms of reference, seek information from any employee, obtain outside legal or other professional advice and secure attendance of outsiders with relevant expertise, if it considers necessary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the comprehensive set of rules governing the composition, independence, expertise, meeting frequency, and powers of the audit committee for listed entities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every listed entity shall constitute a qualified and independent audit committee in accordance with the terms of reference, subject to the following:

policies.policy_description: Regulations specifying composition, meeting frequency, quorum, responsibilities, and powers of the Risk Management Committee for listed entities.
policies.scope: Applies to top 1000 listed entities and high value debt listed entities.
policies.objective: Ensure effective risk oversight and management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines minimum membership and independence requirements for the committee.
subpolicies.control: The Risk Management Committee shall have minimum three members with majority of them being members of the board of directors, including at least one independent director and in case of a listed entity having outstanding SR equity shares, at least two thirds of the Risk Management Committee shall comprise independent directors.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the composition rule from sub-regulation (2).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The Risk Management Committee shall have minimum three members with majority of them being members of the board of directors, including at least one independent director and in case of a listed entity having outstanding SR equity shares, at least two thirds of the Risk Management Committee shall comprise independent directors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies chairperson must be a board member and senior executives may be members.
subpolicies.control: The Chairperson of the Risk management committee shall be a member of the board of directors and senior executives of the listed entity may be members of the committee.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the chairperson requirement from sub-regulation (3).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The Chairperson of the Risk management committee shall be a member of the board of directors and senior executives of the listed entity may be members of the committee.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires at least twice per financial year meetings.
subpolicies.control: The risk management committee shall meet at least twice in a financial year.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Derived from sub-regulation (3A).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The risk management committee shall meet at least twice in a financial year.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Quorum is two members or one third of members, whichever is higher, including at least one board member.
subpolicies.control: The quorum for a meeting of the Risk Management Committee shall be either two members or one third of the members of the committee, whichever is higher, including at least one member of the board of directors in attendance.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts quorum rule from sub-regulation (3B).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The quorum for a meeting of the Risk Management Committee shall be either two members or one third of the members of the committee, whichever is higher, including at least one member of the board of directors in attendance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: No more than 210 days between consecutive meetings.
subpolicies.control: The meetings of the risk management committee shall be conducted in such a manner that on a continuous basis not more than two hundred and ten days shall elapse between any two consecutive meetings.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the maximum interval rule from sub-regulation (3C).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The meetings of the risk management committee shall be conducted in such a manner that on a continuous basis not more than two hundred and ten days shall elapse between any two consecutive meetings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Committee may monitor and review risk management plan and delegate functions, including cyber security.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors shall define the role and responsibility of the Risk Management Committee and may delegate monitoring and reviewing of the risk management plan to the committee and such other functions as it may deem fit such function shall specifically cover cyber security: Provided that the role and responsibilities of the Risk Management Committee shall mandatorily include the performance of functions specified in Part D of Schedule II.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Summarizes role and delegated functions from sub-regulation (4).

policies.policy_description: Regulations establishing a whistleblower policy for directors and employees, including safeguards against victimization.
policies.scope: Applies to listed entities.
policies.objective: Provide a safe channel for reporting concerns and protect reporters.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Listed entity must formulate a vigil mechanism for directors and employees.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall formulate a vigil mechanism /whistle blower policy for directors and employees to report genuine concerns.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the mandatory formulation of vigil mechanism from sub‑regulation (1).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall formulate a vigil mechanism /whistle blower policy for directors and employees to report genuine concerns.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mechanism must protect directors/employees from retaliation and allow access to audit committee chair.
subpolicies.control: The vigil mechanism shall provide for adequate safeguards against victimization of director(s) or employee(s) or any other person who avail the mechanism and also provide for direct access to the chairperson of the audit committee in appropriate or exceptional cases.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures protection and access provisions from sub‑regulation (2).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The vigil mechanism shall provide for adequate safeguards against victimization of director(s) or employee(s) or any other person who avail the mechanism and also provide for direct access to the chairperson of the audit committee in appropriate or exceptional cases.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the mandatory vigil mechanism and anti‑victimization safeguards for listed companies.

policies.policy_description: Rules governing materiality thresholds and approval process for related party transactions.
policies.scope: Applies to listed entities.
policies.objective: Ensure transparency and fairness in related party dealings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines material transaction limits based on rupees 1,000 crore or 10% of turnover, with SME-specific lower threshold.
subpolicies.control: A transaction with a related party shall be considered material, if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceeds rupees one thousand crore or ten per cent of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity, whichever is lower: Provided that a transaction with a related party shall be considered material, if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceeds rupees one thousand crore or ten per cent of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity, whichever is lower.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the materiality definition from sub‑regulation (1).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A transaction with a related party shall be considered material, if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceeds rupees one thousand crore or ten per cent of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity, whichever is lower: Provided that a transaction with a related party shall be considered material, if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceeds rupees one thousand crore or ten per cent of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity, whichever is lower.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: For SME listed entities, material threshold is Rs 50 crore or 10% of turnover, effective from April 1, 2025.
subpolicies.control: Provided further that with effect from April 01, 2025, in case of a listed entity which has listed its specified securities on the SME Exchange, a transaction with a related party shall be considered material, if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceeds Rupees fifty crore or ten per cent. of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity, whichever is lower.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the SME-specific materiality rule from sub‑regulation (1) amendment.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Provided further that with effect from April 01, 2025, in case of a listed entity which has listed its specified securities on the SME Exchange, a transaction with a related party shall be considered material, if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceeds Rupees fifty crore or ten per cent. of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity, whichever is lower.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Transactions involving brand usage or royalty payments are material if they exceed 5% of turnover.
subpolicies.control: [with effect from July 01, 2019] a transaction involving payments made to a related party with respect to brand usage or royalty shall be considered material if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceed five percent of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the specific materiality rule for brand usage/royalty from sub‑regulation (1A).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: [with effect from July 01, 2019] a transaction involving payments made to a related party with respect to brand usage or royalty shall be considered material if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceed five percent of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All related party transactions and material modifications require audit committee approval.
subpolicies.control: All related party transactions and subsequent material modifications shall require prior approval of the audit committee of the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the approval requirement from sub‑regulation (2).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: All related party transactions and subsequent material modifications shall require prior approval of the audit committee of the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Describes materiality criteria and audit committee approval requirements for related party transactions.

policies.policy_description: Defines materiality thresholds, approval, ratification, and omnibus approval processes for related party transactions of listed entities and their subsidiaries.
policies.scope: Listed entities, their subsidiaries, and audit committees
policies.objective: Ensure proper governance, disclosure, and approval of related party transactions

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines when a related party transaction is considered material based on turnover thresholds.
subpolicies.control: A transaction with a related party shall be considered material if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceeds ten percent of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This requirement establishes the materiality threshold for related party transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Explanation. - A transaction with a related party shall be considered material if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceeds ten percent of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Only independent directors on the audit committee may approve related party transactions.
subpolicies.control: [Provided that only those members of the audit committee, who are independent directors, shall approve related party transactions.]

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the category of audit committee members authorized to approve transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: [Provided that only those members of the audit committee, who are independent directors, shall approve related party transactions.]

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prior approval required for subsidiary-related party transactions exceeding 10% of consolidated turnover.
subpolicies.control: a related party transaction to which the subsidiary of a listed entity is a party but the listed entity is not a party, shall require prior approval of the audit committee of the listed entity if the value of such transaction(s) ... exceeds ten per cent of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets a threshold for prior approval involving subsidiary transactions based on consolidated turnover.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: a related party transaction to which the subsidiary ... exceeds ten per cent of the annual consolidated turnover ...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: From April 1, 2023, prior approval required if threshold exceeded based on subsidiary's standalone turnover.
subpolicies.control: with effect from April 1, 2023, a related party transaction to which the subsidiary of a listed entity is a party but the listed entity is not a party, shall require prior approval ... exceeds ten per cent of the annual standalone turnover, as per the last audited financial statements of the subsidiary;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Updates the threshold to use subsidiary's standalone turnover for transactions after April 1, 2023.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: with effect from April 1, 2023, ... exceeds ten per cent of the annual standalone turnover ...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prior approval not required if certain regulatory provisions apply.
subpolicies.control: prior approval of the audit committee of the listed entity shall not be required for a related party transaction to which the subsidiary of a listed entity is a party but the listed entity is not a party, if regulation 23 and sub-regulation (2) of regulation 15 of these regulations are applicable to such listed subsidiary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the exception to the prior approval requirement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: prior approval ... shall not be required ... if regulation 23 and sub-regulation (2) of regulation 15 ... are applicable

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Remuneration and sitting fees to directors etc. are exempt from audit committee approval if not material.
subpolicies.control: remuneration and sitting fees paid by the listed entity or its subsidiary to its director, key managerial personnel or senior management, except who is part of promoter or promoter group, shall not require approval of the audit committee provided that the same is not material in terms of the provisions of sub-regulation (1) of this regulation.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Exempts certain payments from approval when not material.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: remuneration and sitting fees paid ... shall not require approval of the audit committee provided that the same is not material ...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which independent directors may ratify related party transactions within three months.
subpolicies.control: The members of the audit committee, who are independent directors, may ratify related party transactions within three months from the date of the transaction or in the immediate next meeting of the audit committee, whichever is earlier, subject to the following conditions: (i) the value of the ratified transaction(s) with a related party, whether entered into individually or taken together, during a financial year shall not exceed rupees one crore; (ii) the transaction is not material in terms of the provisions of sub-regulation (1) of this regulation; (iii) rationale for inability to seek prior approval for the transaction shall be placed before the audit committee at the time of seeking ratification; (iv) the details of ratification shall be disclosed along with the disclosures of related party transactions in terms of the provisions of sub-regulation (9) of this regulation; (v) any other condition as specified by the audit committee: Provided that failure to seek ratification of the audit committee shall render the transaction voidable at the option of the audit committee and if the transaction is with a related party to any director, or is authorised by any other director, the director(s) concerned shall indemnify the listed entity against any loss incurred by it.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Lists the specific conditions for ratification of transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The members of the audit committee, who are independent directors, may ratify related party transactions within three months ... (i) ... (ii) ... (iii) ... (iv) ... (v) ... indemnify ...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Audit committee must lay down criteria for granting omnibus approval for repetitive transactions.
subpolicies.control: the audit committee shall lay down the criteria for granting the omnibus approval in line with the policy on related party transactions and such approval shall be applicable in respect of transactions which are repetitive in nature;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the need to establish criteria for omnibus approvals.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the audit committee shall lay down the criteria for granting the omnibus approval in line with the policy on related party transactions ...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Audit committee must satisfy itself that the omnibus approval is in the interest of the listed entity.
subpolicies.control: the audit committee shall satisfy itself regarding the need for such omnibus approval and that such approval is in the interest of the listed entity;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires the committee to assess the interest of the entity before granting approval.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the audit committee shall satisfy itself regarding the need for such omnibus approval and that such approval is in the interest of the listed entity;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details that must be included in the omnibus approval, including parties, nature, period, amount, price formula, etc.
subpolicies.control: the omnibus approval shall specify: (i) the name(s) of the related party, nature of transaction, period of transaction, maximum amount of transactions that shall be entered into, (ii) the indicative base price / current contracted price and the formula for variation in the price if any; and (iii) such other conditions as the audit committee may deem fit: Provided that where the need for related party transaction cannot be foreseen and aforesaid details are not available, audit committee may grant omnibus approval for such transactions subject to their value not exceeding rupees one crore per transaction.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the content required in omnibus approvals and the value ceiling for unforeseeable transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the omnibus approval shall specify: (i) the name(s) of the related party, nature of transaction, period of transaction, maximum amount ... (ii) the indicative base price ... (iii) such other conditions ... Provided that where the need ... not exceed rupees one crore per transaction.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Audit committee must review related party transactions under each omnibus approval at least quarterly.
subpolicies.control: the audit committee shall review, at least on a quarterly basis, the details of related party transactions entered into by the listed entity or its subsidiary pursuant to each of the omnibus approvals given.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates periodic review of transactions approved via omnibus.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the audit committee shall review, at least on a quarterly basis, the details of related party transactions entered into ... pursuant to each of the omnibus approvals given.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Omnibus approvals are valid for up to one year and require fresh approvals thereafter.
subpolicies.control: Such omnibus approvals shall be valid for a period not exceeding one year and shall require fresh approvals after the expiry of one year:

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets the validity period and renewal requirement for omnibus approvals.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Such omnibus approvals shall be valid for a period not exceeding one year and shall require fresh approvals after the expiry of one year:

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identified the comprehensive governance framework for related party transactions, covering materiality, approval, ratification, and omnibus approvals as described in the source text.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Explanation. - A transaction with a related party shall be considered material if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceeds ten percent of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity.

policies.policy_title: Related Party Transaction Approval and Exemptions
policies.policy_description: Regulations governing approval and exemption of related party transactions involving listed entities and their subsidiaries.
policies.scope: All related party transactions of listed entities and their subsidiaries
policies.objective: Ensure shareholder approval where required and define exemptions from sub-regulation applicability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Shareholder approval is not required for certain related party transactions involving listed subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: prior approval of the shareholders of a listed entity shall not be required for a related party transaction to which the listed subsidiary is a party but the listed entity is not a party, if regulation 23 and sub-regulation (2) of regulation 15 of these regulations are applicable to such listed subsidiary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the exemption from shareholder approval for specific related party transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: prior approval of the shareholders of a listed entity shall not be required for a related party transaction to which the listed subsidiary is a party but the listed entity is not a party

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Lists cases where sub-regulations (2)-(4) do not apply.
subpolicies.control: The provisions of sub-regulations (2), (3) and (4) shall not be applicable in the following cases: (a) transactions entered into between two public sector companies; (b) transactions entered into between a holding company and its wholly owned subsidiary whose accounts are consolidated with such holding company and placed before the shareholders at the general meeting for approval; (c) transactions entered into between two wholly-owned subsidiaries of the listed holding company, whose accounts are consolidated with such holding company and placed before the shareholders at the general meeting for approval; (d) transactions which are in the nature of payment of statutory dues, statutory fees or statutory charges entered into between an entity on one hand and the Central Government or any State Government or any combination thereof on the other hand; (e) transactions entered into between a public sector company on one hand and the Central Government or any State Government or any combination thereof on the other hand.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the specific exemptions enumerated in sub-regulation (5).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The provisions of sub-regulations (2), (3) and (4) shall not be applicable in the following cases: (a) transactions entered into between two public sector companies;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: States that the regulation applies to all prospective transactions.
subpolicies.control: The provisions of this regulation shall be applicable to all prospective transactions.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the forward‑looking scope of the regulation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The provisions of this regulation shall be applicable to all prospective transactions.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the rules for shareholder approval exemptions and applicability of sub-regulations concerning related party transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: prior approval of the shareholders of a listed entity shall not be required for a related party transaction to which the listed subsidiary is a party but the listed entity is not a party

policies.policy_title: Disclosure Requirements for Related Party Transactions
policies.policy_description: Mandates disclosure of related party transactions by listed entities in a prescribed format and frequency.
policies.scope: All related party transactions of listed entities, including high‑value debt entities.
policies.objective: Ensure timely and adequate disclosure to stock exchanges and investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Listed entities must submit disclosures in the format specified by the Board and publish them on their website, with frequency every six months.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchanges disclosures of related party transactions in the format as specified by the Board from time to time, and publish the same on its website: Provided that a ‘high value debt listed entity’ shall submit such disclosures along with its standalone financial results for the half year: Provided further that the listed entity shall make such disclosures every six months within fifteen days from the date of publication of its standalone and consolidated financial results: Provided further that the listed entity shall make such disclosures every six months on the date of publication of its standalone and consolidated financial results with effect from April 1, 2023.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the required disclosure format, publication method, and six‑monthly frequency.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchanges disclosures of related party transactions in the format as specified by the Board from time to time, and publish the same on its website

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: High value debt listed entities must include disclosures with half‑year financial results.
subpolicies.control: Provided that a ‘high value debt listed entity’ shall submit such disclosures along with its standalone financial results for the half year.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Adds an extra filing requirement for high‑value debt entities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Provided that a ‘high value debt listed entity’ shall submit such disclosures along with its standalone financial results for the half year

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Remuneration and sitting fees to non‑promoter directors, key managerial personnel, or senior management need not be disclosed if not material.
subpolicies.control: Provided further that the remuneration and sitting fees paid by the listed entity or its subsidiary to its director, key managerial personnel or senior management, except who is part of promoter or promoter group, shall not require disclosure under this sub-regulation provided that the same is not material in terms of the provisions of sub-regulation (1) of this regulation.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Exempts certain remuneration payments from disclosure when immaterial.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Provided further that the remuneration and sitting fees paid by the listed entity or its subsidiary to its director, key managerial personnel or senior management, except who is part of promoter or promoter group, shall not require disclosure under this sub-regulation provided that the same is not material in terms of the provisions of sub-regulation (1) of this regulation.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the disclosure obligations, format, frequency, and exemptions for remuneration.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchanges disclosures of related party transactions in the format as specified by the Board from time to time, and publish the same on its website

policies.policy_title: Corporate Governance for Unlisted Material Subsidiaries
policies.policy_description: Sets governance standards for listed entities regarding their unlisted material subsidiaries.
policies.scope: Unlisted material subsidiaries of listed entities, whether incorporated in India or abroad.
policies.objective: Ensure adequate board representation, audit oversight, and transparency of significant transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: At least one independent director on the listed entity's board must also serve on the board of an unlisted material subsidiary.
subpolicies.control: At least one independent director on the board of directors of the listed entity shall be a director on the board of directors of an unlisted material subsidiary, whether incorporated in India or not.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates independent director representation on unlisted material subsidiaries.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: At least one independent director on the board of directors of the listed entity shall be a director on the board of directors of an unlisted material subsidiary, whether incorporated in India or not.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Audit committee must review financial statements, especially investments, of the unlisted subsidiary.
subpolicies.control: The audit committee of the listed entity shall also review the financial statements, in particular, the investments made by the unlisted subsidiary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires audit committee oversight of unlisted subsidiary investments.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The audit committee of the listed entity shall also review the financial statements, in particular, the investments made by the unlisted subsidiary.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minutes of unlisted subsidiary board meetings must be placed before the listed entity's board.
subpolicies.control: The minutes of the meetings of the board of directors of the unlisted subsidiary shall be placed at the meeting of the board of directors of the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Ensures transparency by presenting subsidiary board minutes to the listed entity's board.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The minutes of the meetings of the board of directors of the unlisted subsidiary shall be placed at the meeting of the board of directors of the listed entity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Management must periodically inform the listed entity's board of significant transactions of the unlisted subsidiary.
subpolicies.control: The management of the unlisted subsidiary shall periodically bring to the notice of the board of directors of the listed entity, a statement of all significant transactions and arrangements entered into by the unlisted subsidiary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires periodic reporting of significant transactions by the unlisted subsidiary to the listed entity's board.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The management of the unlisted subsidiary shall periodically bring to the notice of the board of directors of the listed entity, a statement of all significant transactions and arrangements entered into by the unlisted subsidiary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Outlines board composition, audit committee review, minutes placement, and management reporting obligations for unlisted subsidiaries.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: At least one independent director on the board of directors of the listed entity shall be a director on the board of directors of an unlisted material subsidiary, whether incorporated in India or not.

policies.policy_description: Defines what constitutes a significant transaction or arrangement for unlisted material subsidiaries.
policies.scope: Unlisted material subsidiaries of listed entities
policies.objective: Identify transactions exceeding 10% of financial metrics for regulatory scrutiny.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of significant transaction threshold.
subpolicies.control: For the purpose of this regulation, the term “significant transaction or arrangement” shall mean any individual transaction or arrangement that exceeds or is likely to exceed ten percent of the total revenues or total expenses or total assets or total liabilities, as the case may be, of the unlisted subsidiary for the immediately preceding accounting year.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This subpolicy extracts the explicit definition of ‘significant transaction or arrangement’ from clause (5).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: For the purpose of this regulation, the term “significant transaction or arrangement” shall mean any individual transaction or arrangement that exceeds or is likely to exceed ten percent of the total revenues or total expenses or total assets or total liabilities, as the case may be, of the unlisted subsidiary for the immediately preceding accounting year.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: The regulation explicitly defines ‘significant transaction or arrangement’ as any transaction exceeding or likely to exceed ten percent of the subsidiary’s financial metrics, which is the core policy.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: For the purpose of this regulation, the term “significant transaction or arrangement” shall mean any individual transaction or arrangement that exceeds or is likely to exceed ten percent of the total revenues or total expenses or total assets or total liabilities, as the case may be, of the unlisted subsidiary for the immediately preceding accounting year.

policies.policy_description: Regulates disposal of shares and loss of control over material subsidiaries.
policies.scope: Listed entities with material subsidiaries
policies.objective: Ensure shareholder approval for reductions below 50% or cessation of control, except under approved schemes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Threshold for shareholding reduction requiring special resolution.
subpolicies.control: A listed entity shall not dispose of shares in its material subsidiary resulting in reduction of its shareholding (either on its own or together with other subsidiaries) to less than fifty percent or cease the exercise of control over the subsidiary without passing a special resolution in its General Meeting except in cases where such divestment is made under a scheme of arrangement duly approved by a Court/Tribunal[, or under a resolution plan duly approved under section 31 of the Insolvency Code and such an event is disclosed to the recognized stock exchanges within one day of the resolution plan being approved]

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This subpolicy captures the shareholding reduction and control cessation requirement from clause (6).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A listed entity shall not dispose of shares in its material subsidiary resulting in reduction of its shareholding (either on its own or together with other subsidiaries) to less than fifty percent or cease the exercise of control over the subsidiary without passing a special resolution in its General Meeting except in cases where such divestment is made under a scheme of arrangement duly approved by a Court/Tribunal[, or under a resolution plan duly approved under section 31 of the Insolvency Code and such an event is disclosed to the recognized stock exchanges within one day of the resolution plan being approved]

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (6) sets out the conditions under which a listed entity may not dispose of shares or cease control without a special resolution, except for approved schemes.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A listed entity shall not dispose of shares in its material subsidiary resulting in reduction of its shareholding (either on its own or together with other subsidiaries) to less than fifty percent or cease the exercise of control over the subsidiary without passing a special resolution in its General Meeting except in cases where such divestment is made under a scheme of arrangement duly approved by a Court/Tribunal[, or under a resolution plan duly approved under section 31 of the Insolvency Code and such an event is disclosed to the recognized stock exchanges within one day of the resolution plan being approved]

policies.policy_description: Requires shareholder approval for disposal of assets exceeding 20% of subsidiary assets.
policies.scope: Material subsidiaries of listed entities
policies.objective: Obtain special resolution approval for large asset disposals, unless under approved scheme.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Asset disposal threshold requiring shareholder approval.
subpolicies.control: Selling, disposing and leasing of assets amounting to more than twenty percent of the assets of the material subsidiary on an aggregate basis during a financial year shall require prior approval of shareholders by way of special resolution, unless the sale/disposal/lease is made under a scheme of arrangement duly approved by a Court/Tribunal[, or under a resolution plan duly approved under section 31 of the Insolvency Code and such an event is disclosed to the recognized stock exchanges within one day of the resolution plan being approved]

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This subpolicy extracts the asset sale approval requirement from clause (6).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Selling, disposing and leasing of assets amounting to more than twenty percent of the assets of the material subsidiary on an aggregate basis during a financial year shall require prior approval of shareholders by way of special resolution, unless the sale/disposal/lease is made under a scheme of arrangement duly approved by a Court/Tribunal[, or under a resolution plan duly approved under section 31 of the Insolvency Code and such an event is disclosed to the recognized stock exchanges within one day of the resolution plan being approved]

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (6) imposes a 20% asset threshold requiring shareholder approval for disposals, which is treated as a separate policy.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Selling, disposing and leasing of assets amounting to more than twenty percent of the assets of the material subsidiary on an aggregate basis during a financial year shall require prior approval of shareholders by way of special resolution, unless the sale/disposal/lease is made under a scheme of arrangement duly approved by a Court/Tribunal[, or under a resolution plan duly approved under section 31 of the Insolvency Code and such an event is disclosed to the recognized stock exchanges within one day of the resolution plan being approved]

policies.policy_description: Mandates secretarial audit for listed entities and material unlisted subsidiaries, including auditor qualifications and service restrictions.
policies.scope: Listed entities and material unlisted subsidiaries incorporated in India
policies.objective: Ensure secretarial audit is performed by qualified auditors and reported with the annual report, with restrictions on additional services.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandatory secretarial audit by a qualified auditor.
subpolicies.control: Every listed entity and its material unlisted subsidiaries incorporated in India shall undertake Secretarial Audit by a Secretarial Auditor who shall be a Peer Reviewed Company Secretary and shall annex a Secretarial Audit Report in such form as specified, with the annual report of the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This subpolicy captures the requirement to conduct secretarial audit and annex the report with the annual report.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every listed entity and its material unlisted subsidiaries incorporated in India shall undertake Secretarial Audit by a Secretarial Auditor who shall be a Peer Reviewed Company Secretary and shall annex a Secretarial Audit Report in such form as specified, with the annual report of the listed entity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Qualifications and disqualifications for secretarial auditors.
subpolicies.control: A person shall be eligible for appointment as a Secretarial Auditor of the listed entity only if such person is a Peer Reviewed Company Secretary and has not incurred any of the disqualifications as specified by the Board: Provided that a firm whereof majority of partners practising in India are qualified for appointment as aforesaid may be appointed by its firm name to be Secretarial Auditor of the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This subpolicy extracts the eligibility criteria for secretarial auditors from clause (1A)(a).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A person shall be eligible for appointment as a Secretarial Auditor of the listed entity only if such person is a Peer Reviewed Company Secretary and has not incurred any of the disqualifications as specified by the Board: Provided that a firm whereof majority of partners practising in India are qualified for appointment as aforesaid may be appointed by its firm name to be Secretarial Auditor of the listed entity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limits the types of services a secretarial auditor may provide.
subpolicies.control: A Secretarial Auditor appointed under these regulations shall provide to the listed entity only such other services as are approved by the board of directors, but which shall not include any services as specified by the Board in this behalf.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This subpolicy captures the restriction on additional services that a secretarial auditor may render, as per clause (1B).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A Secretarial Auditor appointed under these regulations shall provide to the listed entity only such other services as are approved by the board of directors, but which shall not include any services as specified by the Board in this behalf.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sub-regulation (1) and related clauses (1A)-(1C) define the secretarial audit obligations, auditor eligibility, and service limitations.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every listed entity and its material unlisted subsidiaries incorporated in India shall undertake Secretarial Audit by a Secretarial Auditor who shall be a Peer Reviewed Company Secretary and shall annex a Secretarial Audit Report in such form as specified, with the annual report of the listed entity.

policies.policy_description: Requirements governing the appointment, re‑appointment, continuation, and tenure of Secretarial Auditors for listed entities.
policies.scope: Listed entities from April 1, 2025
policies.objective: Ensure proper selection, tenure calculation, and reporting signatory for Secretarial Auditors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Listed entities must ensure compliance with sub‑regulations (1), (1A) and (1B) for appointment, re‑appointment or continuation of the Secretarial Auditor.
subpolicies.control: Every listed entity shall ensure compliance with sub‑regulation (1), (1A) and (1B) for appointment, re‑appointment or continuation of the Secretarial Auditor of the listed entity

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the core appointment compliance obligation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: effect from April 1, 2025, every listed entity shall ensure compliance with sub‑regulation (1), (1A) and (1B) for appointment, re‑appointment or continuation of the Secretarial Auditor of the listed entity

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Associations appointed before March 31, 2025 are excluded from tenure calculation.
subpolicies.control: Provided that any association of the individual or the firm as the Secretarial Auditor of the listed entity before March 31, 2025 shall not be considered for the purpose of calculating the tenure under clause (b) of sub‑regulation (1)

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the tenure exclusion provision.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Provided that any association of the individual or the firm as the Secretarial Auditor of the listed entity before March 31, 2025 shall not be considered for the purpose of calculating the tenure under clause (b) of sub-regulation (1)

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Annual secretarial compliance report must be signed by the Secretarial Auditor or a qualified Peer‑Reviewed Company Secretary.
subpolicies.control: the Secretarial Compliance Report submitted to the stock exchange(s) on annual basis is signed only by the Secretarial Auditor or by a Peer Reviewed Company Secretary who satisfies the conditions mentioned in sub‑regulations (1A) and (1B) of this regulation

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the signatory requirement for the compliance report.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the Secretarial Compliance Report submitted to the stock exchange(s) on annual basis is signed only by the Secretarial Auditor or by a Peer Reviewed Company Secretary who satisfies the conditions mentioned in sub‑regulations (1A) and (1B) of this regulation

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies obligations concerning Secretarial Auditor appointment, re‑appointment, continuation, tenure exclusion, and reporting signatory.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: effect from April 1, 2025, every listed entity shall ensure compliance with sub‑regulation (1), (1A) and (1B) for appointment, re‑appointment or continuation of the Secretarial Auditor of the listed entity

policies.policy_description: Obligations to prepare and submit secretarial compliance reports to stock exchanges within a specified timeframe.
policies.scope: Listed entities
policies.objective: Ensure timely and properly signed submission of annual secretarial compliance reports.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Submit secretarial compliance report within 60 days after financial year end.
subpolicies.control: Every listed entity shall submit a secretarial compliance report in such form as specified, to stock exchanges, within sixty days from end of each financial year

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the deadline for report submission.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every listed entity shall submit a secretarial compliance report in such form as specified, to stock exchanges, within sixty days from end of each financial year

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Report must be signed by Secretarial Auditor or qualified Peer‑Reviewed Company Secretary.
subpolicies.control: the Secretarial Compliance Report submitted to the stock exchange(s) on annual basis is signed only by the Secretarial Auditor or by a Peer Reviewed Company Secretary who satisfies the conditions mentioned in sub‑regulations (1A) and (1B) of this regulation

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the signatory condition for the compliance report.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the Secretarial Compliance Report submitted to the stock exchange(s) on annual basis is signed only by the Secretarial Auditor or by a Peer Reviewed Company Secretary who satisfies the conditions mentioned in sub‑regulations (1A) and (1B) of this regulation

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the requirement to submit a secretarial compliance report within 60 days after the financial year end and the signatory condition.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every listed entity shall submit a secretarial compliance report in such form as specified, to stock exchanges, within sixty days from end of each financial year

policies.policy_title: Independent Director Appointment, Tenure, and Shareholder Approval
policies.policy_description: Rules governing the appointment, tenure, and shareholder approval of independent directors, including restrictions on alternate directors and maximum tenure.
policies.scope: Listed entities
policies.objective: Regulate independent director appointments, tenure limits, and shareholder approval mechanisms.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 3.1 Prohibition of Alternate Director for Independent Director
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: No appointment or continuation as alternate director for an independent director.
subpolicies.control: No person shall be appointed or continue as an alternate director for an independent director of a listed entity with effect from October 1, 2018.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the prohibition on alternate directors for independent directors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: No person shall be appointed or continue as an alternate director for an independent director of a listed entity with effect from October 1, 2018.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Tenure must comply with Companies Act 2013.
subpolicies.control: The maximum tenure of independent directors shall be in accordance with the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder, in this regard, from time to time.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the tenure limitation reference.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The maximum tenure of independent directors shall be in accordance with the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder, in this regard, from time to time.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Appointment, re‑appointment, or removal of independent director requires special resolution.
subpolicies.control: The appointment, re‑appointment or removal of an independent director of a listed entity, shall be subject to the approval of shareholders by way of a special resolution.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the special resolution requirement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The appointment, re‑appointment or removal of an independent director of a listed entity, shall be subject to the approval of shareholders by way of a special resolution.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 3.4 Removal Conditions for Independent Director Appointed via Special Resolution
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Removal requires votes exceeding those against, including public shareholder votes.
subpolicies.control: Provided that where a special resolution for the appointment of an independent director fails to get the requisite majority of votes but the votes cast in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution and the votes cast by the public shareholders in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution, then the appointment of such an independent director shall be deemed to have been made under sub-regulation (2A): Provided further that an independent director appointed under the first proviso shall be removed only if the votes cast in favour of the resolution proposing the removal exceed the votes cast against the resolution and the votes cast by the public shareholders in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Describes the removal condition for independent directors appointed under the special resolution proviso.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Provided that where a special resolution for the appointment of an independent director fails to get the requisite majority of votes but the votes cast in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution and the votes cast by the public shareholders in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution, then the appointment of such an independent director shall be deemed to have been made under sub-regulation (2A): Provided further that an independent director appointed under the first proviso shall be removed only if the votes cast in favour of the resolution proposing the removal exceed the votes cast against the resolution and the votes cast by the public shareholders in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures provisions on prohibition of alternate director appointments, maximum tenure, and special resolution requirement for appointment/removal of independent directors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: No person shall be appointed or continue as an alternate director for an independent director of a listed entity with effect from October 1, 2018. The maximum tenure of independent directors shall be in accordance with the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder, in this regard, from time to time. The appointment, re‑appointment or removal of an independent director of a listed entity, shall be subject to the approval of shareholders by way of a special resolution.

policies.policy_title: Declaration of Independence and Board Recording
policies.policy_description: Procedures for independent directors to declare independence and for the board to record such declarations.
policies.scope: Independent directors and board of listed entities
policies.objective: Ensure independent directors disclose independence status and that the board acknowledges the declaration.

subpolicies.control: Every independent director shall, at the first meeting of the board in which he participates as a director and thereafter at the first meeting of the board in every financial year or whenever there is any change in the circumstances which may affect his status as an independent director, submit a declaration that he meets the criteria of independence as provided in clause (b) of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 16 and that he is not aware of any circumstance or situation, which exist or may be reasonably anticipated, that could impair or impact his ability to discharge his duties with an objective independent judgment and without any external influence.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Restrictions on entering agreements without board and shareholder approval, and voting abstention.
subpolicies.control: No employee including key managerial personnel or director or promoter of a listed entity shall enter into any agreement for himself or on behalf of any other person, with any shareholder or any other third party with regard to compensation or profit sharing in connection with dealings in the securities of such listed entity, unless prior approval for the same has been obtained from the Board of Directors as well as public shareholders by way of an ordinary resolution. policies.policy_title: Vacancy Filling for Key Managerial Personnel

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Maximum timeframes for filling key managerial vacancies.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Four criteria are used to determine materiality, including quantitative thresholds and board discretion.
subpolicies.control: (i) The listed entity shall consider the following criteria for determination of materiality of events/information: (a) omission likely to result in discontinuity or alteration of event or information already available publicly; or (b) omission likely to cause significant market reaction if the said omission came to light at a later date; (c) omission exceeding lower of (1) 2% turnover, (2) 2% net worth, (3) 5% average profit/loss; (d) board may deem material if not covered by (a)-(c).

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Summarizes the detailed materiality assessment criteria set out in sub-regulation (4).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (i) The listed entity shall consider the following criteria for determination of materiality of events/ information: (a)the omission of an event or information, which is likely to result in discontinuity or alteration of event or information already available publicly; or (b)the omission of an event or information is likely to result in significant market reaction if the said omission came to light at a later date; (c) the omission of an event or information, whose value or the expected impact in terms of value, exceeds the lower of the following: (1) two percent of turnover, ... (2) two percent of net worth, ... (3) five percent of the average of absolute value of profit or loss ...; (d) In case where the criteria specified in sub-clauses (a), (b) and (c) is not applicable, an event or information may be treated as being material if in the opinion of the board of directors of the listed entity, the event or information is considered material: ...

subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchange(s) a statement showing holding of securities and shareholding pattern separately for each class of securities, in the format specified by the Board from time to time within the following timelines - (a) one day prior to listing of its securities on the stock exchange(s); (b) on a quarterly basis, within twenty one days from the end of each quarter; and, (c) within ten days of any capital restructuring of the listed entity resulting in a change exceeding two per cent of the total paid-up share capital: Provided that in case of listed entities which have listed their specified securities on SME Exchange, the above statements shall be submitted on a half yearly basis within twenty one days from the end of each half year.

policies.policy_description: Regulations governing reclassification of promoters in listed entities under the Insolvency Code, including conditions and disclosures.
policies.scope: Applies to listed entities and their promoters undergoing reclassification.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Exemption condition for reclassification based on resolution plan or regulator order.
subpolicies.control: The provisions of sub-regulations (3), (4) and (8) of this regulation shall not apply if reclassification of promoter(s) is as per the resolution plan approved under section 31 of the Insolvency Code or pursuant to an order of a Regulator under any law subject to the condition that - (a) such promoter(s) shall not remain in control of the listed entity; (b) disclosure of the resolution plan or order of the Regulator within twenty- four hours along with an intimation that such promoter(s) would cease to be part of the promoter/promoter group of the listed entity.
