subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Explanation of completion of open offer and scheme of arrangement.
subpolicies.control: Explanation I: For the purpose of this sub-regulation, completion of open offer shall mean: (i) the date of actual transfer of shares from the promoter seeking reclassification to the new acquirer, or (ii) the date on which the new acquirer takes control of the listed entity, whichever is later. Explanation II: For the purpose of this sub-regulation, completion of scheme of arrangement shall mean the date on which shares are credited to all eligible shareholders of the listed entity or the transferee entity or the resulting entity in terms of the approved scheme.

policies.policy_description: Regulation specifying that special rights granted to shareholders must be approved by a special resolution in a general meeting every five years.
policies.scope: Applies to listed entities granting special rights to shareholders.
policies.objective: Ensure shareholder approval and periodic review of special rights.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandatory shareholder approval for special rights every five years.
subpolicies.control: Any special right granted to the shareholders of a listed entity shall be subject to the approval by the shareholders in a general meeting by way of a special resolution once in every five years starting from the date of grant of such special right: Provided that the special rights available to the shareholders of a listed entity as on the date of coming into force of this regulation shall be subject to the approval by shareholders by way of a special resolution within a period of five years from the date of coming into force of this regulation: Provided further that the requirement specified in this regulation shall not be applicable to the special rights made available by a listed entity to a financial institution registered with or regulated by the Reserve Bank of India under a lending arrangement in the normal course of business or to a debenture trustee registered with the Board under a subscription agreement for the debentures issued by the listed entity, if such financial institution or the debenture trustee becomes a shareholder of the listed entity as a consequence of such lending arrangement or subscription agreement for the debentures.

policies.policy_title: Quarterly Statement of Deviation(s) or Variation(s)
policies.policy_description: Requirement to submit quarterly statements on deviations and utilization of proceeds for issues, rights, preferential issues, etc.
policies.scope: Applies to listed entities conducting public, rights, preferential, or qualified institution placements.
policies.objective: Provide transparency on actual vs projected use of funds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Quarterly submission of deviation and utilization statements.
subpolicies.control: (1) The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchange the following statement(s) on a quarterly basis for public issue, rights issue, preferential issue etc., - (a) indicating deviations, if any, in the use of proceeds from the objects stated in the offer document or explanatory statement to the notice for the general meeting, as applicable; (b) indicating category wise variation (capital expenditure, sales and marketing, working capital etc.) between projected utilisation of funds made by it in its offer document or explanatory statement to the notice for the general meeting, as applicable and the actual utilisation of funds.

policies.policy_description: Procedures for board approval, certification, and signing of financial results before submission.
policies.scope: Listed entities required to obtain board approval and proper signing of financial results.
policies.objective: Ensure accountability and correct attribution of financial statements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Submission of quarterly and YTD standalone results within 45 days, with special provision for insolvency resolution plans.

policies.policy_description: Regulations governing filing, approval, and compliance for schemes of arrangement by listed entities.
policies.scope: Listed entities undertaking scheme of arrangement under Companies Act provisions
policies.objective: Standardize the process for obtaining stock exchange approval and court sanction for schemes of arrangement

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to file draft scheme with stock exchange for no‑objection letter before court filing.
subpolicies.control: Without prejudice to provisions of regulation 11, the listed entity desirous of undertaking a scheme of arrangement or involved in a scheme of arrangement, shall file the draft scheme of arrangement, proposed to be filed before any Court or Tribunal under sections 391-394 and 101 of the Companies Act, 1956 or under Sections 230- 234 and Section 66 of Companies Act, 2013, whichever applicable, along with a non-refundable fee as specified in Schedule XI, with the stock exchange(s) for obtaining the No-objection letter, before filing such scheme with any Court or Tribunal, in terms of requirements specified by the Board or stock exchange(s) from time to time.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the obligation to submit draft scheme to stock exchange to obtain no‑objection prior to court filing.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prohibition on filing scheme without prior no‑objection letter from stock exchange.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall not file any scheme of arrangement under sections 391-394 and 101 of the Companies Act, 1956 or under Sections 230- 234 and Section 66 of Companies Act, 2013, whichever applicable, with any Court or Tribunal unless it has obtained the No‑objection letter from the stock exchange(s).

policies.policy_description: Rules governing transfer, transmission, and transposition of securities, including procedural safeguards.
policies.scope: Listed entities handling securities transfers.
policies.objective: Ensure proper registration and processing of securities transfers while respecting statutory prohibitions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Transfer requests only if securities are dematerialised with a depository.
subpolicies.control: Save as otherwise specified in provisions of securities laws or Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder, the listed entity shall also comply with the requirements as specified in this regulation for effecting transfer of securities [:] Provided that requests for effecting transfer of securities shall not be processed unless the securities are held in the dematerialised form with a depository: Provided further that transmission or transposition of securities held in physical or dematerialised form shall be effected only in dematerialised form.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates dematerialised form and depository for processing transfer requests.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Do not register transfer if statutory prohibition or attachment order exists.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall not register transfer when any statutory prohibition or any attachment or prohibitory order of a competent authority restrains it from transferring the securities from the name of the transferor(s).

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Prohibits registration of transfers under statutory prohibitions.

subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall not register the transfer of its securities in the name of the transferee(s) when the transferor(s) objects to the transfer: Provided that the transferor serves on the listed entity, within sixty working days of raising the objection, a prohibitory order of a Court of competent jurisdiction.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 3. Prohibition on Issuing Shares with Superior/Inferior Rights
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Restriction on issuing shares that confer superior or inferior dividend or voting rights compared to existing listed equity shares.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall not issue shares in any manner that may confer on any person; superior or inferior rights as to dividend vis-à-vis the rights on equity shares that are already listed or inferior voting rights vis-à-vis the rights on equity shares that are already listed.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the rule preventing discriminatory rights in share issuance.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall not issue shares in any manner that may confer on any person; superior or inferior rights as to dividend vis-à-vis the rights on equity shares that are already listed or inferior voting rights vis-à-vis the rights on equity shares that are already listed.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 4. Pro Rata Offer to Existing Shareholders
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to offer shares first on a pro rata basis to existing equity shareholders unless the general meeting decides otherwise.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall, issue or offer in the first instance all shares (including forfeited shares), securities, rights, privileges and benefits to subscribe pro rata basis, to the equity shareholders of the listed entity, unless the shareholders in the general meeting decide otherwise.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the pro rata offering obligation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall, issue or offer in the first instance all shares (including forfeited shares), securities, rights, privileges and benefits to subscribe pro rata basis, to the equity shareholders of the listed entity, unless the shareholders in the general meeting decide otherwise.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 5. Redemption on Pro Rata or Lot Basis
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Restriction on selecting listed securities for redemption except on a pro rata or lot basis unless otherwise specified.
subpolicies.control: Unless the terms of issue otherwise provide, the listed entity shall not select any of its listed securities for redemption otherwise than on pro-rata basis or by lot.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the redemption rule to ensure fairness in selection.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Unless the terms of issue otherwise provide, the listed entity shall not select any of its listed securities for redemption otherwise than on pro-rata basis or by lot.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for notifying stock exchanges of record dates and associated notice periods for corporate actions.
policies.scope: Listed entities on stock exchanges
policies.objective: Ensure timely and accurate communication of record dates for dividend, share issues, corporate actions, and maintain adequate notice periods.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 1.1 Intimation of record date for corporate actions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to intimate record date to stock exchanges for specified corporate actions.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall intimate the record date to all the stock exchange(s) where it is listed for the following purposes: (a) declaration of dividend; (b) issue of right or bonus shares; (c) issue of shares for conversion of debentures or any other convertible security; (d) shares arising out of rights attached to debentures or any other convertible security; (e) corporate actions like mergers, de-mergers, splits, etc; (f) such other purposes as may be specified by the stock exchange(s).

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the purposes for which a record date must be intimated to stock exchanges.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall intimate the record date to all the stock exchange(s) where it is listed for the following purposes: (a) declaration of dividend; (b) issue of right or bonus shares; (c) issue of shares for conversion of debentures or any other convertible security; (d) shares arising out of rights attached to debentures or any other convertible security; (e) corporate actions like mergers, de-mergers, splits, etc; (f) such other purposes as may be specified by the stock exchange(s).

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sets the minimum notice period before the record date.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall give notice in advance of at least three working days (excluding the date of intimation and the record date) to stock exchange(s) of record date specifying the purpose of the record date. Provided that for corporate actions through schemes of arrangement covered under regulation 37, the listed entity shall give notice in advance of at least seven working days (excluding the date of intimation and the record date).

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets the minimum notice period before the record date.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall give notice in advance of atleast three working days (excluding the date of intimation and the record date) to stock exchange(s) of record date specifying the purpose of the record date: Provided that in the case of corporate actions through schemes of arrangement covered under regulation 37, the listed entity shall give notice in advance of atleast seven working days (excluding the date of intimation and the record date).

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires a minimum interval of five working days between successive record dates.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall ensure the time gap of at least five working days between two record dates.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires a minimum interval of five working days between successive record dates.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall ensure the time gap of at least five working days between two record dates.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the set of rules governing the intimation of record dates and notice periods to stock exchanges for various corporate actions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall intimate the record date to all the stock exchange(s) where it is listed for the following purposes: (a) declaration of dividend; (b) issue of right or bonus shares; (c) issue of shares for conversion of debentures or any other convertible security; (d) shares arising out of rights attached to debentures or any other convertible security; (e) corporate actions like mergers, de-mergers, splits, etc; (f) such other purposes as may be specified by the stock exchange(s).

policies.policy_description: Obligations related to declaring dividends per share and handling unclaimed dividends.
policies.scope: Listed entities
policies.objective: Ensure dividends are declared and disclosed properly and unclaimed dividends are not forfeited prematurely.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 2.1 Declare and disclose dividend per share
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandates per share basis declaration and disclosure of dividends.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall declare and disclose the dividend on per share basis only.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates per share basis declaration and disclosure of dividends.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall declare and disclose the dividend on per share basis only.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prevents premature forfeiture of unclaimed dividends and requires annulment if done.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall not forfeit unclaimed dividends before the claim becomes barred by law and such forfeiture, if effected, shall be annulled in appropriate cases.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Prevents premature forfeiture of unclaimed dividends and requires annulment if done.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall not forfeit unclaimed dividends before the claim becomes barred by law and such forfeiture, if effected, shall be annulled in appropriate cases.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Covers dividend declaration rules and protection of unclaimed dividend rights.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall declare and disclose the dividend on per share basis only. The listed entity shall not forfeit unclaimed dividends before the claim becomes barred by law and such forfeiture, if effected, shall be annulled in appropriate cases.

policies.policy_description: Mandates formulation and disclosure of dividend distribution policies by top listed entities, including required parameters and change disclosure.
policies.scope: Top 1000 listed entities by market capitalization (and optionally others voluntarily)
policies.objective: Standardize and ensure transparency of dividend distribution policies.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Obligates top entities to create and publicly disclose a dividend distribution policy.
subpolicies.control: The top 404[1000] listed entities based on market capitalization shall formulate a dividend distribution policy which shall be disclosed on the website of the listed entity and a web-link shall also be provided in their annual reports.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Obligates top entities to create and publicly disclose a dividend distribution policy.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The top 404[1000] listed entities based on market capitalization shall formulate a dividend distribution policy which shall be disclosed on the website of the listed entity and a web-link shall also be provided in their annual reports.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies the mandatory content elements of the dividend distribution policy.
subpolicies.control: The dividend distribution policy shall include the following parameters: (a) the circumstances under which the shareholders of the listed entities may or may not expect dividend; (b) the financial parameters that shall be considered while declaring dividend; (c) internal and external factors that shall be considered for declaration of dividend; (d) policy as to how the retained earnings shall be utilized; and (e) parameters that shall be adopted with regard to various classes of shares.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires disclosure of any modifications to dividend parameters and the rationale.
subpolicies.control: If the listed entity proposes to declare dividend on the basis of parameters in addition to clauses (a) to (e) or proposes to change such additional parameters or the dividend distribution policy contained in any of the parameters, it shall disclose such changes along with the rationale for the same in its annual report and on its website.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allows other listed entities to voluntarily disclose their dividend distribution policies.

policies.policy_description: Regulations governing remote e-voting, proxy forms, voting result submission, and meeting obligations for listed entities.
policies.scope: Listed entities
policies.objective: Facilitate shareholder participation, ensure transparency of voting outcomes, and comply with procedural requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandates offering remote e-voting for all shareholder resolutions.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall provide the facility of remote e-voting facility to its shareholders, in respect of all shareholders' resolutions.

policies.policy_title: Change of Name of Listed Entity
policies.policy_description: Regulations governing the alteration of a listed entity's name, including eligibility, filing, and shareholder approval requirements.
policies.scope: Listed entities on Indian stock exchanges
policies.objective: Ensure transparency and stakeholder consent for name changes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires a minimum one‑year gap since the last name change.
subpolicies.control: a time period of at least one year has elapsed from the last name change

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Condition (a) specifies the minimum interval before a name change can be effected.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: a time period of at least one year has elapsed from the last name change

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires that at least 50% of prior year revenue be derived from the new activity.
subpolicies.control: at least fifty percent. of the total revenue in the preceding one year period has been accounted for by the new activity suggested by the new name

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Condition (b) links name change to revenue contribution from the new activity.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: at least fifty percent. of the total revenue in the preceding one year period has been accounted for by the new activity suggested by the new name

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires investment in the new activity to be at least 50% of total assets.
subpolicies.control: the amount invested in the new activity/project is atleast fifty percent. of the assets of the listed entity

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Condition (c) sets an asset‑investment threshold for name change.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the amount invested in the new activity/project is atleast fifty percent. of the assets of the listed entity

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires renaming within six months when activities change but name does not reflect them.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for notifying stock exchanges about board meetings and related proposals.
policies.scope: Listed entities with non-convertible securities
policies.objective: Enable timely communication of material events to market participants.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 1. Prior Intimation of Board Meeting Proposals
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Must give at least two working days prior intimation about board meetings covering specific proposals.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall give prior intimation to the stock exchange of at least two working days in advance, excluding the date of the intimation and the date of the meeting of the board of directors, about the Board meeting in which any of the following proposals is to be considered: (a) an alteration in the form or nature of non-convertible securities that are listed on the stock exchange or in the rights or privileges of the holders thereof; (b) an alteration in the date of the interest/ dividend/ redemption payment of non-convertible securities; (c) financial results viz. quarterly or annual, as the case may be; (d) fund raising by way of issuance of non-convertible securities; or (e) any matter affecting the rights or interests of holders of non-convertible securities.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the detailed prior intimation requirement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall give prior intimation to the stock exchange of at least two working days in advance, excluding the date of the intimation and the date of the meeting of the board of directors, about the Board meeting in which any of the following proposals is to be considered: (a) an alteration in the form or nature of non-convertible securities that are listed on the stock exchange or in the rights or privileges of the holders thereof; (b) an alteration in the date of the interest/ dividend/ redemption payment of non-convertible securities; (c) financial results viz. quarterly or annual, as the case may be; (d) fund raising by way of issuance of non-convertible securities; or (e) any matter affecting the rights or interests of holders of non-convertible securities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Must intimate stock exchange not later than commencement of dispatch of notices for certain meetings.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall also intimate the stock exchange not later than the date of commencement of dispatch of notices, in case of: (a) any annual general meeting or extraordinary general meeting that is proposed to be held for obtaining shareholder approval for the proposals at clauses (c) and (d) under sub-regulation (1) of this regulation; (b) any meeting of the holders of non-convertible securities in relation to the proposal at clause (e) of sub-regulation (1) of this regulation.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the requirement for intimation at notice dispatch.

policies.policy_description: Applicability and definitions for entities that have listed non-convertible securities.
policies.scope: Listed entities with non-convertible securities
policies.objective: Define the scope and interpretation of obligations for such entities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Chapter applies only to listed entities that have listed non-convertible securities as per SEBI regulations.
subpolicies.control: The provisions of this chapter shall apply only to a listed entity which has listed its non-convertible securities on a recognised stock exchange in accordance with Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Non-Convertible Securities) Regulations, 2021.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the applicability condition.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The provisions of this chapter shall apply only to a listed entity which has listed its non-convertible securities on a recognised stock exchange in accordance with Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue and Listing of Non-Convertible Securities) Regulations, 2021.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Interest references are read as dividend for certain listed securities.
subpolicies.control: For the purpose of this chapter, if the listed entity has listed its non-convertible redeemable preference shares, perpetual non-cumulative preference shares or instruments of nature similar to perpetual non-cumulative preference shares, the reference to “interest” in this chapter shall be read as “dividend”.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the definition adjustment.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Default is defined as non-payment of interest/dividend or principal on the due date.
subpolicies.control: For the purpose of this chapter, “default” shall mean non-payment of interest/dividend or principal amount in full on the pre-agreed date and shall be recognized at the first instance of delay in servicing of any interest/dividend or principal amount.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the definition of default.

policies.policy_title: Board Meeting Intimation for Securities Issuance
policies.policy_description: Requires listed entities to notify stock exchanges about board meetings where proposals to issue non‑convertible securities are considered.
policies.scope: Listed entities issuing non‑convertible debt securities or redeemable preference shares.
policies.objective: Ensure timely market awareness of proposed capital‑raising actions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Notice must be given at least two working days before the meeting.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall intimate to the stock exchange(s), at least two working days in advance, excluding the date of the intimation and date of the meeting, regarding the meeting of its board of directors, at which the recommendation or declaration of issue of non convertible debt securities or any other matter affecting the rights or interests of holders of non convertible debt securities or non convertible redeemable preference shares is proposed to be considered.

policies.policy_title: Prompt Disclosure of Price Sensitive Information
policies.policy_description: Obligates listed entities to promptly inform stock exchanges of all price‑sensitive information affecting the entity.
policies.scope: Listed entities.
policies.objective: Enable market participants to receive material information without undue delay.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure must occur within 24 hours of event or receipt of information.
subpolicies.control: The expression ‘promptly inform’, shall imply that the stock exchange shall be informed as soon as reasonably possible but not later than twenty‑four hours from the date of occurrence of the event or receipt of information.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the maximum allowable delay of 24 hours for disclosure.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Explanation - The expression ‘promptly inform’, shall imply that the stock exchange shall be informed as soon as reasonably possible but not later than twenty‑four hours from the date of occurrence of the event or receipt of information.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Delayed disclosures require a justification.
subpolicies.control: In case the disclosure is made after twenty‑four hours of the date of occurrence of the event or receipt of information, the listed entity shall, along with such disclosures provide an explanation for the delay.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires justification for delayed disclosures.

policies.policy_description: Mandates disclosure of events affecting non‑convertible securities and requires hosting on the entity’s website for a minimum period.
policies.scope: Listed entities with listed non‑convertible securities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All disclosed events must be published on the entity's website.

policies.policy_title: Financial Results Preparation, Approval, and Publication
policies.policy_description: Sets out timing, format, approval, signing, and reporting requirements for quarterly and annual financial results.
policies.scope: Listed entities preparing and submitting financial results.
policies.objective: Standardize the presentation and timely release of financial information.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prepare and submit unaudited or audited quarterly financial results within 45 days from quarter‑end.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall prepare and submit un‑audited or audited quarterly and year to date standalone financial results on a quarterly basis in the format as specified by the Board within forty‑five days from the end of the quarter, other than last quarter, to the recognised stock exchange(s).

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies timing and format for quarterly financial results.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall prepare and submit un‑audited or audited quarterly and year to date standalone financial results on a quarterly basis in the format as specified by the Board within forty‑five days from the end of the quarter, other than last quarter, to the recognised stock exchange(s).

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Annual audited results must be submitted in the same format as quarterly results.
subpolicies.control: The audited results for the year shall be submitted to the recognised stock exchange(s) in the same format as is applicable for quarterly financial results.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires annual audited results to follow the same format as quarterly results.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The audited results for the year shall be submitted to the recognised stock exchange(s) in the same format as is applicable for quarterly financial results.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Quarterly unaudited results must be accompanied by a limited review report.
subpolicies.control: Un‑audited financial results on quarterly basis shall be accompanied by limited review report prepared by the statutory auditors of the listed entity, in the format as specified by the Board: Provided that in case of issuers whose accounts are audited by the Comptroller and Auditor General of India, the report shall be provided by any practising Chartered Accountant.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates inclusion of limited review report with unaudited results.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Quarterly results must be approved by the board of directors.
subpolicies.control: The quarterly financial results submitted shall be approved by the board of directors.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires board approval before submission.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The quarterly financial results submitted shall be approved by the board of directors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Results must be signed by authorized director.
subpolicies.control: The financial results submitted to the stock exchange shall be signed by the chairperson or managing director, or a whole time director or in the absence of all of them, it shall be signed by any other director of the listed entity who is duly authorized by the board of directors to sign the financial results.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the signatory for submitted results.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All disclosed events must be hosted on the website for at least five years.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall disclose on its website, all such events or information which have been disclosed to the stock exchange(s) under this regulation and such disclosures shall be hosted on the website of the listed entity for a minimum period of five years and thereafter as per the archival policy of the listed entity, as disclosed on its website.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Ensures continuity of disclosed information on the entity's website.

policies.policy_title: Financial Results Disclosure – Quarterly and Annual

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Submission of assets, liabilities and cash flow statements each half‑year
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall submit a statement of assets and liabilities and statement of cash flows as at the end of every half year, by way of a note, along with the financial results.

policies.policy_description: Comprehensive set of mandatory disclosures and reporting obligations for listed entities under the Insolvency Code, covering financial ratios, preference share disclosures, issue proceeds utilisation, and annual report contents.
policies.scope: Listed entities
policies.objective: Ensure transparent and complete financial reporting and stakeholder information.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to submit quarterly utilisation of issue proceeds statement.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchange(s), along with the quarterly financial results, a statement indicating the utilisation of issue proceeds of non‑convertible securities, in such format as may be specified by the Board, till such proceeds of issue have been fully utilised or the purpose for which the proceeds were raised has been achieved.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the obligation to submit a statement on utilisation of issue proceeds with quarterly results.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (7) The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchange(s), along with the quarterly financial results, a statement indicating the utilisation of issue proceeds of non‑convertible securities, in such format as may be specified by the Board, till such proceeds of issue have been fully utilised or the purpose for which the proceeds were raised has been achieved.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to disclose material deviation in use of issue proceeds.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchange(s), along with the quarterly financial results, a statement disclosing material deviation(s) (if any) in the use of issue proceeds of non‑convertible securities from the objects of the issue, in such format as may be specified by the Board, till such proceeds have been fully utilised or the purpose for which the proceeds were raised has been achieved.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the obligation to disclose material deviations in the use of issue proceeds.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (7A) The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchange(s), along with the quarterly financial results, a statement disclosing material deviation(s) (if any) in the use of issue proceeds of non‑convertible securities from the objects of the issue, in such format as may be specified by the Board, till such proceeds have been fully utilised or the purpose for which the proceeds were raised has been achieved.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to publish financial results and line items within two working days of board meeting conclusion.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall, within two working days of the conclusion of the meeting of the board of directors, publish the financial results and the line items referred to in sub‑regulation (4), in at least one English national daily newspaper circulating in the whole or substantially the whole of India.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the publication timing and newspaper requirement for financial results.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (8) The listed entity shall, within two 479[working] days of the conclusion of the meeting of the board of directors, publish the financial results and 480[the line items]referred to in sub‑ 476 Omitted by the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) (Fifth Amendment) Regulations, 2021 w.e.f. 7.9.2021. Before omission, clause (a) read as follows: “(a)profit for the half year and cumulative profit for the year;”

policies.policy_description: Requirement to submit the annual report and related notices to stock exchange and debenture trustee for timely shareholder communication.
policies.scope: Listed entities on Indian stock exchanges
policies.objective: Ensure timely and accurate disclosure of annual reports to shareholders and regulators

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Submit copy of annual report with AGM notice to stock exchange and debenture trustee before dispatch to shareholders.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchange and the debenture trustee and publish on its website a copy of the annual report sent to the shareholders along with the notice of the annual general meeting, not later than the date of commencement of dispatch to its shareholders.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the specific submission requirement for the annual report.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 487[(2) ... (a) a copy of the annual report sent to the shareholders along with the notice of the annual general meeting, not later than the date of commencement of dispatch to its shareholders;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the regulation mandating submission of annual report to stock exchange and debenture trustee.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 487[(2) The listed entity shall submit to the stock exchange and the debenture trustee and publish on its website- (a) a copy of the annual report sent to the shareholders along with the notice of the annual general meeting, not later than the date of commencement of dispatch to its shareholders;

policies.policy_description: Obligation to maintain and disclose adequate security cover for listed non‑convertible debt securities.
policies.scope: Listed entities issuing secured non‑convertible debt securities
policies.objective: Ensure continuous coverage sufficient to discharge principal and interest of debt securities

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Maintain 100% security cover or higher as per offer document, sufficient to discharge principal and interest.
subpolicies.control: In respect of its 490[secured] listed non‑convertible debt securities, the listed entity shall maintain hundred per cent. 491[security cover or higher security cover] as per the terms of offer document/Information Memorandum and/or Debenture Trust Deed, sufficient to discharge the principal amount 492[and the interest thereon] at all times for the non‑convertible debt securities issued.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the mandatory security cover maintenance requirement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 489[(1) ... shall maintain hundred per cent. 491[security cover or higher security cover] ... sufficient to discharge the principal amount ... at all times ...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclose security cover in financial results in the format specified by the Board.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall disclose the 495[security cover] available in case of non‑convertible debt securities along with its financial results in the format as specified by the Board.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires disclosure of security cover in financial statements as per Board format.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 494[(3) The listed entity shall disclose the 495[security cover] available in case of non‑convertible debt securities along with its financial results in the format as specified by the Board.]

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Covers regulations specifying maintenance and disclosure of security cover for listed non‑convertible debt securities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 489[(1) In respect of its 490[secured] listed non‑convertible debt securities, the listed entity shall maintain hundred per cent. 491[security cover or higher security cover] as per the terms of offer document/Information Memorandum and/or Debenture Trust Deed, sufficient to discharge the principal amount 492[and the interest thereon] at all times for the non‑convertible debt securities issued.]

policies.policy_description: Requirement to annually review credit ratings of listed non‑convertible securities by a registered rating agency.
policies.scope: Listed entities with non‑convertible debt securities
policies.objective: Maintain ongoing assessment of creditworthiness through periodic rating reviews

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Annual review of each credit rating by a registered rating agency.
subpolicies.control: Each rating obtained by the listed entity with respect to non‑convertible 496[***] securities shall be reviewed at least once a year by a credit rating agency registered by the Board.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the annual rating review obligation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 55. Each rating obtained by the listed entity with respect to non‑convertible 496[***] securities shall be reviewed at least once a year by a credit rating agency registered by the Board.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the regulation mandating annual review of credit ratings by a Board‑registered agency.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 55. Each rating obtained by the listed entity with respect to non‑convertible 496[***] securities shall be reviewed at least once a year by a credit rating agency registered by the Board.

policies.policy_title: Documentation and Intimation to Debenture Trustees
policies.policy_description: Obligation to forward various documents, notices, and material events to the debenture trustee and stock exchange.
policies.scope: Listed entities issuing non‑convertible debt securities
policies.objective: Enable the debenture trustee to monitor compliance and material developments affecting debt securities

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Forward annual report and auditor's certificate on fund utilization to debenture trustee.
subpolicies.control: a copy of the annual report at the same time as it is issued along with a copy of certificate from the listed entity's auditors in respect of utilisation of funds during the implementation period of the project for which the funds have been raised:

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the first category of documents to be forwarded.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 56. (1) ... (a) a copy of the annual report at the same time as it is issued along with a copy of certificate from the listed entity's auditors in respect of utilisation of funds ...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Forward copies of all notices, resolutions, and circulars relating to new issues and meetings.
subpolicies.control: a copy of all notices, resolutions and circulars relating to- (i) new issue of non convertible debt securities at the same time as they are sent to shareholders/ holders of non convertible debt securities; (ii) the meetings of holders of non-convertible debt securities at the same time as they are sent to the holders of non convertible debt securities or advertised in the media including those relating to proceedings of the meetings;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Covers dissemination of issue‑related notices and meeting information.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 56. (1) ... (b) a copy of all notices, resolutions and circulars relating to- (i) new issue of non convertible debt securities ... (ii) the meetings of holders ...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provide intimations of rating revisions, defaults, failure to create charge, and covenants.
subpolicies.control: intimations regarding : (i) any revision in the rating; (ii) any default in timely payment of interest or redemption or both in respect of the non convertible debt securities; (iii) failure to create charge on the assets; 497[(iv) all covenants of the issue (including side letters, accelerated payment clause, etc.)]

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Lists the material events that must be intimated to the debenture trustee.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 56. (1) ... (c) intimations regarding : (i) any revision in the rating; (ii) any default ... (iii) failure to create charge on the assets; 497[(iv) all covenants of the issue ...]

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclose material events to debenture trustee simultaneously with stock exchange disclosure.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall also disclose to the Debenture Trustee at the same time as it has intimated to the stock exchange, all material events and/or information as disclosed under regulation 51of these regulations in so far as it relates to the interest, principal, issue and terms of non‑convertible debt securities, rating, creation of charge on the assets, notices, resolutions and meetings of holders of non‑convertible debt securities.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires simultaneous disclosure of material events to both stock exchange and debenture trustee.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 502[(1A) The listed entity shall also disclose to the Debenture Trustee at the same time as it has intimated to the stock exchange, all material events and/or information as disclosed under regulation 51of these regulations ...]

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provide requested information and access to books of accounts to the debenture trustee.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall forward to the debenture trustee any such information sought and provide access to relevant books of accounts as required by the debenture trustee.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates cooperation with the debenture trustee by supplying requested data and account access.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 56. (2) The listed entity shall forward to the debenture trustee any such information sought and provide access to relevant books of accounts as required by the debenture trustee.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Encompasses the series of requirements for forwarding documents and information to debenture trustees as detailed in regulation 56.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 56. (1) The listed entity shall forward the following to the debenture trustee promptly: (a) a copy of the annual report ... (b) a copy of all notices, resolutions and circulars relating to ... (c) intimations regarding ... (iv) all covenants of the issue ... 502[(1A) The listed entity shall also disclose to the Debenture Trustee ... all material events ...]

policies.policy_description: Regulatory obligations related to insolvency code disclosures and reporting for listed entities.
policies.scope: Listed entities issuing non-convertible securities and related obligations.
policies.objective: Ensure timely and accurate disclosure of financial and structural information.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allows electronic/fax submission of required information to stock exchanges with debenture trustee consent.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity may, subject to the consent of the debenture trustee, send the information stipulated in sub-regulation (1), in electronic form/fax.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: This clause defines the method of submission for required information.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity may, subject to the consent of the debenture trustee, send the information stipulated in sub-regulation (1), in electronic form/fax.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires certificate to stock exchange on status of payment of interest/dividend/principal.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall submit a certificate to the stock exchange regarding status of payment of interest or dividend or repayment or redemption of principal of non-convertible securities, within one working day of it becoming due, in the manner and format as specified by the Board from time to time.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates timely certificate submission to stock exchange.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall submit a certificate to the stock exchange regarding status of payment of interest or dividend or repayment or redemption of principal of non-convertible securities, within one working day of it becoming due, in the manner and format as specified by the Board from time to time.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies documents to be sent to holders of non-convertible securities.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall send the following documents: (a) Soft copies of the full annual reports to all the holders of non-convertible securities who have registered their email address(es) either with the listed entity or with any depository; (b) Hard copy of statement containing the salient features of all the documents, as specified in Section 136 of Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder to those holders of non convertible securities who have not so registered; (c) Hard copies of full annual reports to those holders of non convertible securities who request for the same.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Enumerates the types of documents that must be provided to security holders.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall send the following documents: (a) Soft copies of the full annual reports to all the holders of non-convertible securities who have registered their email address(es) either with the listed entity or with any depository; (b) Hard copy of statement containing the salient features of all the documents, as specified in Section 136 of Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder to those holders of non convertible securities who have not so registered; (c) Hard copies of full annual reports to those holders of non convertible securities who request for the same.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allows material modification of non-convertible debt securities structure with stock exchange approval.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall not make material modification without prior approval of the stock exchange(s) where the non convertible debt securities or non-convertible redeemable preference shares, as applicable, are listed, to: (a) the structure of the non-convertible debt securities debenture in terms of coupon, redemption, or otherwise.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets approval requirement for structural changes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Transfer unclaimed amounts to escrow account
subpolicies.control: Where the interest/dividend/redemption amount has not been claimed within thirty days from the due date, a listed entity shall within seven days from the date of expiry of the said period of thirty days, transfer the amount to an escrow account to be opened by the listed entity in any scheduled bank: Provided that the interest/ dividend/ redemption amount that is unclaimed and outstanding for a period of less than seven years as on the date of notification of this sub-regulation shall be transferred to the escrow account within thirty days, where it shall remain for the intervening period up to seven years.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board composition requirements: optimum mix, at least one woman director, at least 50% non‑executive directors, independent director thresholds.
subpolicies.control: (a) board of directors shall have an optimum combination of executive and non‑executive directors with at least one woman director and not less than fifty per cent. of the board of directors shall comprise of non‑executive directors; (b) where the chairperson of the board of directors is a non‑executive director, at least one‑third of the board of directors shall comprise of independent directors and where the listed entity does not have a regular non‑executive chairperson, at least half of the board of directors shall comprise of independent directors: Provided that where the non‑executive chairperson is a promoter of the listed entity or is related to any promoter or person occupying management positions at the level of board of director or at one level below the board of directors, at least half of the board of directors of the listed entity shall consist of independent directors. Explanation: - For the purpose of this clause, the expression “related to any promoter” shall have the following meaning: (i) if the promoter is a listed entity, its directors other than the independent directors, its employees or its nominees shall be deemed to be related to it; (ii) if the promoter is an unlisted entity, its directors, its employees or its nominees shall be deemed to be related to it.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Age limit for non‑executive directors and special resolution requirement.
subpolicies.control: No HVDLE shall appoint a person or continue the directorship of any person as a non‑executive director who has attained the age of seventy five years unless a special resolution is passed to that effect, in which case the explanatory statement annexed to the notice for such motion shall indicate the justification for appointing such a person.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the age restriction and special resolution condition.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: No HVDLE shall appoint a person or continue the directorship of any person as a non‑executive director who has attained the age of seventy five years unless a special resolution is passed to that effect, in which case the explanatory statement annexed to the notice for such motion shall indicate the justification for appointing such a person.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Shareholder approval for appointment/reappointment of directors and managers, with timelines and prior approval for previously rejected candidates.
subpolicies.control: The HVDLE shall ensure that approval of shareholders for appointment or re‑appointment of a person on the board of directors or as a manager is taken at the next general meeting or within a time period of three months from the date of appointment, whichever is earlier: Provided that a public sector company shall ensure that the approval of the shareholders for appointment or re‑appointment of a person on the board of directors or as a Manager is taken at the next general meeting: Provided further that the appointment or a re‑appointment of a person, including as a managing director or a whole‑time director or a manager, who was earlier rejected by the shareholders at a general meeting, shall be done only with the prior approval of the shareholders: Provided further that the statement referred to under sub‑section (1) of section 102 of the Companies Act, 2013, annexed to the notice to the shareholders, for considering the appointment or re‑appointment of such a person earlier rejected by the shareholders shall contain a detailed explanation and justification by the nomination and remuneration committee (if constituted) and the board of directors for recommending such a person for appointment or re‑appointment.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Describes shareholder approval process and conditions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The HVDLE shall ensure that approval of shareholders for appointment or re‑appointment of a person on the board of directors or as a manager is taken at the next general meeting or within a time period of three months from the date of appointment, whichever is earlier...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Director continuation approval every five years, with special provision for HVDLE as of March 31, 2025, and exceptions for certain director types.
subpolicies.control: The continuation of a director serving on the board of directors of a listed entity shall be subject to the approval of the shareholders in a general meeting at least once in every five years from the date of their appointment or reappointment, as the case may be: Provided that the continuation of the director serving on the board of directors of a HVDLE as on March 31, 2025, without the approval of the shareholders for the last five years or more shall be subject to the approval of shareholders in the first general meeting to be held after March 31, 2025: Provided further that the requirement specified in this regulation shall not be applicable to the Whole‑Time Director, Managing Director, Manager, Independent Director or a Director retiring as per the sub‑section (6) of section 111 152 of the Companies Act, 2013, if the approval of the shareholders for the reappointment or continuation of the aforesaid directors or Manager

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Outlines the five‑year re‑approval requirement and exemptions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The continuation of a director serving on the board of directors of a listed entity shall be subject to the approval of the shareholders in a general meeting at least once in every five years from the date of their appointment or reappointment, as the case may be...

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the comprehensive governance framework for HVDLEs as described in the provided text.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Applicability. 62C. (1) The provisions of this chapter shall apply to a listed entity which only has non‑convertible debt securities listed, with an outstanding value of Rupees One Thousand Crore and above and does not have any listed specified securities.

policies.policy_description: Defines the required composition of the board, including categories of directors and independence criteria.
policies.scope: Board of Directors of HVDLE
policies.objective: Ensure appropriate board makeup and independence

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board must include whole-time directors, managers or employees of the entity or related entities.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors of every listed entity shall consist of a combination of directors who are whole-time directors, managers or employees of such entity or its related entities, and such other directors as may be prescribed.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the composition requirement for directors as stated in clause (1).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The board of directors of every listed entity shall consist of a combination of directors who are whole-time directors, managers or employees of such entity or its related entities, and such other directors as may be prescribed.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to appoint at least one independent director.
subpolicies.control: Every listed entity shall appoint at least one director who is or was a whole-time director or manager or employee of such entity or its related entities and who is independent, as per the definition in these regulations.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the independent director appointment mandate from clause (2).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every listed entity shall appoint at least one director who is or was a whole-time director or manager or employee of such entity or its related entities and who is independent, as per the definition in these regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Government-appointed directors are excluded from composition rules.
subpolicies.control: The requirement specified in this regulation shall not be applicable to the director appointed pursuant to the order of a Court or a Tribunal or a resolution plan approved under section 31 of the Insolvency Code or to a nominee director of the Government on the board of a listed entity...

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the exclusion for government-appointed directors as per clause (3).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The requirement specified in this regulation shall not be applicable to the director appointed pursuant to the order of a Court or a Tribunal or a resolution plan approved under section 31 of the Insolvency Code or to a nominee director of the Government on the board of a listed entity...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Financial institution nominated directors are excluded from composition rules.
subpolicies.control: The requirement specified in this regulation shall not be applicable to a director nominated by a financial institution registered with or regulated by the Reserve Bank of India under a lending arrangement in its normal course of business or nominated by a debenture trustee...

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the exclusion for financial institution nominated directors as per clause (4).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The requirement specified in this regulation shall not be applicable to a director nominated by a financial institution registered with or regulated by the Reserve Bank of India under a lending arrangement in its normal course of business or nominated by a debenture trustee...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Vacancy in director office must be filled within three months.
subpolicies.control: Any vacancy in the office of a director shall be filled by the listed entity at the earliest and in any case not later than three months from the date of such vacancy: Provided that if the HVDLE becomes non-compliant with the requirement under sub-regulation (1) of this regulation, due to expiration of the term of office of any director, the resulting vacancy shall be filled by the listed entity not later than the date of such office is vacated: Provided further that this sub-regulation shall not apply if the HVDLE fulfils the requirement under sub-regulation (1) of this regulation without filling the vacancy.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the vacancy filling timeline from clause (5).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Any vacancy in the office of a director shall be filled by the listed entity at the earliest and in any case not later than three months from the date of such vacancy...

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the policy that sets out board composition requirements, including whole-time directors, independent directors, and exclusions for government and financial institution nominees.

policies.policy_description: Sets out frequency of board meetings and quorum requirements.
policies.scope: Board of Directors of HVDLE
policies.objective: Ensure regular board oversight and adequate participation

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board must meet at least four times a year with max 120 days between meetings.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors shall meet at least four times a year, with a maximum time gap of one hundred and twenty days between any two meetings.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the meeting frequency requirement from clause (6).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The board of directors shall meet at least four times a year, with a maximum time gap of one hundred and twenty days between any two meetings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Quorum is one-third of total strength or three directors, whichever is higher, including at least one independent director; video participation counts.
subpolicies.control: The quorum for every meeting of the board of directors shall be one-third of its total strength or three directors, whichever is higher, including at least one independent director. Explanation:- For removal of doubts, it is clarified that the participation of the directors by video conferencing or by other audio-visual means shall also be considered for the purposes of such quorum.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the quorum definition and inclusion of video participation from clause (7).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The quorum for every meeting of the board of directors shall be one-third of its total strength or three directors, whichever is higher, including at least one independent director. Explanation:- For removal of doubts, it is clarified that the participation of the directors by video conferencing or by other audio-visual means shall also be considered for the purposes of such quorum.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the policy governing meeting frequency and quorum rules for board sessions.

policies.policy_description: Outlines board duties regarding compliance review, succession, code of conduct, risk management, and information placement.
policies.scope: Board of Directors of HVDLE
policies.objective: Ensure systematic oversight, compliance, and governance practices

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board must periodically review compliance reports and remediation steps.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors shall periodically review compliance reports pertaining to all laws applicable to the HVDLE, prepared by the HVDLE as well as steps taken by the HVDLE to rectify instances of non-compliances.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the compliance review duty from clause (8).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The board of directors shall periodically review compliance reports pertaining to all laws applicable to the HVDLE, prepared by the HVDLE as well as steps taken by the HVDLE to rectify instances of non-compliances.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Succession planning for board and senior management must be in place.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors of the HVDLE shall satisfy itself that plans are in place for orderly succession by appointment to the board of directors and senior management.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the succession planning requirement from clause (9).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The board of directors of the HVDLE shall satisfy itself that plans are in place for orderly succession by appointment to the board of directors and senior management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board must adopt a code of conduct incorporating independent director duties.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors shall lay down a code of conduct suitably incorporating the duties of independent directors as laid down in the Companies Act, 2013 for all members of board of directors and senior management of the HVDLE.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the code of conduct requirement from clause (10).

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Information from Part A of Schedule II must be placed before the board.

ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The information specified in Part A of Schedule II shall be placed before the board of directors.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the compliance certificate provision from clause (14).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The chief executive officer and the chief financial officer of the HVDLE shall provide the compliance certificate to the board of directors as specified in Part B of Schedule II.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Procedures for risk assessment and management must be established.
subpolicies.control: The HVDLE shall lay down procedures to inform the board of directors about risk assessment and minimization procedures. The board of directors shall be responsible for framing, implementing and monitoring the risk management plan for the HVDLE.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the risk management procedure requirement from clause (15).

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board must evaluate independent directors on performance and independence.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors shall evaluate independent directors on the following parameters: (a) performance of the directors; and (b) fulfillment of the independence criteria as specified in these regulations and their independence from the management: Provided that in the above evaluation, the directors who are subject to evaluation shall not participate.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the independent director evaluation requirement from clause (16).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The board of directors shall evaluate independent directors on the following parameters: (a) performance of the directors; and (b) fulfillment of the independence criteria as specified in these regulations and their independence from the management: Provided that in the above evaluation, the directors who are subject to evaluation shall not participate.

policies.policy_description: Regulates fees and compensation for non-executive and executive directors, including shareholder approval requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board recommends fees for non-executive directors and requires shareholder approval.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Shareholder approval must specify limits for stock options to non-executive directors.
subpolicies.control: The approval of shareholders mentioned in clause (a) of this sub-regulation shall specify the limits for the maximum number of stock options that may be granted to non-executive directors, in any financial year and in aggregate.

subpolicies.control: The approval of shareholders by special resolution shall be obtained every year, in which the annual remuneration payable to a single non-executive director exceeds fifty per cent. of the total annual remuneration payable to all non-executive directors, giving details of the remuneration thereof.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the annual approval condition from clause (11)(d).

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Independent directors are not entitled to stock options.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Executive director remuneration subject to shareholder special resolution if thresholds exceeded.
