ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (3) The chairperson of the risk management committee shall be a member of the board of directors and senior executives of the HVDLE may be members of the committee.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Meet at least twice a year; max 210 days between meetings.
subpolicies.control: The risk management committee shall meet at least twice in a year and not more than two hundred and ten days shall elapse between any two consecutive meetings.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets the minimum and maximum interval between meetings.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (4) The risk management committee shall meet at least twice in a year and not more than two hundred and ten days shall elapse between any two consecutive meetings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Quorum is either two members or one third, whichever is higher, including at least one board member.
subpolicies.control: Quorum shall be either two members or one third of the members of the committee, whichever is higher, including at least one member of the board of directors in attendance.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the quorum requirement including board member presence.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (5) The quorum for a meeting of the risk management committee shall be either two members or one third of the members of the committee, whichever is higher, including at least one member of the board of directors in attendance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: May seek information, obtain external advice, and secure attendance of outsiders as needed.
subpolicies.control: The risk management committee shall have powers to seek information from any employee, obtain outside legal or other professional advice and secure attendance of outsiders with relevant expertise, if it considers necessary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Lists the investigative and informational powers of the committee.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (7) The risk management committee shall have powers to seek information from any employee, obtain outside legal or other professional advice and secure attendance of outsiders with relevant expertise, if it considers necessary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from sections 62I which detail the Risk Management Committee's structure and responsibilities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (1) The board of directors shall ensure that the functions of the Risk Management Committee as specified in Part D of the Schedule II are discharged by audit committee or by the board of directors or a risk management committee constituted by the board of directors as per this regulation.

policies.policy_description: Policy establishing a whistle‑blower mechanism for directors and employees, with safeguards against victimization and access to the Audit Committee chair.
policies.scope: Directors and employees of listed entities (HVDLE)
policies.objective: Enable reporting of genuine concerns and protect reporters from retaliation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Formulate a vigil mechanism/whistleblower policy for reporting concerns.
subpolicies.control: The HVDLE shall formulate a vigil mechanism/ whistle blower policy for directors and employees to report genuine concerns.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the requirement to create the vigil mechanism.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (1) The HVDLE shall formulate a vigil mechanism/ whistle blower policy for directors and employees to report genuine concerns.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provide safeguards against victimization of those using the mechanism.
subpolicies.control: The vigil mechanism shall provide for adequate safeguards against victimization of director(s) or employee(s) or any other person who avail the mechanism.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies protection against retaliation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (2) The vigil mechanism shall provide for adequate safeguards against victimization of director(s) or employee(s) or any other person who avail the mechanism and also provide for direct access to the chairperson of the audit committee in appropriate or exceptional cases.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allow direct access to the chairperson of the audit committee in appropriate cases.
subpolicies.control: Provide for direct access to the chairperson of the audit committee in appropriate or exceptional cases.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Details the access right to the audit committee chair.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (2) ... also provide for direct access to the chairperson of the audit committee in appropriate or exceptional cases.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from section 62J which sets out the vigil mechanism requirements.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (1) The HVDLE shall formulate a vigil mechanism/ whistle blower policy for directors and employees to report genuine concerns.

policies.policy_description: Policy defining materiality thresholds and review requirements for related party transactions.
policies.scope: Listed entities (HVDLE) and its board
policies.objective: Ensure transparency and fairness in related party dealings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Transaction is material if it exceeds ₹1,000 crore or 10% of annual consolidated turnover, whichever is lower.
subpolicies.control: A transaction with a related party shall be considered material if it exceeds Rupees one thousand crore or ten per cent. of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity, whichever is lower.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the general materiality threshold for related party transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (1) ... a transaction with a related party shall be considered material, if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceeds Rupees one thousand crore or ten per cent. of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity, whichever is lower.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Transaction is material if it exceeds 5% of annual consolidated turnover.
subpolicies.control: A transaction involving payments to a related party for brand usage or royalty shall be considered material if it exceeds five per cent. of the annual consolidated turnover.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies a lower threshold for certain related party payments.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (2) Notwithstanding the above, a transaction involving payments made to a related party with respect to brand usage or royalty shall be considered material if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceed five per cent. of the annual consolidated turnover of the HVDLE as per the last audited financial statements of the HVDLE.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Policy must be reviewed by the board at least once every three years and updated accordingly.
subpolicies.control: The policy shall be reviewed by the board of directors at least once every three years and updated accordingly.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets the review frequency and requirement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (1) ... such policy shall be reviewed by the board of directors at least once every three years and updated accordingly.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from section 62K which outlines materiality criteria, thresholds, and review frequency for related party transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (1) The HVDLE shall formulate a policy on materiality of related party transactions and on dealing with related party transactions including clear threshold limits duly approved by the board of directors and such policy shall be reviewed by the board of directors at least once every three years and updated accordingly: Provided that a transaction with a related party shall be considered material, if the transaction(s) to be entered into individually or taken together with previous transactions during a financial year, exceeds Rupees one thousand crore or ten per cent. of the annual consolidated turnover of the listed entity as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity, whichever is lower.

policies.policy_title: Related Party Transaction Approval and Disclosure
policies.policy_description: Governance requirements for related party transactions of a HVDLE, including approval thresholds, omnibus approvals, debenture trustee and shareholder approvals, exemptions, and disclosures.
policies.scope: Applies to all related party transactions of a listed holding company (HVDLE) and its subsidiaries.
policies.objective: Ensure proper oversight, materiality assessment, stakeholder approval, and transparent disclosure of related party transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The audit committee must define 'material modifications' and disclose them as part of the materiality policy.
subpolicies.control: The audit committee of a HVDLE shall define “material modifications” and disclose it as part of the policy on materiality of related party transactions and on dealing with related party transactions.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the requirement to define and disclose material modifications.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the audit committee of a HVDLE shall define “material modifications” and disclose it as part of the policy on materiality of related party transactions

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Related party transactions involving a subsidiary that exceed 10% of the listed entity's consolidated turnover require audit committee approval.
subpolicies.control: A related party transaction to which the subsidiary of a listed entity is a party but the listed entity is not a party, shall require prior approval of the audit committee of the HVDLE if the value ... exceeds ten per cent. of the annual consolidated turnover, as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets a materiality threshold based on consolidated turnover for approval.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: exceeds ten per cent. of the annual consolidated turnover, as per the last audited financial statements of the listed entity

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Related party transactions involving a subsidiary that exceed 10% of the subsidiary's standalone turnover require audit committee approval.
subpolicies.control: A related party transaction to which the subsidiary of a HVDLE is a party but the HVDLE is not a party, shall require prior approval of the audit committee of the HVDLE if the value ... exceeds ten per cent. of the annual standalone turnover, as per the last audited financial statements of the subsidiary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets a materiality threshold based on the subsidiary's standalone turnover.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: exceeds ten per cent. of the annual standalone turnover, as per the last audited financial statements of the subsidiary

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 3(d) Exemption when Regulation 23 and Sub‑regulation (2) of Regulation 15 Apply
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: No audit committee approval needed if the transaction falls under applicable regulations.
subpolicies.control: prior approval of the audit committee of the HVDLE shall not be required for a related party transaction ... if regulation 23 and sub‑regulation (2) of regulation 15 of these regulations are applicable to such listed subsidiary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Provides an exemption from approval when specific regulations apply.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: prior approval of the audit committee of the HVDLE shall not be required ... if regulation 23 and sub‑regulation (2) of regulation 15 of these regulations are applicable

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Audit committee must lay down criteria for granting omnibus approval in line with the policy.
subpolicies.control: the audit committee shall lay down the criteria for granting the omnibus approval in line with the policy on related party transactions of the HVDLE and such approval shall be applicable in respect of transactions which are repetitive in nature.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires the audit committee to define criteria for omnibus approval.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the audit committee shall lay down the criteria for granting the omnibus approval in line with the policy on related party transactions of the HVDLE

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Audit committee must satisfy itself that omnibus approval is in the interest of the listed entity.
subpolicies.control: the audit committee shall satisfy itself regarding the need for such omnibus approval and that such approval is in the interest of the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Ensures the approval serves the listed entity's interest.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the audit committee shall satisfy itself regarding the need for such omnibus approval and that such approval is in the interest of the listed entity

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Approval must specify related party name, transaction nature, period, max amount, price details, and other conditions.
subpolicies.control: the omnibus approval shall specify: (i) the name(s) of the related party, nature of transaction, period of transaction, maximum amount of transactions that shall be entered into; (ii) the indicative base price / current contracted price and the formula for variation in the price if any; and (iii) such other conditions as the audit committee may deem fit.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates detailed specification of approved transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the omnibus approval shall specify: (i) the name(s) of the related party, nature of transaction, period of transaction, maximum amount of transactions that shall be entered into; (ii) the indicative base price / current contracted price and the formula for variation in the price if any; and (iii) such other conditions as the audit committee may deem fit

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Includes requirement for price variation formula.
subpolicies.control: the indicative base price / current contracted price and the formula for variation in the price if any

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the need for a price variation formula.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the indicative base price / current contracted price and the formula for variation in the price if any

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Audit committee may impose any other conditions it deems fit.
subpolicies.control: such other conditions as the audit committee may deem fit

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Allows audit committee to add further conditions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: such other conditions as the audit committee may deem fit

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Omnibus approval may be granted for transactions where details are not foreseen, provided value ≤ Rs 1 crore per transaction.
subpolicies.control: where the need for related party transaction cannot be foreseen and aforesaid details are not available, audit committee may grant omnibus approval for such transactions subject to their value not exceeding Rupees one crore per transaction.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Creates a fallback approval for unforeseen transactions with a monetary cap.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: where the need for related party transaction cannot be foreseen and aforesaid details are not available, audit committee may grant omnibus approval for such transactions subject to their value not exceeding Rupees one crore per transaction

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Audit committee must review details of related party transactions at least quarterly.
subpolicies.control: the audit committee shall review, at least on a quarterly basis, the details of related party transactions entered into by the HVDLE pursuant to each of the omnibus approvals given.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires periodic review of omnibus approvals.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the audit committee shall review, at least on a quarterly basis, the details of related party transactions entered into by the HVDLE pursuant to each of the omnibus approvals given

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Omnibus approvals are valid for a maximum of one year.
subpolicies.control: Such omnibus approvals shall be valid for a period not exceeding one year.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Limits the duration of omnibus approvals.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Such omnibus approvals shall be valid for a period not exceeding one year.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Material related party transactions require prior No‑Objection Certificate from the Debenture Trustee.
subpolicies.control: All material related party transactions and subsequent material modifications as defined by the audit committee under sub‑regulation (3) shall require prior No‑Objection Certificate from the Debenture Trustee

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates Debenture Trustee approval for material transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: All material related party transactions and subsequent material modifications as defined by the audit committee under sub-regulation (3) shall require prior No‑Objection Certificate from the Debenture Trustee

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Debenture Trustee must obtain No‑Objection from debenture holders representing >50% in value.
subpolicies.control: the Debenture Trustee shall in turn obtain No‑Objection from the debenture holders who are not related with the Issuer and hold at least more than fifty per cent. of the debentures in value, on the basis of voting including e‑voting.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires debenture holders' approval representing majority value.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the Debenture Trustee shall in turn obtain No‑Objection from the debenture holders who are not related with the Issuer and hold at least more than fifty per cent. of the debentures in value, on the basis of voting including e‑voting

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: After Debenture Holder approval, shareholder resolution must be obtained.
subpolicies.control: After obtaining approval of the debenture holders, approval of the shareholders through resolution shall be obtained.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Ensures final shareholder approval for material related party transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: After obtaining approval of the debenture holders, approval of the shareholders through resolution shall be obtained.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If No‑Objection is withheld, the matter cannot be taken forward to shareholders.
subpolicies.control: If the No‑Objection Certificate has been withheld, the matter shall not be taken forward for shareholders’ consideration.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clarifies the consequence of a withheld No‑Objection.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: If the No‑Objection Certificate has been withheld, the matter shall not be taken forward for shareholders’ consideration.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: No‑Objection required for listed debt securities issued on or after April 1, 2025, with exceptions for existing securities.
subpolicies.control: This No‑Objection Certificate from Debenture Trustee and debenture holders shall be obtained in respect of listed debt securities issued on or after April 01, 2025: Provided that in case of outstanding listed debt securities as on March 31, 2025, No‑Objection Certificate ... shall not be required for existing or prospective material related party transactions.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the temporal scope and exceptions for No‑Objection requirement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: This No‑Objection Certificate from Debenture Trustee and debenture holders shall be obtained in respect of listed debt securities issued on or after April 01, 2025

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: For unlisted subsidiaries, shareholder approval and Debenture Trustee letter are required, except for resolution plans under Insolvency Code.
subpolicies.control: For related party transactions of unlisted subsidiaries of a listed subsidiary as referred above, the prior approval of the shareholders and No‑ objection Letter from Debenture Trustee of the listed subsidiary ... shall be obtained: Provided that the requirements specified under this sub‑regulation shall not apply in respect of a resolution plan approved under section 31 of the Insolvency Code, subject to the event being disclosed to the recognized stock exchanges within one day of the resolution plan being approved.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets approval path for unlisted subsidiary transactions and carves out an insolvency exception.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the prior approval of the shareholders and No‑ objection Letter from Debenture Trustee of the listed subsidiary ... shall be obtained: Provided that the requirements specified under this sub‑regulation shall not apply in respect of a resolution plan approved under section 31 of the Insolvency Code

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Transactions between two government companies are exempt from these provisions.
subpolicies.control: transactions entered into between two government companies

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Provides an exemption for government‑government transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: transactions entered into between two government companies

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Transactions between a holding company and its wholly‑owned subsidiary that is consolidated and presented to shareholders are exempt.
subpolicies.control: transactions entered into between a holding company and its wholly owned subsidiary whose accounts are consolidated with such holding company and placed before the shareholders at the general meeting for approval

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Exempts certain consolidated subsidiary transactions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: transactions entered into between a holding company and its wholly owned subsidiary whose accounts are consolidated with such holding company and placed before the shareholders at the general meeting for approval

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Transactions between two wholly‑owned subsidiaries of the listed holding company, consolidated and presented to shareholders, are exempt.
subpolicies.control: transactions entered into between two wholly‑owned subsidiaries of the listed holding company, whose accounts are consolidated with such holding company and placed before the shareholders at the general meeting for approval

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Exempts intra‑group transactions of wholly‑owned subsidiaries.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: transactions entered into between two wholly‑owned subsidiaries of the listed holding company, whose accounts are consolidated with such holding company and placed before the shareholders at the general meeting for approval

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All provisions apply to transactions entered into on or after April 1, 2025.
subpolicies.control: The provisions of this regulation shall be applicable to all transactions entered into on or after April 01, 2025.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets the effective date for the regulation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The provisions of this regulation shall be applicable to all transactions entered into on or after April 01, 2025.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 9(1) Independent Director on Unlisted Material Subsidiary Board
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: At least one independent director of the HVDLE must also serve on the board of an unlisted material subsidiary.
subpolicies.control: At least one independent director, on the board of directors of the HVDLE, shall be a director on the board of directors of an unlisted material subsidiary, whether incorporated in India or not.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Ensures board‑level oversight of unlisted material subsidiaries.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: At least one independent director, on the board of directors of the HVDLE, shall be a director on the board of directors of an unlisted material subsidiary

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 9(2) Audit Committee Review of Subsidiary Financials
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Audit committee must review the financial statements and investments of the unlisted material subsidiary.
subpolicies.control: The audit committee of the HVDLE shall also review the financial statements and in particular, the investments made by the unlisted material subsidiary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates audit committee oversight of subsidiary finances.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The audit committee of the HVDLE shall also review the financial statements and in particular, the investments made by the unlisted material subsidiary.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minutes of the subsidiary's board meetings must be presented to the HVDLE board.
subpolicies.control: The minutes of the meetings of the board of directors of the unlisted material subsidiary shall be placed at the meeting of the board of directors of the HVDLE.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires transparency of subsidiary board minutes to the HVDLE board.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The minutes of the meetings of the board of directors of the unlisted material subsidiary shall be placed at the meeting of the board of directors of the HVDLE.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Management must regularly inform the HVDLE board of all significant transactions by the subsidiary.
subpolicies.control: The management of the unlisted material subsidiary shall periodically bring to the notice of the board of directors of the HVDLE, a statement of all significant transactions and arrangements entered into by the unlisted material subsidiary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Ensures ongoing monitoring of material subsidiary activities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The management of the unlisted material subsidiary shall periodically bring to the notice of the board of directors of the HVDLE, a statement of all significant transactions and arrangements entered into by the unlisted material subsidiary.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the comprehensive set of rules governing related party transactions, approvals, omnibus mechanisms, and disclosures for HVDLEs.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: modifications shall require prior approval of the audit committee of the HVDLE: Provided that only those members of the audit committee, who are independent directors, shall approve related party transactions: ... (full excerpt omitted)

policies.policy_title: Secretarial Audit and Secretarial Compliance Report
policies.policy_description: Requirements for secretarial audit and compliance reporting for HVDLEs and their material unlisted subsidiaries.
policies.scope: HVDLEs and their material unlisted subsidiaries incorporated in India
policies.objective: Ensure timely secretarial audit and compliance reporting to stock exchanges

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandate to conduct secretarial audit and attach audit report to annual report.
subpolicies.control: Every HVDLE and its material unlisted subsidiaries incorporated in India shall undertake secretarial audit and shall annex a secretarial audit report given by a company secretary in practice, in such form as specified by the Board, with the annual report of the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the specific audit obligation for HVDLEs and subsidiaries.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every HVDLE and its material unlisted subsidiaries incorporated in India shall undertake secretarial audit and shall annex a secretarial audit report ... with the annual report of the listed entity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to submit secretarial compliance report within 60 days after financial year end.
subpolicies.control: Every HVDLE shall submit a secretarial compliance report in such form as specified by the Board, to stock exchanges, within sixty days from end of each financial year.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the timing and reporting obligation for secretarial compliance.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every HVDLE shall submit a secretarial compliance report ... within sixty days from end of each financial year.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the regulatory obligations for secretarial audit and compliance reporting outlined in clause 62M.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Secretarial Audit and Secretarial Compliance Report. 62M. (1) Every HVDLE and its material unlisted subsidiaries incorporated in India shall undertake secretarial audit and shall annex a secretarial audit report ... (2) Every HVDLE shall submit a secretarial compliance report ... within sixty days from end of each financial year.

policies.policy_title: Obligations with respect to Independent Directors
policies.policy_description: Regulatory framework governing appointment, tenure, removal, meetings, and liabilities of independent directors of HVDLEs.
policies.scope: Independent directors of HVDLEs
policies.objective: Ensure independence, tenure, accountability, and proper governance of independent directors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prohibition on appointing alternate directors for independent directors.
subpolicies.control: No person shall be appointed as an alternate director for an independent director of a HVDLE.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the rule disallowing alternate directors for independent directors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: No person shall be appointed as an alternate director for an independent director of a HVDLE.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Maximum tenure of independent directors must comply with Companies Act 2013.
subpolicies.control: The maximum tenure of independent directors shall be in accordance with the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies tenure limits for independent directors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The maximum tenure of independent directors shall be in accordance with the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Shareholder special resolution required for appointment, re‑appointment or removal of independent directors with specific voting conditions.
subpolicies.control: The appointment, re-appointment or removal of an independent director of a HVDLE, shall be subject to the approval of shareholders by way of a special resolution: Provided that where a special resolution for the appointment of an independent director fails to get the requisite majority of votes but the votes cast in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution and the votes cast by the public shareholders in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution, then the appointment of such an independent director shall be deemed to have been made under sub-regulation (3): Provided further that an independent director appointed under the first proviso shall be removed only if the votes cast in favour of the resolution proposing the removal exceed the votes cast against the resolution and the votes cast by the public shareholders in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the special resolution and voting conditions for independent director appointments and removals.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The appointment, re-appointment or removal of an independent director of a HVDLE, shall be subject to the approval of shareholders by way of a special resolution: ... Provided that where a special resolution for the appointment of an independent director fails to get the requisite majority of votes but the votes cast in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution and the votes cast by the public shareholders in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution, then the appointment of such an independent director shall be deemed to have been made under sub-regulation (3): ... Provided further that an independent director appointed under the first proviso shall be removed only if the votes cast in favour of the resolution proposing the removal exceed the votes cast against the resolution and the votes cast by the public shareholders in favour of the resolution exceed the votes cast against the resolution.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Independent directors must hold at least one meeting per financial year without non‑independent directors or management.
subpolicies.control: The independent directors of the HVDLE shall hold at least one meeting in a financial year, without the presence of non‑independent directors and members of the management and all the independent directors shall strive to be present at such meeting.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the mandatory meeting of independent directors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The independent directors of the HVDLE shall hold at least one meeting in a financial year, without the presence of non‑independent directors and members of the management and all the independent directors shall strive to be present at such meeting.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Matters to be reviewed by independent directors in their meeting.
subpolicies.control: The independent directors in the meeting referred in sub-regulation (4) shall, inter alia- 121 a) review the performance of non‑independent directors and the board of directors as a whole; b) review the performance of the chairperson of the HVDLE, taking into account the views of executive directors and non‑executive directors; c) assess the quality, quantity and timeliness of flow of information between the management of the HVDLE and the board of directors that is necessary for the board of directors to effectively and reasonably perform their duties.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Lists the review responsibilities of independent directors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The independent directors in the meeting referred in sub-regulation (4) shall, inter alia- 121 a) review the performance of non‑independent directors and the board of directors as a whole; b) review the performance of the chairperson of the HVDLE, taking into account the views of executive directors and non‑executive directors; c) assess the quality, quantity and timeliness of flow of information between the management of the HVDLE and the board of directors that is necessary for the board of directors to effectively and reasonably perform their duties.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Liability of independent directors limited to acts with knowledge, consent, or connivance.
subpolicies.control: An independent director shall be held liable, only in respect of such acts of omission or commission by the HVDLE which had occurred with his/her knowledge, attributable through processes of board of directors, and with his/ her consent or connivance or where he/ she had not acted diligently with respect to the provisions contained in these regulations.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the limited liability of independent directors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: An independent director shall be held liable, only in respect of such acts of omission or commission by the HVDLE which had occurred with his/her knowledge, attributable through processes of board of directors, and with his/ her consent or connivance or where he/ she had not acted diligently with respect to the provisions contained in these regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Replacement of resigned or removed independent director within three months, unless board already meets independent director requirement.
subpolicies.control: An independent director who resigns or is removed from the board of directors of the HVDLE shall be replaced by a new independent director by the HVDLE not later than three months from the date of such vacancy: Provided that where the HVDLE fulfils the requirement of independent directors in its board of directors without filling the vacancy created by such resignation or removal, the requirement of replacement by a new independent director shall not apply.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets replacement timeline and exception for independent director vacancies.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: An independent director who resigns or is removed from the board of directors of the HVDLE shall be replaced by a new independent director by the HVDLE not later than three months from the date of such vacancy: Provided that where the HVDLE fulfils the requirement of independent directors in its board of directors without filling the vacancy created by such resignation or removal, the requirement of replacement by a new independent director shall not apply.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Induction programmes for independent directors on industry, business model, roles, etc.
subpolicies.control: The HVDLE shall undertake Directors and Officers insurance D and O insurance for all its independent directors for such sum assured and for such risks as may be determined by its board of directors

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the requirement to familiarise independent directors through various programmes.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The HVDLE shall undertake Directors and Officers insurance D and O insurance for all its independent directors for such sum assured and for such risks as may be determined by its board of directors

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Declaration by independent director on independence criteria and lack of impairing circumstances.
subpolicies.control: Every independent director shall, at the first meeting of the board in which he participates as a director and thereafter at the first meeting of the board in every financial year or whenever there is any change in the circumstances which may affect his status as an independent director, submit a declaration that he meets the criteria of independence as provided in clause (b) of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 62D and that he is not aware of any circumstance or situation, which exist or may be reasonably anticipated, that could impair or impact his ability to discharge his duties with an objective independent judgment and without any external influence.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Describes the mandatory declaration by independent directors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every independent director shall, at the first meeting of the board in which he participates as a director and thereafter at the first meeting of the board in every financial year or whenever there is any change in the circumstances which may affect his status as an independent director, submit a declaration that he meets the criteria of independence ... and that he is not aware of any circumstance or situation, which exist or may be reasonably anticipated, that could impair or impact his ability to discharge his duties with an objective independent judgment and without any external influence.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board must record the declaration and confirmation submitted by independent director.
subpolicies.control: The board of directors of the HVDLE shall take on record the declaration and confirmation submitted by the independent director under sub-regulation (9) after undertaking due assessment of the veracity of the same.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies that the board records the declaration after assessment.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The board of directors of the HVDLE shall take on record the declaration and confirmation submitted by the independent director under sub-regulation (9) after undertaking due assessment of the veracity of the same.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: D and O insurance for independent directors.
subpolicies.control: The HVDLE shall undertake Directors and Officers insurance D and O insurance for all its independent directors for such sum assured and for such risks as may be determined by its board of directors.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the insurance obligation for independent directors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The HVDLE shall undertake Directors and Officers insurance D and O insurance for all its independent directors for such sum assured and for such risks as may be determined by its board of directors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Restriction on appointing resigned independent director to executive roles within one year.
subpolicies.control: No independent director, who resigns from a HVDLE, shall be appointed as an executive / whole time director on the board of the HVDLE, its holding, subsidiary or associate company or on the board of a company belonging to its promoter group, unless a period of one year has elapsed from the date of resignation as an independent director.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets the cooling‑off period before a resigned independent director can take an executive role.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: No independent director, who resigns from a HVDLE, shall be appointed as an executive / whole time director on the board of the HVDLE, its holding, subsidiary or associate company or on the board of a company belonging to its promoter group, unless a period of one year has elapsed from the date of resignation as an independent director.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Covers all provisions related to independent directors found in clause 62N.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Obligations with respect to independent directors. 62N. (1) No person shall be appointed as an alternate director for an independent director of a HVDLE. (2) The maximum tenure of independent directors shall be in accordance with the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder. (3) The appointment, re-appointment or removal of an independent director of a HVDLE, shall be subject to the approval of shareholders by way of a special resolution: ... (12) No independent director, who resigns from a HVDLE, shall be appointed as an executive / whole time director ... unless a period of one year has elapsed ...

policies.policy_title: Obligations with respect to Employees, Senior Management, Directors and Promoters
policies.policy_description: Controls concerning committee membership limits, disclosures, code of conduct, and D and O insurance for directors and senior management.
policies.scope: Directors, senior management, employees, and promoters of HVDLEs
policies.objective: Ensure compliance with committee limits, disclosures, code of conduct, and insurance requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Committee membership limits for directors.
subpolicies.control: A director shall not be a member in more than ten committees or act as chairperson of more than five committees across all listed entities in which he/she is a director which shall be determined as follows: a) for calculating the limit of the committees on which a director may serve, all public limited companies, whether listed or not, and HVDLEs, shall be included and all other companies including private limited companies, foreign companies and companies under Section 8 of the Companies Act, 2013 shall be excluded; b) for the purpose of determination of limit, chairpersonship and membership of the audit committee and the stakeholders' relationship committee alone shall be considered.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the limit on committee membership and chairperson roles for directors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A director shall not be a member in more than ten committees or act as chairperson of more than five committees across all listed entities in which he/she is a director which shall be determined as follows: a) for calculating the limit of the committees on which a director may serve, all public limited companies, whether listed or not, and HVDLEs, shall be included and all other companies including private limited companies, foreign companies and companies under Section 8 of the Companies Act, 2013 shall be excluded; b) for the purpose of determination of limit, chairpersonship and membership of the audit committee and the stakeholders' relationship committee alone shall be considered.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Notification of committee positions and changes.
subpolicies.control: Every director shall inform the HVDLE about the positions he or she occupies in committees of other listed entities/HVDLEs and notify changes as and when they take place.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires directors to disclose committee positions and any changes.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every director shall inform the HVDLE about the positions he or she occupies in committees of other listed entities/HVDLEs and notify changes as and when they take place.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Annual affirmation of code of conduct compliance.
subpolicies.control: All members of the board of directors and senior management personnel shall affirm compliance with the code of conduct of board of directors and senior management on an annual basis.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates annual compliance affirmation by board members and senior management.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: All members of the board of directors and senior management personnel shall affirm compliance with the code of conduct of board of directors and senior management on an annual basis.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Covers the provisions in clause 62O regarding committee limits, notifications, code of conduct affirmation, and related matters.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A director shall not be a member in more than ten committees or act as chairperson of more than five committees across all listed entities in which he/she is a director which shall be determined as follows: a) for calculating the limit of the committees on which a director may serve, all public limited companies, whether listed or not, and HVDLEs, shall be included and all other companies including private limited companies, foreign companies and companies under Section 8 of the Companies Act, 2013 shall be excluded; b) for the purpose of determination of limit, chairpersonship and membership of the audit committee and the stakeholders' relationship committee alone shall be considered. (2) Every director shall inform the HVDLE about the positions he or she occupies in committees of other listed entities/HVDLEs and notify changes as and when they take place. (3) All members of the board of directors and senior management personnel shall affirm compliance with the code of conduct of board of directors and senior management on an annual basis.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for senior management to disclose material transactions and manage conflicts of interest, including approval and disclosure of agreements.
policies.scope: Senior management and key personnel of HVDLEs
policies.objective: Prevent conflicts of interest and ensure transparent disclosure of material transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandates disclosure of material transactions by senior management to the board.
subpolicies.control: Senior management shall make disclosures to the board of directors relating to all material, financial and commercial transactions, where they have personal interest that may have a potential conflict with the interest of the HVDLE at large.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the explicit disclosure obligation for senior management.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Senior management shall make disclosures to the board of directors relating to all material, financial and commercial transactions, where they have personal interest that may have a potential conflict with the interest of the HVDLE at large.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 2. Conflict of Interest and Agreement Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires prior board and shareholder approval for compensation or profit sharing agreements involving employees, directors, or promoters.
subpolicies.control: No employee including key managerial personnel or director or promoter of a HVDLE shall enter into any agreement for himself/herself or on behalf of any other person, with any shareholder or any other third party with regard to compensation or profit sharing in connection with dealings in the securities of such HVDLE, unless prior approval for the same has been obtained from the board of directors as well as public shareholders by way of an ordinary resolution.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets out the approval and disclosure conditions for agreements that could affect compensation or profit sharing.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: No employee including key managerial personnel or director or promoter of a HVDLE shall enter into any agreement for himself/herself or on behalf of any other person, with any shareholder or any other third party with regard to compensation or profit sharing in connection with dealings in the securities of such HVDLE, unless prior approval for the same has been obtained from the board of directors as well as public shareholders by way of an ordinary resolution.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires disclosure of agreements to stock exchanges and abstention from voting by interested persons.
subpolicies.control: Such agreement, if any, whether subsisting or expired, entered during the preceding three years from the date of coming into force of this sub-regulation, shall be disclosed to the stock exchanges for public dissemination: Provided that subsisting agreement, if any, as on the date of coming into force of this sub-regulation shall be placed for approval before the board of directors in the forthcoming Board meeting: Provided further that if the board of directors approve such agreement, the same shall be placed before the public shareholders for approval by way of an ordinary resolution in the forthcoming general meeting: Provided further that all interested persons involved in the transaction covered under the agreement shall abstain from voting in the general meeting.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the procedural steps for disclosure and voting abstention for related agreements.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Such agreement, if any, whether subsisting or expired, entered during the preceding three years from the date of coming into force of this sub-regulation, shall be disclosed to the stock exchanges for public dissemination...

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the policy governing disclosures, conflict of interest, and agreement approvals for senior management.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Senior management shall make disclosures to the board of directors relating to all material, financial and commercial transactions, where they have personal interest that may have a potential conflict with the interest of the HVDLE at large.

policies.policy_description: Procedures for filling vacancies in key managerial positions within the timeframes and conditions specified.
policies.scope: Vacancies in CEO, Managing Director, Whole Time Director, Manager, and CFO
policies.objective: Ensure timely and compliant filling of critical leadership positions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Vacancies must be filled within three months, or six months if regulatory approval is required.
subpolicies.control: Any vacancy in the office of Chief Executive Officer, Managing Director, Whole Time Director or Manager shall be filled by the HVDLE at the earliest and in any case not later than three months from the date of such vacancy: Provided that where the HVDLE is required to obtain approval of regulatory, government or statutory authorities to fill up such vacancies, then the vacancies shall be filled up by the HVDLE at the earliest and in any case not later than six months from the date of vacancy.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the mandatory timelines for filling senior executive vacancies.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Any vacancy in the office of Chief Executive Officer, Managing Director, Whole Time Director or Manager shall be filled by the HVDLE at the earliest and in any case not later than three months from the date of such vacancy: Provided that where the HVDLE is required to obtain approval of regulatory, government or statutory authorities to fill up such vacancies, then the vacancies shall be filled up by the HVDLE at the earliest and in any case not later than six months from the date of vacancy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Similar timeline for Chief Financial Officer vacancies.
subpolicies.control: Any vacancy in the office of the Chief Financial Officer shall be filled by the HVDLE at the earliest and in any case not later than three months from the date of such vacancy: Provided that where the HVDLE is required to obtain approval of regulatory, government or statutory authorities to fill up such vacancy, then the vacancy shall be filled up by the HVDLE at the earliest and in any case not later than six months from the date of vacancy.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets the same timeline for CFO vacancies, with extended period if approvals needed.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Any vacancy in the office of the Chief Financial Officer shall be filled by the HVDLE at the earliest and in any case not later than three months from the date of such vacancy: Provided that where the HVDLE is required to obtain approval of regulatory, government or statutory authorities to fill up such vacancy, then the vacancy shall be filled up by the HVDLE at the earliest and in any case not later than six months from the date of vacancy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Interim appointments are only allowed under specific legal conditions.
subpolicies.control: The HVDLE shall not fill such vacancy by appointing a person in interim capacity, unless such appointment is made in accordance with the laws applicable in case of a fresh appointment to such office and the obligations under such laws are made applicable to such person.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Restricts interim appointments to those complying with applicable laws.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The HVDLE shall not fill such vacancy by appointing a person in interim capacity, unless such appointment is made in accordance with the laws applicable in case of a fresh appointment to such office and the obligations under such laws are made applicable to such person.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Covers the rules for vacating and appointing key managerial personnel, including timelines and interim appointment restrictions.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Any vacancy in the office of Chief Executive Officer, Managing Director, Whole Time Director or Manager shall be filled by the HVDLE at the earliest and in any case not later than three months from the date of such vacancy...

policies.policy_title: Periodic Compliance and Corporate Governance Reporting
policies.policy_description: Obligations to submit compliance reports, disclose related party transactions, cyber security incidents, and sustainability reports.
policies.scope: Listed entities (HVDLEs) required to submit periodic reports to stock exchanges
policies.objective: Ensure ongoing transparency and accountability through regular disclosures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Submit compliance report within 21 days after period end, signed by compliance officer or CEO.
subpolicies.control: The HVDLE shall submit a periodic compliance report on corporate governance in the format as specified by the Board from time to time to the recognized stock exchange(s) within twenty one days from the end of the period. The report mentioned in clause (a) of sub-regulation (2) shall be signed either by the compliance officer or the chief executive officer of the HVDLE.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets the reporting frequency, format, and signing authority.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The HVDLE shall submit a periodic compliance report on corporate governance in the format as specified by the Board from time to time to the recognized stock exchange(s) within twenty one days from the end of the period. The report mentioned in clause (a) of sub-regulation (2) shall be signed either by the compliance officer or the chief executive officer of the HVDLE.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclose all material related party transactions in the compliance report.
subpolicies.control: Details of all material transactions with related parties shall be disclosed along with the report mentioned in clause (a) of this sub-regulation.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates disclosure of material related party transactions alongside the compliance report.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Details of all material transactions with related parties shall be disclosed along with the report mentioned in clause (a) of this sub-regulation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Report cyber security incidents or data loss in the compliance report.
subpolicies.control: Details of cyber security incidents or breaches or loss of data or documents shall be disclosed along with the report mentioned in clause (a) of this sub-regulation, as may be specified.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires disclosure of cyber incidents as part of the compliance report.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Details of cyber security incidents or breaches or loss of data or documents shall be disclosed along with the report mentioned in clause (a) of this sub-regulation, as may be specified.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Include ESG disclosures in the annual report as specified.
subpolicies.control: HVDLEs may provide in the annual report, a Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report on the environmental, social and governance disclosures as specified in clause (f) of the sub-regulation (2) of the regulation 34, in the format as may be specified by the Board from time to time.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Allows inclusion of ESG reporting in the annual report.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: HVDLEs may provide in the annual report, a Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report on the environmental, social and governance disclosures as specified in clause (f) of the sub-regulation (2) of the regulation 34, in the format as may be specified by the Board from time to time.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the reporting requirements for compliance, related party transactions, cyber incidents, and sustainability disclosures.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The HVDLE shall submit a periodic compliance report on corporate governance in the format as specified by the Board from time to time to the recognized stock exchange(s) within twenty one days from the end of the period. Details of all material transactions with related parties shall be disclosed along with the report mentioned in clause (a) of this sub-regulation. Details of cyber security incidents or breaches or loss of data or documents shall be disclosed along with the report mentioned in clause (a) of this sub- regulation, as may be specified. The report mentioned in clause (a) of sub-regulation (2) shall be signed either by the compliance officer or the chief executive officer of the HVDLE.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for listed entities with specified securities to comply with Chapter IV and specific Chapter V regulations.
policies.scope: Listed entities that have listed specified securities and non‑convertible debt or redeemable preference shares
policies.objective: Ensure adherence to detailed regulatory obligations under Chapters IV and V.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Entities with listed specified securities must comply with Chapter IV.
subpolicies.control: An entity which has listed its ‘specified securities’ and ‘non-convertible securities’ on any recognised stock exchange, shall be bound by the provisions in Chapter IV of these regulations.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: States the mandatory compliance with Chapter IV for listed entities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: An entity which has listed its ‘specified securities’ and ‘non-convertible securities’ on any recognised stock exchange, shall be bound by the provisions in Chapter IV of these regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Must comply with listed regulations (50, 51, 52, 53‑62) in Chapter V.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall additionally comply with the following regulations in Chapter V: regulation 50; regulation 51; regulation 52(3), (4), (6) and (7); regulations 53 to 62.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Lists the specific regulations that must be observed.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity described in sub-regulation (1) shall additionally comply with the following regulations in Chapter V: regulation 50; regulation 51; regulation 52(3), (4), (6) and (7); regulations 53 to 62.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: No need to re‑submit previously disclosed information unless requested.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity which has submitted any information to the stock exchange in compliance with the disclosure requirements under Chapter IV of these regulations, need not re‑submit any such information under the provisions of this regulations without prejudice to any power conferred on the Board or the stock exchange or any other authority under any law to seek any such information from the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Allows reuse of previously disclosed information, subject to powers of board or exchange.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity which has submitted any information to the stock exchange in compliance with the disclosure requirements under Chapter IV of these regulations, need not re-submit any such information under the provisions of this regulations without prejudice to any power conferred on the Board or the stock exchange or any other authority under any law to seek any such information from the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Describes the overarching obligations of listed entities to follow Chapter IV and specific Chapter V regulations.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: An entity which has listed its ‘specified securities’ and ‘non-convertible securities’ on any recognised stock exchange, shall be bound by the provisions in Chapter IV of these regulations. The listed entity described in sub-regulation (1) shall additionally comply with the following regulations in Chapter V: regulation 50; regulation 51; regulation 52(3), (4), (6) and (7); regulations 53 to 62.

policies.policy_title: Delisting of Non‑Convertible Debt Securities and Preference Shares
policies.policy_description: Regulatory framework specifying the procedures, approvals, and disclosure obligations for voluntary delisting of listed non‑convertible debt securities and non‑convertible redeemable preference shares.
policies.scope: Applicable to listed entities seeking to delist non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares on any stock exchange.
policies.objective: Ensure proper stakeholder approval, timely disclosures, and compliance with SEBI regulations throughout the delisting process.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: General delisting obligations for listed entities.
subpolicies.control: In the event specified securities of the listed entity are delisted from the stock exchange, the listed entity shall comply with all the provisions in Chapter V of these regulations. In the event that non‑convertible debt securities and non‑convertible redeemable preference shares of the listed entity do not remain listed on the stock exchange, the listed entity shall comply with all the provisions in Chapter IV of these regulations.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the baseline delisting obligations stated in clause (1) of regulation 64.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: In the event specified securities of the listed entity are delisted from the stock exchange, the listed entity shall comply with all the provisions in Chapter V of these regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which the delisting chapter applies.
subpolicies.control: The provisions of this Chapter shall be applicable to voluntary delisting of all listed non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares from all or any of the stock exchanges where such securities are listed except where: (a) a listed entity that has outstanding listed non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares issued by way of a public issue; or (b) a listed entity has more than two‑hundred securities holders excluding qualified institutional buyers in any International Securities Identification Number relating to listed non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares; or (c) non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares have been delisted by the stock exchanges as a consequence of any penalty or action initiated against the listed entity or on any grounds as specified under rule 21 of the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Rules, 1957; or (d) non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares have been delisted by the stock exchanges pursuant to redemption of such securities or shares; or (e) non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares have been delisted pursuant to a resolution plan as per Section 31 of the Insolvency Code.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the specific applicability conditions listed in clause (1) of regulation 64A.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The provisions of this Chapter shall be applicable to voluntary delisting of all listed non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares from all or any of the stock exchanges where such non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares are listed except where: (a) a listed entity that has outstanding listed non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares issued by way of a public issue; or (b) a listed entity has more than two‑hundred securities holders excluding qualified institutional buyers in any International Securities Identification Number relating to listed non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares; or (c) non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares have been delisted by the stock exchanges as a consequence of any penalty or action initiated against the listed entity or on any grounds as specified under rule 21 of the Securities Contracts (Regulation) Rules, 1957; (d) non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares have been delisted by the stock exchanges pursuant to redemption of such securities or shares; (e) non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares have been delisted pursuant to a resolution plan as per Section 31 of the Insolvency Code.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Process for obtaining in‑principle approval from stock exchanges.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall make an application to the relevant stock exchange(s) for seeking in‑principle approval for the proposed delisting of non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares in the form specified by such stock exchange, not later than fifteen working days from the date of passing of the board resolution to that effect or of receipt of any other statutory or regulatory approval, whichever is later. The application seeking in‑principle approval for the delisting of the non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares shall be disposed of by the relevant stock exchange(s) within a period not exceeding fifteen working days from the date of receipt of such application that is complete in all respects. The stock exchange shall take into account all relevant matters including the following while granting the in‑principle approval: (a) necessary approvals of the board of directors in respect of the delisting proposal; (b) due resolution of all investor grievances by the listed entity; (c) due payment of all listing fees or fines or penalties to the stock exchange; (d) compliance with all provisions of these regulations; (e) pendency of any litigation or action against the listed entity pertaining to its activities in the securities market; (f) non‑payment of any penalty imposed by the Board or the existence of any restrictions or limitations imposed by the Board upon the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Summarizes the requirements for in‑principle approval outlined in regulation 64B.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall make an application to the relevant stock exchange(s) for seeking in‑principle approval for the proposed delisting of non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares in the form specified by such stock exchange, not later than fifteen working days from the date of passing of the board resolution to that effect or of receipt of any other statutory or regulatory approval, whichever is later. The application seeking in‑principle approval for the delisting of the non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares shall be disposed of by the relevant stock exchange(s) within a period not exceeding fifteen working days from the date of receipt of such application that is complete in all respects. The stock exchange shall take into account all relevant matters including the following while granting the in‑principle approval: (a) necessary approvals of the board of directors in respect of the delisting proposal; (b) due resolution of all investor grievances by the listed entity; (c) due payment of all listing fees or fines or penalties to the stock exchange; (d) compliance with all provisions of these regulations; (e) pendency of any litigation or action against the listed entity pertaining to its activities in the securities market; (f) non‑payment of any penalty imposed by the Board or the existence of any restrictions or limitations imposed by the Board upon the listed entity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Post‑approval obligations and disclosure requirements.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall ensure that the process of obtaining necessary approval from all holders of non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares commences within three working days of the grant of in‑principle approval by the stock exchange(s). All the events in respect of the proposal of delisting for non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares beginning with the placing of the agenda for delisting before the board of directors till the delisting is completed, shall be disclosed as material information to the stock exchanges as per regulation 51 of these regulations. In addition to the information disclosed to the stock exchanges as per regulation 51 of these regulations, the following information shall be disclosed by the listed entity on its website as well as to the stock exchanges, within two working days from the date of receipt of in‑principle approval from the stock exchanges for delisting of non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares: (a) the name(s) of the stock exchange(s) from which the non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares are sought to be delisted together with the details of all such securities or shares that are sought to be delisted; (b) the cut‑off date specified for determining the list of holders of non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares to whom notice for approving the delisting proposal is mandated to be sent; (c) the objects and reasons for delisting of non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares; (d) the proposed time table from the cut‑off date as specified in clause (b) till the date of making final application to the stock exchanges for delisting of non‑convertible debt securities or non‑convertible redeemable preference shares; (e) a disclaimer specifying the following: "Once the said securities are delisted: (i) the delisted non‑convertible debt securities/ non‑convertible redeemable preference shares shall cease to be under the purview of the Act and the rules and regulations prescribed thereunder; and (ii) the holders of such non‑convertible debt securities/ non‑convertible redeemable preference shares shall not have any recourse to the investor grievance mechanism for any reason including change/ removal of the debenture trustee or in case of default, such as dispute resolution mechanism, grievance redress mechanism (SCORES), etc. under the Act and the rules and regulations made thereunder."; (f) a statement by the board of directors of the listed entity confirming that all material information which is required to be disclosed under the provisions of these regulations has been disclosed to the stock exchange; (g) a

subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall promptly inform to the stock exchange(s) of all events which are material, all information which is price sensitive and/or have bearing on performance/operation of the listed entity. subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall comply with the corporate governance provisions as applicable in its home country and other jurisdictions in which its equity shares are listed. subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to ensure equitable treatment of IDR holders in corporate actions.
subpolicies.control: If the listed entity's equity shares or other securities representing equity shares are also listed on the stock exchange(s) in countries other than its home country, it shall ensure that IDR Holders are treated in a manner equitable with security holders in home country. (2) The listed entity shall ensure that for all corporate actions, except those which are not permitted by Indian laws, it shall treat IDR holders in a manner equitable with security holders in the home country. (3) In case of take-over or delisting or buy-back of its equity shares, the listed entity shall, while following the laws applicable in its home country, give equitable treatment to IDR holders vis-à-vis security holder in home country. (4) The listed entity shall ensure protection of interests of IDR holders particularly with respect to all corporate benefits permissible under Indian laws and the laws of its home country and shall address all investor grievances adequately.

subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall publish the following information in the newspaper : (a) periodical financial results required to be disclosed; (b) Notices given to its IDR Holders by advertisement; (2) The information specified in sub-regulation (1) shall be issued in at one English national daily newspaper circulating in the whole or substantially the whole of India and in one Hindi national daily newspaper in India.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement regarding dividend payment timing and unclaimed dividends.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall pay the dividend as per the timeframe applicable in its home country or other jurisdictions where its securities are listed, whichever is earlier, so as to reach the IDR Holders on or before the date fixed for payment of dividend to holders of its equity share or other securities. (2) The listed entity shall not forfeit unclaimed dividends before the claim becomes barred by law in the home country of the listed entity, as may be applicable, and that such forfeiture, when effected, shall be annulled in appropriate cases. (3) The Indian Depository Receipts shall have two-way fungibility in the manner specified by the Board from time to time.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement regarding underlying shares and lien restrictions.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall ensure that the underlying shares of IDRs shall rank pari- passu with the existing shares of the same class and the fact of having different classes of shares based on different criteria, if any, shall be disclosed by the listed entity in the annual report. (2) The listed entity shall not exercise a lien on the fully paid underlying shares, against which the IDRs are issued, and that in respect of partly paid underlying shares, against which the IDRs are issued and shall also not exercise any lien except in respect of moneys called or payable at a fixed time in respect of such underlying shares. (3) The listed entity, subject to the requirements under the laws and regulations of its home country, if any amount be paid up in advance of calls on any underlying shares against which the IDRs are issued, shall stipulate that such amount may carry interest but shall not in respect thereof confer a right to dividend or to participate in profits.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the definition of key terms.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Intimation of intention to issue new securitised debt instruments.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall intimate the Stock exchange, of its intention to issue new securitized debt instruments either through a public issue or on private placement basis (if it proposes to list such privately placed debt securities on the Stock exchange) prior to issuing such securities.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the requirement to notify stock exchange of intention to issue.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall intimate the Stock exchange, of its intention to issue new securitized debt instruments either through a public issue or on private placement basis (if it proposes to list such privately placed debt securities on the Stock exchange) prior to issuing such securities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Advance notice of board meeting to consider issuance matters.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall intimate to the stock exchange(s), at least two working days in advance, excluding the date of the intimation and date of the meeting, regarding the meeting of its board of trustees, at which the recommendation or declaration of issue of securitized debt instruments or any other matter affecting the rights or interests of holders of securitized debt instruments is proposed to be considered.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the advance notice requirement for board meetings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Monthly reporting of statements, reports, or information to stock exchange.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall submit such statements, reports or information including financial information pertaining to Schemes to stock exchange within seven days from the end of the month/ actual payment date, either by itself or through the servicer, on a monthly basis in the format as specified by the Board from time to time: Provided that where periodicity of the receivables is not monthly, reporting shall be made for the relevant periods.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the monthly reporting obligation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Submission of loan level information to stock exchange without disclosing borrower details.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall provide the stock exchange, either by itself or through the servicer, loan level information, without disclosing particulars of individual borrowers, in manner specified by stock exchange.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the requirement to provide loan level information while protecting borrower privacy.
