subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prompt disclosure of performance‑relevant and price‑sensitive information.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall promptly inform the stock exchange(s) of all information having bearing on the performance/operation of the listed entity and price sensitive information.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the prompt disclosure requirement for material information.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall promptly inform the stock exchange(s) of all information having bearing on the performance/operation of the listed entity and price sensitive information.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Periodic review of credit ratings by registered agencies.
subpolicies.control: Every rating obtained by the listed entity with respect to securitised debt instruments shall be periodically reviewed, preferably once a year, by a credit rating agency registered by the Board.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the annual credit rating review requirement.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dissemination of rating revisions to stock exchanges.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the requirement to disseminate rating changes.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Any revision in rating(s) shall be disseminated by the stock exchange(s).

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provision of loan level information to investors without borrower details.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall provide either by itself or through the servicer, loan level information without disclosing particulars of individual borrower to its investors.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the obligation to share loan level data with investors.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall provide either by itself or through the servicer, loan level information without disclosing particulars of individual borrower to its investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure of rating revisions to investors.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the need to inform investors of rating changes.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall provide information regarding revision in rating as a result of credit rating done periodically in terms of regulation 84 above to its investors.

ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The information at sub-regulation (1) and (2) may be sent to investors in electronic form/fax if so consented by the investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Display of grievance redressal contact details.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall display the email address of the grievance redressal division and other relevant details prominently on its website and in the various materials / pamphlets/ advertisement campaigns initiated by it for creating investor awareness.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires public display of contact information for grievance redressal.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prohibition of material modifications to instrument terms without prior approval.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall ensure that no material modification shall be made to the structure of the securitized debt instruments in terms of coupon, conversion, redemption, or otherwise without prior approval of the recognised stock exchange(s) where the securitized debt instruments are listed and the listed entity shall make an application to the recognised stock exchange(s) only after the approval by Trustees.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the requirement for prior approval before modifying instrument terms.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Timely payment of interest and redemption.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall ensure timely interest/ redemption payment.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the obligation to make timely interest and redemption payments.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall ensure timely interest/ redemption payment.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Continuous availability of credit enhancement for listed instruments.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall ensure that where credit enhancement has been provided for, it shall make credit enhancement available for listed securitized debt instruments at all times.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires continuous credit enhancement availability.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall ensure that where credit enhancement has been provided for, it shall make credit enhancement available for listed securitized debt instruments at all times.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Transfer of unclaimed interest and principal to Investor Protection Fund after seven years.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall not forfeit unclaimed interest and principal and such unclaimed interest and principal shall be, after a period of seven years, transferred to the Investor Protection and Education Fund established under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Investor Protection and Education Fund) Regulations, 2009.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Describes the handling of unclaimed interest and principal.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall not forfeit unclaimed interest and principal and such unclaimed interest and principal shall be, after a period of seven years, transferred to the Investor Protection and Education Fund established under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Investor Protection and Education Fund) Regulations, 2009.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Redemption must be pro‑rata or by lot unless otherwise specified.
subpolicies.control: Unless the terms of issue provide otherwise, the listed entity shall not select any of its listed securitized debt instruments for redemption otherwise than on pro rata basis or by lot and shall promptly submit to the recognised stock exchange(s) the details thereof.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates pro‑rata or lot‑based redemption and reporting to stock exchange.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Unless the terms of issue provide otherwise, the listed entity shall not select any of its listed securitized debt instruments for redemption otherwise than on pro rata basis or by lot and shall promptly submit to the recognised stock exchange(s) the details thereof.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Obligation to remain listed until maturity or redemption, with stock exchange delisting rights.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall remain listed till the maturity or redemption of securitised debt instruments or till the same are delisted as per the procedure laid down by the Board Provided that the provisions of this sub-regulation shall not restrict the right of the recognised stock exchange(s) to delist, suspend or remove the securities at any time and for any reason which the recognised stock exchange(s) considers proper in accordance with the applicable legal provisions.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Stipulates the listing duration and stock exchange's delisting authority.

policies.policy_title: Obligations of Listed Entity Which Has Listed Its Security Receipts
policies.policy_description: Requirements for issuers of security receipts that have listed such receipts.
policies.scope: Issuers and sponsors of security receipts
policies.objective: Ensure compliance with the provisions applicable to listed security receipts.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Applicability of the chapter to issuers and sponsors of listed security receipts.
subpolicies.control: The provisions of this chapter shall apply to the issuer of security receipts which has listed its security receipts and the issuer and its sponsor shall ensure compliance with each of the provisions of these Regulations.

policies.policy_title: Disclosure of Events or Information to Stock Exchanges (Regulation 87B)
policies.policy_description: Requirements for timely disclosure of material events and information to stock exchanges.
policies.scope: Listed entities on stock exchanges
policies.objective: Ensure prompt and accurate reporting of events and updates

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: First disclosure of events/information within 24 hours.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall first disclose to stock exchange(s) of all events or information, as specified in Part E of Schedule III, as soon as reasonably possible but not later than twenty four hours from occurrence of the event or information: Provided that in case the disclosure is made after twenty four hours of occurrence of the event or information, the listed entity shall, along with such disclosures provide explanation for the delay.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the mandatory 24‑hour disclosure requirement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall first disclose to stock exchange(s) of all events or information, as specified in Part E of Schedule III, as soon as reasonably possible but not later than twenty four hours from occurrence of the event or information.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regular updates until event is resolved.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity with respect to disclosures referred to in this regulation, shall provide updates related to such disclosures on a regular basis, till such time the event is resolved/closed, with relevant explanations.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets out the ongoing update obligation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity with respect to disclosures referred to in this regulation, shall provide updates related to such disclosures on a regular basis, till such time the event is resolved/closed, with relevant explanations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reply to queries from stock exchanges.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall provide specific and adequate reply to all queries raised by stock exchange(s) with respect to any events or information.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates detailed responses to exchange queries.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall provide specific and adequate reply to all queries raised by stock exchange(s) with respect to any events or information.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Suo moto confirm or deny reported information.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity, suo moto, may confirm or deny any reported event or information to stock exchange(s).

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Allows the entity to self‑certify or reject reported events.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity, suo moto, may confirm or deny any reported event or information to stock exchange(s).

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Website disclosure and archival.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall disclose on its website or on the website of the sponsor all such events or information which has been disclosed to stock exchange(s) under this regulation, and such disclosures shall be hosted on the website of the listed entity for a minimum period of five years and thereafter as per the archival policy of the listed entity, as disclosed on its website.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires public website disclosure and five‑year archival.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity shall disclose on its website or on the website of the sponsor all such events or information which has been disclosed to stock exchange(s) under this regulation, and such disclosures shall be hosted on the website of the listed entity for a minimum period of five years and thereafter as per the archival policy of the listed entity, as disclosed on its website.

policies.scope: Issuers of listed security receipts
policies.objective: Ensure accurate and timely valuation and NAV reporting

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Quarter‑end valuation requirement.
subpolicies.control: the listed security receipts are valued at the end of each quarter i.e. as on March 31, June 30, September 30 and December 31 of every year;

ai_analysis.source_excerpt: valuation is conducted by an independent valuer;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NAV declaration within 15 days.
subpolicies.control: the net asset value is calculated on the basis of such independent valuation and the same is declared by the asset reconstruction company within fifteen days of the end of the quarter.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets 15‑day NAV declaration deadline.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the net asset value is calculated on the basis of such independent valuation and the same is declared by the asset reconstruction company within fifteen days of the end of the quarter.

policies.policy_description: Rules governing transferability and payment of security receipts.

policies.objective: Regulate transferability and payment mechanisms

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Transferability limited to qualified buyers.
subpolicies.control: Any security receipt issued would be transferable only in favour of qualified buyers in terms of Securitisation and Reconstruction of Financial Assets and Enforcement of Security Interest Act, 2002.

policies.policy_description: Procedures for fixing and notifying record dates for payments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Fixing of record date for payments.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates fixing of record date.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Advance notice of record date.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall give notice in advance of at least seven working days (excluding the date of intimation and the record date) to the stock exchange(s) of the record date or of as 140 many days as the stock exchange may agree to or require specifying the purpose of the record date.

policies.policy_description: Applicability and compliance requirements for listed mutual fund schemes.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines scope of chapter to asset management companies.

policies.policy_description: Mandates submission of annual impact reports by Social Enterprises.

policies.objective: Ensure transparent reporting of social impact

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Annual impact report must be assessed by a Social Impact Assessment Firm.

policies.policy_description: Requires listed not‑for‑profit organisations to submit quarterly statements on fund utilisation.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the required content of the quarterly statement.

policies.policy_description: Requires stock exchanges to coordinate with depositories for lock‑in and freeze compliance.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Describes the Board's power to issue guidance and clarify regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allows relaxation of strict enforcement if certain conditions are met.
subpolicies.control: (1) The Board may in the interest of investors and securities market and for the development of the securities market, relax the strict enforcement of any requirement of these regulations, if the Board is satisfied that: (a) any provision of Act(s), Rule(s), regulation(s) under which the listed entity is established or is governed by, is required to be given precedence to; or (b) the requirement may cause undue hardship to investors; or (c) the disclosure requirement is not relevant for a particular industry or class of listed entities; or (d) the requirement is technical in nature; or (e) the non‑compliance is caused due to factors affecting a class of entities but being beyond the control of the entities. (2) For seeking relaxation under sub-regulation (1), an application, giving details and the grounds on which such relaxation has been sought, shall be filed with the Board. (3) The application referred to under sub-regulation (2) shall be accompanied by a non‑refundable fee of rupees one lakh payable by way of direct credit into the bank account ...

policies.policy_description: Compliance certificate obligations for CEO and CFO as per Schedule II Part B.
policies.scope: Listed entities
policies.objective: Ensure accuracy of financial statements, absence of fraud, and proper internal controls.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: CEO and CFO must certify that financial statements present a true and fair view.
subpolicies.control: They have reviewed financial statements and the cash flow statement for the year and that to the best of their knowledge and belief: (1) these statements do not contain any materially untrue statement or omit any material fact or contain statements that might be misleading; (2) these statements together present a true and fair view of the listed entity’s affairs and are in compliance with existing accounting standards, applicable laws and regulations.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates certification of financial statements' accuracy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Certification that no fraudulent, illegal, or code‑of‑conduct violations occurred.

policies.policy_description: Set of discretionary governance requirements for listed entities as per SEBI Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements regulations.
policies.scope: Applicable to listed entities ranked 1001-2000 and other listed entities as specified.
policies.objective: Outline governance norms including board composition, shareholder rights, audit opinions, director appointments, auditor reporting, independent director meetings, and risk management committees.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Non-executive chairperson may maintain office at entity's expense and receive expense reimbursement.
subpolicies.control: A non-executive chairperson may be entitled to maintain a chairperson's office at the listed entity's expense and also allowed reimbursement of expenses incurred in performance of his/her duties.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the specific entitlement for chairperson office and expense reimbursement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A non-executive chairperson may be entitled to maintain a chairperson's office at the listed entity's expense and also allowed reimbursement of expenses incurred in performance of his/her duties.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Listed entities ranked 1001-2000 must have at least one woman independent director.
subpolicies.control: The listed entities ranked from 1001 to 2000 as per the list prepared by recognized stock exchanges shall endeavour to have at least one woman independent director on its board of directors.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the requirement for at least one woman independent director for certain ranked entities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entities ranked from 1001 to 2000 as per the list prepared by recognized stock exchanges in terms of sub-regulation (2) of regulation 3 shall endeavour to have atleast one woman independent director on its board of directors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Half-yearly financial performance declaration to shareholders.
subpolicies.control: A half-yearly declaration of financial performance including summary of the significant events in last six-months, may be sent to each household of shareholders.

ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A half-yearly declaration of financial performance including summary of the significant events in last six-months, may be sent to each household of shareholders.

subpolicies.control: The listed entity may move towards a regime of financial statements with unmodified audit opinion.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Allows the entity to aim for unmodified audit opinion.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity may move towards a regime of financial statements with unmodified audit opinion.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Separate posts for Chairperson and MD/CEO with non-executive and non-relative conditions.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity may appoint separate persons to the post of the Chairperson and the Managing Director or the Chief Executive Officer, such that the Chairperson shall be a non-executive director and not be related to the Managing Director or the Chief Executive Officer as per the definition of the term “relative” defined under the Companies Act, 2013.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets conditions for separate appointments of Chairperson and MD/CEO with independence requirements.

policies.policy_description: Comprehensive set of disclosure and reporting obligations for listed entities under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) regulations, covering acquisitions, sales, securities actions, board decisions, agreements, and fraud/default events.
policies.scope: Applies to all listed entities and their subsidiaries, associates, promoters, directors, key managerial personnel, and related parties in India.
policies.objective: Ensure timely and accurate disclosure of material corporate actions and events that affect management, control, or financial position of listed entities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'acquisition' and reporting thresholds for control, shareholding, and voting rights.
subpolicies.control: Acquiring control, directly or indirectly; acquiring or agreeing to acquire shares or voting rights such that the listed entity holds five percent or more of shares or voting rights, or a change exceeding two percent from the last disclosure.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the definition and threshold requirements for acquisition disclosures.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the word 'acquisition' shall mean,- (i) acquiring control, whether directly or indirectly; or, (ii) acquiring or agreeing to acquire shares or voting rights in, a company, whether directly or indirectly, such that - (a) the listed entity holds shares or voting rights aggregating to five per cent or more of the shares or voting rights in the said company, or; (b) there has been a change in holding from the last disclosure made under sub-clause (a) of clause (ii) of the Explanation to this sub-para and such change exceeds two per cent of the total shareholding or voting rights in the said company.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 2. Sale or disposal of subsidiary or associate
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for disclosure of sale, disposal, or agreement to sell shares that affect subsidiary or associate status.
subpolicies.control: Agreement to sell or sale of shares or voting rights that cause a company to cease being a wholly owned subsidiary, subsidiary, or associate, or where the sale exceeds the threshold specified in sub‑regulation (4) of regulation 30.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracts the definition of sale/disposal of subsidiary or associate and the associated disclosure trigger.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Explanation (2) - For the purpose of this sub‑paragraph, “sale or disposal of subsidiary” and “sale of stake in associate company” shall include- (i) an agreement to sell or sale of shares or voting rights in a company such that the company ceases to be a wholly owned subsidiary, a subsidiary or an associate company of the listed entity; or (ii) an agreement to sell or sale of shares or voting rights in a subsidiary or associate company such that the amount of the sale exceeds the threshold specified in sub‑clause (c) of clause (i) of sub‑regulation (4) of regulation 30.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of 'undertaking' and 'substantially the whole of the undertaking' as per section 180 of the Companies Act, 2013.
subpolicies.control: Undertaking and substantially the whole of the undertaking have the same meaning as given under section 180 of the Companies Act, 2013.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Provides the legal definition used in the context of disclosures.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Explanation (3)- For the purpose of this sub‑paragraph, “undertaking” and “substantially the whole of the undertaking” shall have the same meaning as given under section 180 of the Companies Act, 2013.

subpolicies.control: Issuance or forfeiture of securities, split or consolidation of shares, buyback of securities, any restriction on transferability of securities or alteration in terms or structure of existing securities including forfeiture, reissue of forfeited securities, alteration of calls, redemption of securities etc.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure of any agreements entered into by shareholders, promoters, etc., that affect management or control, including amendments or rescission.
subpolicies.control: (5A) Agreements entered into by the shareholders, promoters, promoter group entities, related parties, directors, key managerial personnel, employees of the listed entity or of its holding, subsidiary or associate company, among themselves or with the listed entity or with a third party, solely or jointly, which, either directly or indirectly or potentially or whose purpose and effect is to, impact the management or control of the listed entity or impose any restriction or create any liability upon the listed entity, shall be disclosed to the Stock Exchanges, including disclosure of any rescission, amendment or alteration of such agreements thereto, whether or not the listed entity is a party to such agreements: Provided that such agreements entered into by a listed entity in the normal course of business shall not be required to be disclosed unless they, either directly or indirectly or potentially or whose purpose and effect is to, impact the management or control of the listed entity or they are required to be disclosed in terms of any other provisions of these regulations.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for disclosing fraud and defaults by promoters, key managerial personnel, and listed entities.
policies.scope: Listed entities and their promoters, key managerial personnel
policies.objective: Ensure transparent reporting of fraudulent activities and payment defaults.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of fraud as per SEBI regulations.
subpolicies.control: ‘Fraud’ shall include fraud as defined under Regulation 2(1)(c) of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Prohibition of Fraudulent and Unfair Trade Practices relating to Securities Market) Regulations, 2003.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the definition of fraud mentioned in item 6(i).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: ‘Fraud’ shall include fraud as defined under Regulation 2(1)(c) of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Prohibition of Fraudulent and Unfair Trade Practices relating to Securities Market) Regulations, 2003.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of default as non‑payment of dues.
subpolicies.control: ‘Default’ shall mean non‑payment of interest or principal amount in full on the date when the debt has become due and payable.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the definition of default in item 6(ii).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: ‘Default’ shall mean non‑payment of interest or principal amount in full on the date when the debt has become due and payable.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Default criteria for revolving facilities.
subpolicies.control: In case of revolving facilities like cash credit, an entity would be considered to be in ‘default’ if the outstanding balance remains continuously in excess of the sanctioned limit or drawing power, whichever is lower, for more than thirty days.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures Explanation 1 from item 6.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Explanation 1- In case of revolving facilities like cash credit, an entity would be considered to be in ‘default’ if the outstanding balance remains continuously in excess of the sanctioned limit or drawing power, whichever is lower, for more than thirty days.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Impact of default by promoter or related parties.
subpolicies.control: Default by a promoter, director, key managerial personnel, senior management, subsidiary shall mean default which has or may have an impact on the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures Explanation 2 from item 6.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Explanation 2- Default by a promoter, director, key managerial personnel, senior management, subsidiary shall mean default which has or may have an impact on the listed entity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure requirement for senior management fraud unrelated to promoter.
subpolicies.control: Fraud by senior management, other than who is promoter, director or key managerial personnel, shall be required to be disclosed only if it is in relation to the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures Explanation 3 from item 6.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Explanation 3 - Fraud by senior management, other than who is promoter, director or key managerial personnel, shall be required to be disclosed only if it is in relation to the listed entity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identified as a distinct policy covering fraud and default definitions and disclosures under item 6.

policies.policy_title: Change in Key Management and Auditor Disclosure
policies.policy_description: Disclosure obligations arising from changes in directors, key managerial personnel, auditors, and compliance officers.
policies.scope: Listed entities
policies.objective: Inform stakeholders of resignations and reasons to ensure transparency.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandatory disclosure of auditor resignation reasons within 24 hours.
subpolicies.control: In case of resignation of the auditor of the listed entity, detailed reasons for resignation of auditor, as given by the said auditor, shall be disclosed by the listed entities to the stock exchanges as soon as possible but not later than twenty four hours of receipt of such reasons from the auditor.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from item 7(A) regarding auditor resignation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: In case of resignation of the auditor of the listed entity, detailed reasons for resignation of auditor, as given by the said auditor, shall be disclosed by the listed entities to the stock exchanges as soon as possible but not later than twenty four hours of receipt of such reasons from the auditor.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure of resignation letter, reasons, and related details for independent directors within seven days.
subpolicies.control: In case of resignation of an independent director of the listed entity, within seven days from the date of resignation, the following disclosures shall be made: i. The letter of resignation along with detailed reasons for the resignation; ii. Names of listed entities in which the resigning director holds directorships, indicating the category of directorship and membership of board committees, if any.; iii. The independent director shall, along with the detailed reasons, also provide a confirmation that there is no other material reasons other than those provided.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from item 7(B) detailing independent director resignation disclosures.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: In case of resignation of an independent director of the listed entity, within seven days from the date of resignation, the following disclosures shall be made: i. The letter of resignation along with detailed reasons for the resignation; ii. The independent director shall, along with the detailed reasons, also provide a confirmation that there is no other material reasons other than those provided.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure of resignation letter and reasons for key managerial personnel, senior management, compliance officer, or non‑independent director within seven days.
subpolicies.control: In case of resignation of key managerial personnel, senior management, Compliance Officer or director other than an independent director; the letter of resignation along with detailed reasons for the resignation as given by the key managerial personnel, senior management, Compliance Officer or director shall be disclosed to the stock exchanges by the listed entities within seven days from the date that such resignation comes into effect.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Extracted from item 7(C) covering key managerial personnel and senior management resignation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: In case of resignation of key managerial personnel, senior management, Compliance Officer or director other than an independent director; the letter of resignation along with detailed reasons for the resignation as given by the key managerial personnel, senior management, Compliance Officer or director shall be disclosed to the stock exchanges by the listed entities within seven days from the date that such resignation comes into effect.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure if Managing Director or CEO is indisposed/unavailable for >45 days in a 90‑day rolling period.

policies.policy_title: Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements – Non‑Convertible Securities

policies.scope: Listed entities issuing non‑convertible securities on Indian stock exchanges

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to disclose expected default in payment of interest, dividend, redemption, or repayment, and default in creation of security.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (1) defines the event of expected default that must be reported.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure of any orders restraining transfer of non‑convertible securities, including holder details.
subpolicies.control: any attachment or prohibitory orders restraining the listed entity from transferring non‑convertible securities from the account of the registered holders along‑with the particulars of the numbers of securities so affected , the names of the registered holders and their demat account details;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (2) mandates reporting of regulatory orders affecting securities transfer.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Report any action that results in redemption, reduction, cancellation, or retirement of non‑convertible securities.
subpolicies.control: any action which shall result in the redemption, reduction, cancellation, retirement in whole or in part of any non‑convertible securities;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (3) covers corporate actions that alter the structure of non‑convertible securities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclose actions affecting interest or dividend payments, including defaults and failure to create charges.
subpolicies.control: any action that shall affect adversely payment of interest on non‑convertible debt securities or payment of dividend on non‑convertible redeemable preference shares including default by issuer to pay interest on non‑convertible debt securities or redemption amount and failure to create a charge on the assets;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (4) requires reporting of events that impair payment obligations.

subpolicies.control: any change in the form or nature of any of its non‑convertible securities that are listed on the stock exchange(s) or in the rights or privileges of the holders thereof and make an application for listing of the securities as changed, if the stock exchange(s) so require;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (5) obligates disclosure of modifications to listed securities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Report changes in business character, natural calamity disruption, or commencement of commercial operations.
subpolicies.control: any changes in the general character or nature of business / activities, disruption of operation due to natural calamity, and commencement of commercial production / commercial operations;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (6) captures material business changes affecting the issuer.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclose strikes or lock‑outs that affect payment capacity.
subpolicies.control: any events such as strikes and lock outs. which have a bearing on the interest payment/ dividend payment / principal repayment capacity;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Clause (9) sets a threshold for reporting prolonged defaults.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 10. Failure to create charge on assets

subpolicies.control: failure to create charge on the assets within the stipulated time period;

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) events.
subpolicies.control: the following events in relation to the Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) of a listed corporate debtor under the Insolvency Code: a) Filing of application by the corporate applicant for initiation of CIRP, also specifying the amount of default; b) Filing of application by the financial creditors for initiation of CIRP against the corporate debtor, also specifying the amount of default; c) Admission of application by the Tribunal, along with the amount of default or rejection or withdrawal, as applicable; d) Public announcement made pursuant to the order passed by the Tribunal under section 13 of Insolvency Code; e) List of creditors as required to be displayed by the corporate debtor under regulation 13(2)(c) of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (Insolvency Resolution Process for Corporate Persons) Regulations, 2016; f) Appointment/ Replacement of the Resolution Professional; g) Prior or post-facto intimation of the meetings of Committee of Creditors; h) Brief particulars of invitation of resolution plans under section 25(2)(h) of Insolvency Code in the Form specified under regulation 36A (5) of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (Insolvency Resolution Process for Corporate Persons) Regulations, 2016; i) Number of resolution plans received by Resolution Professional; j) Filing of resolution plan with the Tribunal; k) Approval of resolution plan by the Tribunal or rejection, if applicable; l) Specific features and details of the resolution plan as approved by the Adjudicating Authority under the Insolvency Code, not involving commercial secrets, including details such as: (i) Pre and Post net-worth of the company; (ii) Details of assets of the company post CIRP; (iii) Details of securities continuing to be imposed on the companies’ assets; (iv) Other material liabilities imposed on the company; (v) Detailed pre and post shareholding pattern assuming 100% conversion of convertible securities; (vi) Details of funds infused in the company, creditors paid-off; (vii) Additional liability on the incoming investors due to the transaction, source of such funding etc.; (viii) Impact on the investor - revised P/E, RONW ratios etc.; (ix) Names of the new promoters, key managerial persons(s), if any and their past experience in the business or employment. In case where promoters are companies, history of such company and names of natural persons in control; (x) Brief description of business strategy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Handling of non‑quantifiable audit qualifications
subpolicies.control: With respect to audit qualifications where the impact of the qualification is not quantifiable: i. The management shall mandatorily make an estimate which the auditor shall review and report accordingly. ii. Notwithstanding the above, the management may be permitted to not provide estimate on matters like going concerns or sub-judice matters; in which case, the management shall provide the reasons and the auditor shall review the same and report accordingly.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Resolution or explanation of auditor reservations
subpolicies.control: If the auditor has expressed any modified opinion(s) or other reservation(s) in his 673[/her] audit report or limited review report in respect of the financial results of any previous financial year or quarter which has an impact on the profit or loss of the reportable period, the listed entity shall include as a note to the financial results - (i) how the modified opinion(s) or other reservation(s) has been resolved; or (ii) if the same has not been resolved, the reason thereof and the steps which the listed entity intends to take in the matter.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires note disclosing resolution or explanation of auditor reservations affecting prior periods.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: If the auditor has expressed any modified opinion(s) or other reservation(s) in his 673[/her] audit report or limited review report in respect of the financial results of any previous financial year or quarter which has an impact on the profit or loss of the reportable period, the listed entity shall include as a note to the financial results - (i) how the modified opinion(s) or other reservation(s) has been resolved; or (ii) if the same has not been resolved, the reason thereof and the steps which the listed entity intends to take in the matter.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure of name change and new business line
subpolicies.control: If the listed entity has changed its name suggesting any new line of business, it shall disclose the net sales or income, expenditure and net profit or loss after tax figures pertaining to the said new line of business separately in the financial results and shall continue to make such disclosures for the three years succeeding the date of change in name: Provided that the tax expense shall be allocated between the said new line of business and other business of the listed entity in the ratio of the respective figures of net profit before tax, subject to any exemption, deduction or concession available under the tax laws.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates separate disclosure of financials for the new business line after a name change.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: If the listed entity has changed its name suggesting any new line of business, it shall disclose the net sales or income, expenditure and net profit or loss after tax figures pertaining to the said new line of business separately in the financial results and shall continue to make such disclosures for the three years succeeding the date of change in name: Provided that the tax expense shall be allocated between the said new line of business and other business of the listed entity in the ratio of the respective figures of net profit before tax, subject to any exemption, deduction or concession available under the tax laws.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure for uncommenced commercial production
subpolicies.control: If the listed entity had not commenced commercial production or commercial operations during the reportable period, the listed entity shall, instead of submitting financial results, disclose the following details: (i) details of amount raised i.e. proceeds of any issue of shares or debentures made by the listed entity; (ii) the portions thereof which is utilized and that remaining unutilized; (iii) the details of investment made pending utilisation ; (iv) brief description of the project which is pending completion; (v) status of the project and (vi) expected date of commencement of commercial production or commercial operations: Provided that the details mentioned above shall be approved by the board of directors based on certification by the chief executive officer and chief financial officer.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires disclosure of fundraising and project status when commercial operations have not started.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: If the listed entity had not commenced commercial production or commercial operations during the reportable period, the listed entity shall, instead of submitting financial results, disclose the following details: (i) details of amount raised i.e. proceeds of any issue of shares or debentures made by the listed entity; (ii) the portions thereof which is utilized and that remaining unutilized; (iii) the details of investment made pending utilisation ; (iv) brief description of the project which is pending completion; (v) status of the project and (vi) expected date of commencement of commercial production or commercial operations: Provided that the details mentioned above shall be approved by the board of directors based on certification by the chief executive officer and chief financial officer.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure of exceptional items
subpolicies.control: All items of income and expenditure arising out of transactions of exceptional nature shall be disclosed.

ai_analysis.source_excerpt: All items of income and expenditure arising out of transactions of exceptional nature shall be disclosed.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure of extraordinary items
subpolicies.control: Extraordinary items, if applicable, shall be disclosed in accordance with Accounting Standard 5 (AS 5 - Net Profit or Loss for the Period, Prior Period Items and Changes in Accounting Policies) or Companies (Accounting Standards) Rules, 2006, whichever is applicable.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Seasonal variation disclosure
subpolicies.control: The listed entity, whose revenues are subject to material seasonal variations, shall disclose the seasonal nature of their activities and the listed entity may supplement their financial results with information for the twelve month period ending on the last day of the quarter for the current and preceding years on a rolling basis.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires disclosure of seasonal nature and supplemental rolling twelve‑month data.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity, whose revenues are subject to material seasonal variations, shall disclose the seasonal nature of their activities and the listed entity may supplement their financial results with information for the twelve month period ending on the last day of the quarter for the current and preceding years on a rolling basis.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Material events and transactions disclosure
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall disclose any event or transaction which occurred during or before the quarter that is material to an understanding of the results for the quarter including but not limited to completion of expansion and diversification programmes, strikes and lock-outs, change in management, change in capital structure and the listed entity shall also disclose similar material events or transactions that take place subsequent to the end of the quarter.

policies.policy_description: Requirement to disclose the effect of material changes in the composition of the listed entity on financial results.
policies.scope: Listed entities
policies.objective: Inform stakeholders about material changes that could affect financial performance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Obligation to disclose material changes affecting financial results.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall disclose the effect on the financial results of material changes in the composition of the listed entity, if any, including but not limited to business combinations, acquisitions or disposal of subsidiaries and long term investments, any other form of restructuring and discontinuance of operations.

policies.policy_description: Requirement to prepare segment reports in accordance with AS-17 / Ind AS 108.

policies.policy_description: Comprehensive rules for preparing, auditing, and disclosing financial results, including periodicity, accounting principles, and audit reporting.
policies.scope: Listed entities preparing financial results
policies.objective: Standardize the preparation, audit, and disclosure of financial statements across jurisdictions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Financial results can be disclosed annually, half-yearly, or quarterly as required.
subpolicies.control: Financial results may be given on annual, half yearly and/or quarterly basis, as required under the requirements of the home country.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the frequency options for financial result disclosure.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Financial results may be given on annual, half yearly and/or quarterly basis, as required under the requirements of the home country.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Permitted accounting frameworks and required reconciliations.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity may prepare and disclose its financial results in accordance with Indian GAAP or International Financial Reporting Standards IFRS or US GAAP (2) In case the listed entity prepares and discloses the financial results as per US GAAP, a reconciliation statement vis-a-vis Indian GAAP and summary of significant differences between the Indian GAAP and US GAAP has to be annexed (3) If financial results are prepared in accordance with IFRS, then listed entity shall annex only the summary of significant differences between the Indian GAAP and IFRS (4) If the listed entity is shifting from IFRS to US GAAP or vice versa then the accounts relating to the previous period shall be properly restated for comparison (5) The Accounting / Reporting Standard followed for any interim results shall be consistent with that of the Annual results (6) The financial results so submitted shall be based on the same set of accounting policies as those followed in the previous year provided that in case, there are changes in the accounting policies, the results of previous year shall be restated as per the present accounting policies, to make it comparable with current year results;

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Enumerates permissible accounting frameworks and required reconciliation/restatement practices.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The listed entity may prepare and disclose its financial results in accordance with Indian GAAP or International Financial Reporting Standards IFRS or US GAAP

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Auditing requirements based on the accounting framework used.
subpolicies.control: In case the listed entity prepares and discloses the financial results as per Indian GAAP, the listed entity shall ensure that the annual, half yearly and/or quarterly results, as required under the laws , rules or regulations of home country, shall be audited or subject to limited review by a Chartered Accountant in accordance with Auditing ad Assurance Standards (2) In case the listed entity prepares and discloses the financial results as per US GAAP or IFRS, the listed entity shall ensure that the annual, half yearly and/or quarterly results, as required under the laws, rules or regulations of home country shall be audited or subject to limited review by professional accountant or certified public accountant in accordance with the International Standards on Auditing The auditor’s report shall also be prepared in accordance with the International Standards on Auditing.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies audit requirements depending on the accounting standard applied.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: In case the listed entity prepares and discloses the financial results as per Indian GAAP, the listed entity shall ensure that the annual, half yearly and/or quarterly results, as required under the laws , rules or regulations of home country, shall be audited or subject to limited review by a Chartered Accountant in accordance with Auditing ad Assurance Standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandatory disclosures including audit qualifications, format, currency, and translation.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall disclose the audit qualification(s) or any other audit reservation(s) along with the financial results in addition to the explanatory statement as to how audit qualification(s) or any other audit reservation(s) in respect of the audited accounts of the previous accounting year have been addressed in the financial results (2) Format (a) The listed entity shall ensure that, if Indian GAAP is followed in preparation of the financial results the format of the disclosure of financial results shall be as prescribed by the Board (b) In case if Indian GAAP is not followed, the format of such disclosure shall be as per the disclosure requirements of the listed entity in the home country where the listed entity is listed (3) The listed entity shall make disclosures of its financial information in its functional currency/reporting currency/national currency and the reporting currency shall be restricted to Sterling Pound/Euro/Yen/US Dollar (4) The listed entity shall provide convenient translation into Indian Rupees of the latest year’s/periods statements (as the case may be) of consolidated profit and losses, assets and liabilities and cash flows, at the closing rate of exchange, as at the date on which the financial information is presented (5) The listed entity shall provide convenient translations in English and other notes such that the IDR Holders are able to understand such financial statements;

policies.policy_description: Comprehensive set of disclosure and governance obligations for listed entities as outlined in the section.
policies.scope: Listed entities
policies.objective: Ensure transparent reporting and compliance with corporate governance norms.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: General shareholder information disclosure requirements.
subpolicies.control: General shareholder information: (a) quarterly results; (b) newspapers wherein results normally published; (c) any website, where displayed; (d) whether it also displays official news releases; and (e) presentations made to institutional investors or to the analysts; (f) credit ratings; (g) credit rating revisions; (h) suspension explanation; (i) registrar and share transfer agents; (j) share transfer system; (k) shareholding distribution; (l) dematerialization; (m) GDR/ADR/warrants; (n) commodity price risk and hedging; (o) plant locations; (p) correspondence address; (q) credit ratings list.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the full set of shareholder information items enumerated under clause (9).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (9) General shareholder information: (a) quarterly results; (b) newspapers wherein results normally published; (c) any website, where displayed; (d) whether it also displays official news releases; and (e) presentations made to institutional investors or to the analysts; (f) 685[***]; (g) 686[***]; (h) in case the securities are suspended from trading, the directors report shall explain the reason thereof; (i) registrar to an issue and share transfer agents; (j) share transfer system; (k) distribution of shareholding; (l) dematerialization of shares and liquidity; (m) outstanding global depository receipts or american depository receipts or warrants or any convertible instruments, conversion date and likely impact on equity; (n) commodity price risk or foreign exchange risk and hedging activities; (o) plant locations; (p) address for correspondence; 688[(q) list of all credit ratings obtained by the entity along with any revisions thereto during the relevant financial year, for all debt instruments of such entity or any fixed deposit programme or any scheme or proposal of the listed entity involving mobilization of funds, whether in India or abroad.]

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Other disclosures including related party transactions, compliance, and additional details.
subpolicies.control: Other Disclosures: (a) material related party transaction disclosures; (b) non‑compliance details; (c) vigil mechanism and whistleblower policy; (d) compliance with mandatory and non‑mandatory requirements; (e) web link for material subsidiary policy; (f) web link for related party transaction policy; (g) commodity price risk disclosure; (h) utilization of funds raised; (i) auditor debarment certificate; (j) board non‑acceptance of committee recommendation; (k) total auditor fees; (l) sexual harassment complaints; (m) loans to related directors; (n) material subsidiary details.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Encompasses the extensive list of additional disclosure obligations under clause (10).
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (10) Other Disclosures: (a) disclosures on materially significant related party transactions that may have potential conflict with the interests of listed entity at large; 685 Omitted ... (b) details of non-compliance by the listed entity, penalties, strictures imposed on the listed entity by stock exchange(s) or the board or any statutory authority, on any matter related to capital markets, during the last three years; (c) details of establishment of vigil mechanism 689[/] whistle blower policy, and affirmation that no personnel has been denied access to the audit committee; (d) details of compliance with mandatory requirements and adoption of the non- mandatory requirements; (e) web link where policy for determining ‘material’ subsidiaries is disclosed; (f) web link where policy on dealing with related party transactions; (g) disclosure of commodity price risks and commodity hedging activities. 690[(h) Details of utilization of funds raised through preferential allotment or qualified institutions placement as specified under Regulation 32 (7A). (i) a certificate from a company secretary in practice that none of the directors on the board of the company have been debarred or disqualified from being appointed or continuing as directors of companies by the Board/Ministry of Corporate Affairs or any such statutory authority. (j) where the board had not accepted any recommendation of any committee of the board which is mandatorily required, in the relevant financial year, the same to be disclosed along with reasons thereof: Provided that the clause shall only apply where recommendation of / submission by the committee is required for the approval of the Board of Directors and shall not apply where prior approval of the relevant committee is required for undertaking any transaction under these Regulations. (k) total fees for all services paid by the listed entity and its subsidiaries, on a consolidated basis, to the statutory auditor and all entities in the network firm/network entity of which the statutory auditor is a part.] 691[(l) disclosures in relation to the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013: a. number of complaints filed during the financial year b. number of complaints disposed of during the financial year c. number of complaints pending as on end of the financial year.] 692[(m) disclosure by listed entity and its subsidiaries of ‘Loans and advances in the nature of loans to firms/companies in which directors are interested by name and amount’: Provided that this requirement shall be applicable to all listed entities except for listed banks.] 693[(n) Details of material subsidiaries of the listed entity; including the date and place of incorporation and the name and date of appointment of the statutory auditors of such subsidiaries.]

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure of non‑compliance reasons for governance report.
subpolicies.control: Non‑compliance of any requirement of corporate governance report of sub‑paras (2) to (10) above, with reasons thereof shall be disclosed.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies that any non‑compliance with governance report requirements must be explained.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (11) Non-compliance of any requirement of corporate governance report of sub- paras (2) to (10) above, with reasons thereof shall be disclosed.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Extent of adoption of discretionary governance requirements.
subpolicies.control: The corporate governance report shall also disclose the extent to which the discretionary requirements as specified in Part E of Schedule II have been adopted.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates reporting on adoption level of discretionary governance requirements.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (12) The corporate governance report shall also disclose the extent to which the discretionary requirements as specified in Part E of Schedule II have been adopted.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Placement of governance disclosures in annual report.
subpolicies.control: The disclosures of the compliance with corporate governance requirements specified in regulation 17 to 27 and clauses (b) to (i) of sub-regulation (2) of regulation 46 shall be made in the section on corporate governance of the annual report.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines where governance compliance disclosures must appear in the annual report.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: (13) The disclosures of the compliance with corporate governance requirements specified in regulation 17 to 27 and clauses (b) to (i) of sub-regulation (2) of regulation 46 shall be made in the section on corporate governance of the annual report.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: CEO declaration on code of conduct compliance.
subpolicies.control: Declaration signed by the chief executive officer stating that the members of board of directors and senior management personnel have affirmed compliance with the code of conduct of board of directors and senior management.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires a signed declaration from the CEO regarding code of conduct compliance.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: D. Declaration signed by the chief executive officer stating that the members of board of directors and senior management personnel have affirmed compliance with the code of conduct of board of directors and senior management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Auditor or CS compliance certificate.
subpolicies.control: Compliance certificate from either the auditors or practicing company secretaries regarding compliance of conditions of corporate governance shall be annexed with the directors’ report.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires a compliance certificate from auditors or company secretaries to be attached.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: E. Compliance certificate from either the auditors or practicing company secretaries regarding compliance of conditions of corporate governance shall be annexed with the directors’ report.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Demat suspense account disclosures.
subpolicies.control: Disclosures with respect to demat suspense account/ unclaimed suspense account (1) The listed entity shall disclose the following details in its annual report, as long as there are shares in the demat suspense account or unclaimed suspense account, as applicable : (a) aggregate number of shareholders and the outstanding shares in the suspense account lying at the beginning of the year; (b) number of shareholders who approached listed entity for transfer of shares from suspense account during the year; (c) number of shareholders to whom shares were transferred from suspense account during the year; (d) aggregate number of shareholders and the outstanding shares in the suspense account lying at the end of the year; (e) that the voting rights on these shares shall remain frozen till the rightful owner of such shares claims the shares.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates detailed reporting on demat suspense account activity.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: F. Disclosures with respect to demat suspense account/ unclaimed suspense account (1) The listed entity shall disclose the following details in its annual report, as long as there are shares in the demat suspense account or unclaimed suspense account, as applicable : (a) aggregate number of shareholders and the outstanding shares in the suspense account lying at the beginning of the year; (b) number of shareholders who approached listed entity for transfer of shares from suspense account during the year; (c) number of shareholders to whom shares were transferred from suspense account during the year; (d) aggregate number of shareholders and the outstanding shares in the suspense account lying at the end of the year; (e) that the voting rights on these shares shall remain frozen till the rightful owner of such shares claims the shares.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Disclosure of binding agreements for listed entities.
subpolicies.control: G. 694[Disclosure of certain types of agreements binding listed entities (1) Information disclosed under clause 5A of paragraph A of Part A of Schedule III of these

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requires disclosure of certain binding agreements as per Schedule III.
