policies.policy_title: Manner of dealing with unclaimed shares (Schedule VI)
policies.policy_description: Procedures for handling unclaimed shares, including delegation, reminders, suspense accounts, claims, and corporate benefits.
policies.scope: Listed entities managing unclaimed shares in physical and demat form.
policies.objective: Ensure proper management and eventual transfer of unclaimed shares to rightful owners.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The listed entity may delegate procedural requirements to a share transfer agent.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity may delegate the following procedural requirements to a share transfer agent.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the delegation authority granted to share transfer agents.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A. The listed entity may delegate the following procedural requirements to a share transfer agent.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The listed entity must send at least three reminders to shareholders of unclaimed shares.
subpolicies.control: The listed entity shall send at least three reminders at the address as mentioned below: (a) For shares in physical form, reminders shall be sent to the address given in the application form as well as last available address as per listed entity’s record. (b) For shares in demat form, reminders shall be sent to the address captured in depository’s database or address given in the application form, in case of application made in physical form.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies the reminder procedure for physical and demat shares.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: B. Reminders to be sent (1) The listed entity shall send at least three reminders...

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 3. Procedure in case of non receipt of response to reminders
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Handling unclaimed shares when no response is received, including suspense accounts and record-keeping.
subpolicies.control: For shares in demat form, the unclaimed shares shall be credited to a demat suspense account with a Depository Participant; for physical form, shares shall be transferred to an "Unclaimed Suspense Account" and dematerialised; the listed entity must maintain details of each allottee and hold the suspense account purely on their behalf, with shares only transferable upon claim; unpaid/unclaimed dividends must be transferred per Section 124(5) and 124(6) of the Companies Act.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Describes the procedural steps for non‑receipt of responses and the creation of suspense accounts.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: C. Procedure in case of non receipt of response to reminders (1) For shares in demat form, the unclaimed shares shall be credited to a demat suspense account...

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 4. Procedure in case of claim by allottee
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Process for crediting shares to the rightful allottee when they come forward to claim.
subpolicies.control: As and when the allottee approaches the listed entity, after verification of identity, the listed entity shall credit the shares in the Unclaimed Suspense Account or demat suspense account to the allottee’s demat account to the extent of entitlement.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Outlines the claim procedure and verification steps.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: D. Procedure in case of claim by allottee (1) As and when the allottee approaches the listed entity, the listed entity shall, after proper verification...

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: 5. Dealing with Corporate Benefits and Voting Rights
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Treatment of corporate benefits and voting rights on unclaimed shares.
subpolicies.control: Any corporate benefits in terms of securities accruing on such shares shall also be credited to such demat suspense account or unclaimed suspense account for up to seven years; voting rights on unclaimed shares remain frozen until claimed.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the handling of benefits and frozen voting rights.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: E. Dealing with Corporate Benefits (in terms of securities accruing) and Voting Rights on such Unclaimed Shares (1) Any corporate benefits...

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identifies the set of rules governing unclaimed share handling under Schedule VI.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Schedule VI: Manner of dealing with unclaimed shares

policies.policy_title: Transfer and transmission of securities (Schedule VII)
policies.policy_description: Rules governing the registration, verification, and documentation of securities transfer and transmission.
policies.scope: Listed entities and participants involved in the transfer and transmission of securities.
policies.objective: Ensure accurate and secure registration of transfers, including PAN requirements and signature verification.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Transferee and transferor must provide PAN card for registration of transfer.
subpolicies.control: For registration of transfer of securities, the transferee(s) as well as transferor(s) shall furnish a copy of their PAN card to the listed entity for registration of transfer of securities.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates PAN submission for transfer registration.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A. REQUIREMENT OF PAN (1) For registration of transfer of securities, the transferee(s) as well as transferor(s) shall furnish a copy of their PAN card...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Procedure when signatures differ only slightly.
subpolicies.control: In case of minor differences in the signature of the transferor(s), the listed entity shall promptly send to the first transferor(s) via speed post an intimation of the defect and inform the transferor(s) that objection, supported by valid proof, is not lodged within fifteen days; if no objection is received, the securities shall be transferred, provided no fraud or forgery is suspected.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets out the handling of minor signature discrepancies.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: B. DIFFERENCES IN SIGNATURE (1) In case of minor differences in the signature of the transferor(s), the listed entity shall promptly send to the first transferor(s), via speed post an intimation...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Procedure when signatures differ significantly or are unavailable.
subpolicies.control: In case of major differences in, or non‑availability of, the signature of the transferor(s), the listed entity shall send an Objection Memo to both transferor(s) and transferee(s) via speed post, request additional documents (affidavit, cancelled cheque, banker’s attestation, contact details), and only transfer the securities after receiving the requested documents and confirming no fraud or forgery, maintaining proof of delivery.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Describes the steps for major signature issues and required documentation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: B. DIFFERENCES IN SIGNATURE (2) In case of major differences in, or non‑availability of, the signature of the transferor(s), the listed entity shall follow the following procedure...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Alternative documentation when PAN is unavailable and handling mismatches.
subpolicies.control: In cases where PAN card is not available (e.g., residents of Sikkim), it may be substituted with identity proof; for mismatches in PAN details or name changes, the listed entity may collect the PAN card after verifying supporting documents; this is subject to verification of identity.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Covers PAN exemptions and verification of name/mismatch scenarios.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: A. REQUIREMENT OF PAN (3) In cases where PAN card is not available i.e. in case of residents of Sikkim, the requirement of PAN Card may be substituted with Identity proof. (4) In case of mismatch in PAN card details as well as difference in maiden name and current name, in case of married women, of the holder(s) of securities, the listed entity may collect the PAN card as submitted by the transferee(s) or transferor(s) as the case maybe: Provided that this shall be subject to the listed entity verifying the veracity...

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the procedural framework for securities transfer under Schedule VII.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: SCHEDULE VII: TRANSFER AND TRANSMISSION OF SECURITIES

policies.policy_title: Transmission of Securities – Documentation and Threshold Requirements
policies.policy_description: Regulations specifying documentation, affidavit, indemnity bond, and threshold limits for transmission of securities in physical and dematerialized mode.
policies.scope: Applies to listed entities, share transfer agents, and beneficiaries dealing with transmission of securities under SEBI LODR (Fourth Amendment) Regulations, 2022.
policies.objective: Ensure proper verification of ownership and legal claim, and define monetary thresholds for simplified documentation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Threshold limit for dematerialized mode without nominee.
subpolicies.control: Threshold limit is rupees five lakhs only per beneficiary owner account for transmission of securities held in dematerialized mode without a nominee.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines the monetary threshold for simplified documentation in dematerialized mode.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the threshold limit is rupees five lakhs only per beneficiary owner account.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Required documents for physical mode with nominee.
subpolicies.control: Duly signed transmission request form by the nominee; original or copy of death certificate attested by a notary public or gazetted officer; self‑attested copy of PAN card of the nominee.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Lists the documentation required when a nominee exists for physical transmission.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: duly signed transmission request form by the nominee; (ii) original or copy of death certificate duly attested by a notary public or by a gazetted officer; (iii) self attested copy of PAN card of the nominee.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Required documents for physical mode without nominee.
subpolicies.control: Notarized affidavit from all legal heirs on non‑judicial stamp paper; optionally succession certificate or probate; otherwise no objection certificate or family settlement deed; indemnity bond if value exceeds Rs 2 lakh.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Specifies documentation for transmission when no nominee exists in physical mode.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: a notarized affidavit from all legal heir(s) made on non‑judicial stamp paper of appropriate value, to the effect of identification and claim of legal ownership to the securities

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Threshold requiring succession certificate/probate for physical mode (up to Rs 2 lakh).
subpolicies.control: Value up to rupees two lakh per listed entity may be transmitted using a succession certificate or probate of will, etc.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets a lower monetary threshold where simplified proof is acceptable.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: for value of securities, threshold limit of up to rupees two lakh only, per listed entity, as on date of application, a succession certificate or probate of will ... may be submitted

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Alternative documentation allowed for physical mode up to Rs 5 lakh.
subpolicies.control: If primary documents are unavailable, legal heirs may submit a no objection certificate and a notarized indemnity bond; entity may enhance threshold up to Rs 5 lakh.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Provides alternative documentation and defines a higher threshold for physical mode.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: the legal heir(s) /claimant(s) may submit the following documents: (i) no objection certificate ... (ii) a notarized indemnity bond ... Provided that the listed entity may, at its discretion, enhance the value of securities from the threshold limit of rupees five lakhs, in case of securities held in physical mode.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Threshold requiring succession certificate/probate for dematerialized mode (up to Rs 5 lakh).
subpolicies.control: Value up to rupees five lakhs per beneficial owner in dematerialized mode may be transmitted using a succession certificate or probate of will, etc.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines a monetary threshold for dematerialized securities similar to physical mode.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: for cases where value of securities is up to rupees five lakhs per listed entity in case of securities held in physical mode, and up to rupees fifteen lakhs per beneficial owner in case of securities held in dematerialized mode

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Alternative documentation allowed for dematerialized mode up to Rs 15 lakh.
subpolicies.control: If primary documents are unavailable, legal heirs may submit a no objection certificate and a notarized indemnity bond; entity may enhance threshold up to Rs 15 lakh.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Provides alternative documentation and defines a higher threshold for dematerialized securities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: for cases where value of securities is up to rupees fifteen lakhs per beneficial owner in case of securities held in dematerialized mode, ... the legal heir(s) /claimant(s) may submit the following documents: (i) no objection certificate ... (ii) a notarized indemnity bond ...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Indemnity bond requirement for transmission.
subpolicies.control: An indemnity bond on appropriate non‑judicial stamp paper must be executed by the legal heir(s)/claimant(s) to indemnify the share transfer agent or listed entity, in the format specified by the Board.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Mandates execution of an indemnity bond as part of the documentation set.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: an indemnity bond made on appropriate non judicial stamp paper, indemnifying the Share Transfer Agent / listed entity

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: The section enumerates detailed documentation and threshold requirements for transmitting securities, covering both nominee and non‑nominee scenarios, physical and dematerialized holdings, and associated procedural controls.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: In case of transmission of securities held in dematerialized mode, where the securities are held in a single name without a nominee, for the purpose of simplified documentation ... the threshold limit is rupees five lakhs only per beneficiary owner account.

policies.policy_title: Insolvency Code – Audit Report Review and Regulatory Amendments
policies.policy_description: Regulations governing the qualified audit report review committee, its recommendations, board actions, revised pro-forma financial results submission, review timelines, and amendments to SEBI regulations.
policies.scope: Applicable to listed entities, stock exchanges, and intermediaries under the SEBI framework.
policies.objective: Ensure proper handling of modified audit opinions, protect investors, and enforce compliance with reporting standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Composition of Qualified Audit Report Review Committee
subpolicies.control: The qualified audit report review committee shall be constituted by the board comprising of representatives from Institute of Chartered Accountants of India, stock exchange(s), Ministry of Corporate Affairs etc.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requirement defining the composition of the committee.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The qualified audit report review committee shall be constituted by the board comprising of representatives from Institute of Chartered Accountants of India, stock exchange(s), Ministry of Corporate Affairs etc.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Review of Cases and Guidance to Board
subpolicies.control: The qualified audit report review committee shall review the cases received from the stock exchange(s) and guide the Board in processing the annual audit reports with modified opinion(s).

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requirement describing the review function and guidance to the Board.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: The qualified audit report review committee shall review the cases received from the stock exchange(s) and guide the Board in processing the annual audit reports with modified opinion(s).

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recommendation – Impact Not Significant
subpolicies.control: If qualified audit report review committee is of the view that the impact of modified opinion is not significant, it may recommend rectification of such modified opinion in the subsequent financial year.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requirement specifying recommendation for non‑significant impact.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: If qualified audit report review committee is of the view that the impact of modified opinion is not significant, it may recommend rectification of such modified opinion in the subsequent financial year.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recommendation – Significant Impact and Unsatisfactory Explanation
subpolicies.control: If qualified audit report review committee is of the view that the impact of modified opinion is significant and the explanation given by the listed entity concerned in Form B is unsatisfactory, the case may be referred to the Financial Reporting Review Board of Institute of Chartered Accountants of India, for their opinion on whether the modified opinion is justified.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Requirement specifying referral for significant impact and unsatisfactory explanation.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: If qualified audit report review committee is of the view that the impact of modified opinion is significant and the explanation given by the listed entity concerned in Form B is unsatisfactory, the case may be referred to the Financial Reporting Review Board of Institute of Chartered Accountants of India, for their opinion on whether the modified opinion is justified.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Insertion of new Chapter XI on Listing of Securities on Stock Exchanges.
subpolicies.control: 'CHAPTER XI LISTING OF SECURITIES ON STOCK EXCHANGES In-principle approval of recognized stock exchange(s). 107. (1) The issuer or the issuing company, as the case may be, shall obtain in-principle approval from recognised stock exchange as follows: (a) in case of an initial public offer or an issue of Indian Depository Receipts (hereinafter referred to as ‘IDRs’) , from all the recognised stock exchange(s) on which the issuer or the issuing company, proposes to get its specified securities or IDRs, as the case may be, listed; and (b) in case of other issues, before issuance of further securities, as follows: (i) where the securities are listed only on recognised stock exchange(s) having nationwide trading terminals, from all such stock exchange(s); (ii) where the securities are not listed on any recognised stock exchange having nationwide trading terminals, from all the stock exchange(s) on which the securities of the issuer are proposed to be listed; (iii) where the specified are listed on recognised stock exchange(s) having nationwide trading terminals as well as on the recognised stock exchange(s) not having nationwide trading terminals, from all recognised stock exchange(s) having nationwide trading terminals.'

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Completion of pre-listing formalities and application for listing within twenty days.
subpolicies.control: (1) The issuer or the issuing company, as the case may be, shall complete the pre-listing formalities within the time lines specified by the Board from time to time. (2) The issuer or the issuing company, as the case may be, shall, make an application for listing, within twenty days from the date of allotment, to one or more recognized stock exchange(s) along with the documents specified by stock exchange(s) from time to time. (3) In case of delay in making application for listing beyond twenty days from the date of allotment, the issuer or the issuing company, as the case may be, shall pay penal interest to allottees for each day of delay at the rate of atleast ten per cent. per annum from the expiry of thirty days from date of allotment till the listing of such securities to the allottees. (4) In the event of non-receipt of listing permission from the stock exchange(s) by the issuer or the issuing company, as the case may be, or withdrawal of Observation Letter issued by the Board, wherever applicable, the securities shall not be eligible for listing and the issuer or the issuing company, as the case may be, shall be liable to refund the subscription monies, if any, to the respective allottees immediately alongwith interest at the rate of ten per cent. per annum from the date of allotment.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Item (2) of Chapter XI details the timeline and consequences for listing application.

policies.policy_title: Amendment to SEBI (Issue and Listing of Debt Securities) Regulations, 2008
policies.policy_description: Amendments to the SEBI (Issue and Listing of Debt Securities) Regulations, 2008 as specified in the excerpt.
policies.scope: Debt securities issuance and listing
policies.objective: Introduce new provisions for allotment, listing agreements, security deposits, and continuous listing conditions for debt securities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allotment of securities and payment of interest for public debt issues.
subpolicies.control: Allotment of securities and payment of interest. 12A. (1) The Issuer shall ensure that that in case of listing of debt securities issued to public, allotment of securities offered to public shall be made within thirty days of the closure of the public issue. (2) Where the debt securities are not allotted and/or application moneys are not refunded within the stipulated period in sub-regulation (1), the issuer shall undertake to pay interest at the rate of fifteen per cent. per annum. (3) Credit to demat accounts of the allottees shall be made within two working days from the date of allotment.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Regulation 12A introduces allotment timing and interest obligations for debt securities.

(1) The Issuer shall ensure that that in case of listing of debt securities issued to public, allotment of securities offered to public shall be made within thirty days of the closure of the public issue. (2) Where the debt securities are not allotted and/or application moneys are not refunded within the stipulated period in sub-regulation (1), the issuer shall undertake to pay interest at the rate of fifteen per cent. per annum. (3) Credit to demat accounts of the allottees shall be made within two working days from the date of allotment.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Listing agreement requirement for debt securities.
subpolicies.control: Listing Agreement. 19A. (1) Every issuer desirous of listing its debt securities on a recognised stock exchange shall execute an agreement with such stock exchange. subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to execute a listing agreement for specified securities.
subpolicies.control: Every issuer desirous of listing its non‑convertible redeemable preference shares, or perpetual non‑cumulative preference shares or innovative perpetual debt instruments on a recognised stock exchange shall execute an agreement with such stock exchange.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Captures the core obligation to sign a listing agreement for the listed securities.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: Every issuer desirous of listing its non‑convertible redeemable preference shares, or perpetual non‑cumulative preference shares or innovative perpetual debt instruments on a recognised stock exchange, shall execute an agreement with such stock exchange.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to execute a fresh listing agreement within six months of SEBI notification.
subpolicies.control: Every issuer which has previously entered into agreements with a recognised stock exchange to list non‑convertible redeemable preference shares, or perpetual non‑cumulative preference shares or innovative perpetual debt instruments shall execute a fresh listing agreement with such stock exchange within six months of the date of notification of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Sets a timeframe for existing issuers to update their agreements after regulatory change.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to deposit 1% of the securities offered for subscription as security.
subpolicies.control: The issuer shall deposit, before the opening of subscription list, and keep deposited with the stock exchange(s) an amount calculated at the rate of one per cent. of the amount of securities offered for subscription to the public.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Imposes a security deposit obligation based on a percentage of the offering amount.

subpolicies.control: The amount stipulated in sub-regulation (1) shall be deposited in the manner specified by Board and/or stock exchange(s).

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to file listing particulars for private placement and disseminate information.
subpolicies.control: In case of a private placement of securitised debt instruments, the special purpose distinct entity shall file listing particulars with the recognised stock exchange, along with the application made under sub-regulation (1) of regulation 35, containing such information as may be necessary for any investor in the secondary market to make an informed investment decision in respect of its securitised debt instruments and the special purpose distinct entity shall promptly disseminate such information, as prescribed, in such manner as the recognised stock exchange(s) may determine from time to time.

subpolicies.control: An entity with listed specified securities... shall remit a fee at the rate of 0.1% of the paid‑up share capital... not exceeding five lakh rupees.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Defines fee calculation based on share capital.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: 701[Schedule XI - Fee in respect of draft scheme of arrangement [see regulations 37, 59A, 94 and 94A] 1. An entity with listed specified securities, or listed specified securities and listed non-convertible debt securities or non-convertible redeemable preference shares, shall, along with the draft scheme of arrangement, remit a fee at the rate of 0.1% of the paid‑up share capital of the listed/ transferee/ resulting company, whichever is higher, post the sanction of the scheme by the National Company Law Tribunal: Provided that the total amount of fees payable shall not exceed five lakh rupees.]
